4. In the Schema Details page, type the name and description previously entered for the new attribute in the Relationship Definition section.
 Before creating any schema objects, you should ensure that the schema object does not already exist in the system to avoid data duplication.
5. In the Relationship Definition Information section, select IDevTag from the list in the Interface Definition at End 1 box.
6. In the Interface Definition at End 2 box, enter the interface definition or use a search and select the interface definition from the list. For example, ISPFSupplier.
 You can select The direction of the Relationship definition will be 12 option to control the direction of the relationship.
7. Click Next.
8. In the Advanced Settings page, enter the name Tag for role 1 and Supplier for role 2 in the Role Display Name Details section.
 The role names are displayed when you right-click on the new relationship in the Desktop Client.
9. In the Cardinality Details section, select zero as the Min1 and Min2 settings and one as the Max1 and Max2 settings.
 In this example, the maximum cardinalities are set to one, because the supplier has only one tag and the tag has only one supplier. However, if the Max1 value is set to *, the supplier can have many tags, but each tag would
only have one supplier.
10. View the Summary, and click Finish.
Create classification
1. In the Parent Classification page, select the parent classification DEV Telecom Equipment from the tree view, and click Next.
2. In the Classification Details page, enter the name and description for the new classification Mobile Hub, and click Next.
 If the parent classification has a corresponding enumeration, you have the option to create a new enumeration for the new classification. You can control whether the new enumeration is an EnumListType or is an EnumEnum
by selecting the Create as EnumEnum option.
3. View the Summary, and click Finish.
SmartPlant Business Intelligence
SmartPlant Business Intelligence is delivered and installed with SmartPlant Foundation and provides a convenient way to easily produce summary statistical information from your database. SmartPlant Foundation information can be
displayed as graphic chart reports, which you can drill down for further details. SmartPlant Business Intelligence is powered by the same viewing technology as the existing Desktop Client ad-hoc reports.
Using Business Intelligence
The SmartPlant Business Intelligence software is used to create, view, and edit chart reports, providing a fast way to view your plant information. You can view and print the report results in predefined style layouts, such as bar chart or pie
chart, and export the results as an image file or as a Microsoft Excel file. With the appropriate role and access permissions, you can create new chart reports and choose the setup, criteria, and filtering to fully configure the details you want
to display in the report. For more information on configuring and creating a chart report as an administrator, see Configuring Business Intelligence.
Only users with specific role and access permissions are able to create, edit, or delete chart report definitions. Users with view only access permission can only view, print, and export predefined business intelligence reports. For any
role or access changes, contact your system administrator for further information.
SmartPlant Business Intelligence only displays information from the create scope defined in the Desktop Client. Therefore, you can only view a report with information from one query scope.
Business Intelligence software
When you open the Business Intelligence software, it displays chart reports in two panes in the application window.
Reports - This pane displays any existing chart report names in a list.
Graphics results - This pane displays the chart report result.
The Business Intelligence software provides the following functions in chart reports:
Counts the total number of objects matching your query criteria, and displays the statistics on a chart report.
Counts the number of objects by category, or by category and series, and then displays the statistics on a chart report.
Grouping and series grouping definitions provide a sub-categorization of the groupings already collated by the category definition.
Chart reports are defined based on view definitions and their interface properties.
Supports drill-down operations from a chart report object.
Supports changing chart report style, where the style format of the results of the query are of more emphasis.
Open Business Intelligence
Click the Business Intelligence panel in SmartPlant Dashboard to start Business Intelligence.
You can select tool commands from the main toolbar to perform various actions, such as creating a new chart report or editing existing chart reports.
More than one chart report can be displayed in the graphics results pane. You can alternate between reports by clicking on each tab.
You can view, resize, print, and export the chart report in the graphics results pane, as well as drill down to see more detailed information on the properties displayed in the chart report.
Click Minimize 
 to reduce the Reports pane to a side tab. This lets the displayed chart report result fill the application window. Click the tab to expand the reports pane into the main application window.
Run a chart report
1. Click the chart report you want to display in the Reports pane.
2. Click Run the selected report 
. The chart report pulls the latest data from the database to display in the graphics results pane.
Change report style
You can change how the chart report is displayed in the graphics results pane into a variety of display styles. This is most useful where the format of the results of the query is of more emphasis than the detail.
Click on a style option shown in the chart report to change to another chart style. The main chart styles available to use in analysis of plant data are:
Vertical Bar Chart 
 - for showing data from selected objects in single vertical bars.
Stacked Bar Chart 
 - for showing data from selected objects in stacked columns.
Horizontal Bar Chart 
 - for comparing data from selected objects in horizontal bars.
Pie Chart 
 - for showing the relative shares of objects of a total.
Line Graph 
 - for showing trend information of objects in a series graph over time.
Horizontal Stacked Bar Chart - for comparing data from selected objects in horizontal stacked bars.
 Chart report style availability is limited based on the type of report. For example, the following chart report displays only two styles as the chart report includes series grouping, which does not work as a pie chart.
Sort grouping column order
You can sort the order of grouping columns displayed in the graphics results pane, either alphabetically into ascending or descending order or by promoting or demoting a specific grouping column to set a custom column order. This is
most useful when the order of the grouping columns requires more emphasis than the detail of the results.
1. Click Sort Order 
 in the graphics results pane toolbar.
2. In the Set grouping sequence dialog box, click Ascending 
 or Descending 
 to change the alphabetical order of the grouping columns in the list view.
 You can select a specific grouping column and use the up Arrow 
 or down Arrow 
 to move the column to a set order in the list.
3. Click Finish to confirm the order of the grouping columns.
The changes made to the grouping column order are dynamically displayed in the graphics results pane.
The Sort Order command is not supported for series grouping columns due to a technical limitation.
Drill down into a chart report
You can drill down through a chart report to view the published data for a specific section of the chart report. Drilling down through a segment of a chart report displays the data for those properties and displays them in an item list.
 The drill down function is available when you the Enable drill down on charts check box is selected when you first create a chart report, see Select the setup for the chart report.
1. Click on a specific segment of a chart object in a bar, column or pie chart. All the properties related to the specific section are displayed in an item list view.
2. To close the item list view, click Close 
.
When creating a report, the results are listed as items in pages. The default drill down quantity of items per page is 20. If the results number more than 20 items, the items are spread over multiple pages. For more information on
setting drill down and quantity, see Select the setup for the chart report.
To change the order in which the item list results are displayed, click the column. This reorders the results from ascending to descending.
To go to specific pages of the item list, click the page arrows.
To open the items listed on one page as a single paged query within the Desktop Client, click Open page in SmartPlant Foundation Client 
.
To open specific items as a paged query within the Desktop Client, select the objects in the item list, and click Open selected items in SmartPlant Foundation Client 
. Only the selected items appear in a paged query in the
Desktop Client.
Click Show All to display all the items from each page in a single list on one page. This could result in a long list if the items spread over multiple pages are displayed all on one page. You can view all the items in a single paged
query in the Desktop Client by clicking Open page in SmartPlant Foundation Client 
.
Filter item list results
You can refine the item list results by filtering a column to get a usable set of data to match your requirements.
1. On a column, click Filter 
.
2. Select the property or properties to only show those results in the item list.
 You can further refine the results by selecting a condition value, such as Is equal to or Starts with.
3. Click Filter 
 to display the filtered results automatically in the item list
 By filtering on several properties, you can refine the results to get to a specific set of data. The following example displays only three centrifugal pumps that match having a DesignTempMin of 6C and a DesignPressureMin of
1070kPag.
Hide zero count groups
Click Hide zero count groups 
 to suppress the display of any groupings or series groupings that have zero values appearing in the chart report.
Refresh chart report
Click Refresh the chart 
 to refresh any chart report details in the graphics results pane with the latest data from the database.
Reset chart to original state
Click and drag over a specific area of interest in a chart report and release the mouse. The display zooms into the selected area.
 Click Reset chart to original state 
 to display the chart report so that it fills the screen.
Print chart report
1. Select the chart report you want to print in the graphics results pane.
2. Click Print the chart 
 on the graphics results toolbar to open the Print dialog box.
3. Select the printer and print options.
4. Click Print.
Export to Excel
1. Select the chart report you want to export to Microsoft Excel in the graphics results pane.
2. Click Export the chart to Excel 
 on the graphics results toolbar.
3. In the Excel dialog box, browse to the location to save the Excel file.
4. Type the name for the Excel file
5. Click Save to save the report is saved as an Excel file to the location of your choice.
Export to image
1. Select the chart report you want to display in the graphics results pane.
2. Click Export the chart as an image 
 on the graphics results toolbar.
3. Browse to the location for the image.
4. Type the name for the image.
5. Click Save to save the report as a .png file to a location of your choice.
Switch application modules
You can switch to another application by using the sidebar menu, without having to access the home menu. In the main window of any of the applications, hover over the sidebar menu Expand  button. The sidebar expands to display the
applications that your role has access to.
To change to another application, click the tile for the module, for example, Business Intelligence.
To keep the sidebar menu open, click Pin 
.
Press CTRL+TAB to cycle between the applications on the sidebar menu.
Configuring Business Intelligence
You can define new chart reports by choosing the setup, criteria, and filtering to fully configure the details you want to display in your report. Only users with specific role and access permissions are able to create, edit, or delete chart
reports. SmartPlant Business Intelligence displays information only from the user's active query scope, as defined by the user in the Desktop Client.
 Users with view access permission only cannot configure, create, edit or delete reports. They can only view predefined business intelligence reports. For any role or access changes, contact your system administrator for further
information.
Business Intelligence overview
As an administrator with the appropriate role and access permissions, you can configure the content and format of a chart report using view definitions as the basis for the report. These are created and edited in the Schema Editor, from
which you create the custom report to your specific requirements.
SmartPlant Business Intelligence provides a quick way to perform these complex queries and view project information across structured data. Access groups and owning groups provide access control to the chart reports, which can be
used by all established projects and by owner operators who are in maintenance mode.
Generate chart reports
You can create a new chart report using the Business Intelligence Report Wizard, which guides you through the steps needed to add the details and the criteria filtering required for your report.
 You can navigate through the Business Intelligence Report Wizard to the next page by clicking on the side menu options.
Select the setup for the chart report
1. Click New Chart report 
 to start the Business Intelligence Report Wizard.
2. In Setup > Name, Description, and Basics page, type the name, chart title, description, X-axis label, and Y-axis label details into the options for the new chart.
Select the Hide zero count groups check box to suppress the display of statistics when no matching objects are found.
Select Enable drill down on charts check box to activate drill down operation on a chart to display the details of the properties. Type the number of drill down items per page required in the Number of drill down items per
page box. The default number of items per page is set to 20.
If the Enable drill down on charts check box is selected, the Column set box becomes active. Type the column set name in the Column set box, or type * and click Search 
 and select a column set from the menu list that
matches how you want to display the item list details.
Some options are mandatory, a warning message displays if nothing is typed in those boxes.
The default text limit for text boxes, such as chart title, description, and axis labels, is set to 50 characters. The chart name is limited to 32 characters.
3. Click Next.
Select a view definition
1. In Setup > Select a View Definition page, click the name of the view definition for the report from the list.
 You must select the view definition to be used for the basis of the report, which displays the properties you want to report against. A default view definition list is supplied with the installation of SmartPlant Foundation,
such as Document List or Instrument List. However, you can define your own new view definitions as required using the Schema Editor. For more information, see SmartPlant Schema Editor Overview.
2. Click Next.
Define access control
1. In Setup > Define Access Control page, select the Owning Group for the chart report from the list menu.
2. Add any access groups by selecting each one from the Available Access Groups list, and click left arrow 
 to add the access groups to the Selected Access Groups list.
3. Click Next.
 For more information on configuring access and owning groups, see How to Configure the Security Model.
Define criteria and prompts
1. In Setup > Define Criteria and Prompts page, click the property for which you want to add criteria or set a prompt.
2. Select one of the following Join Operator options to join multiple criteria for a single chart report.
Use And if you want all the criteria to match before including the item in the report.
Use Or if you want only a single criterion to match before including the item in the report.
You cannot mix And and Or when defining multiple criteria for a single chart report.
3. Click Add Criteria 
.
4. In the Criteria Definition page, type the criteria to add to the property in the Add new criterion field.
5. Click Add 
.
6. Select the condition to add to the criteria from the Condition list.
 The conditions vary depending on the criteria selected, so a good working knowledge of the properties in the system, and what the user wants to display, is needed as a prerequisite for adding the correct criteria.
7. Click Finish to add the property condition and criteria to the criteria list.
 Repeat this action to add further criteria and conditions for the property.
8. Click Next to close the Criteria Definition page.
9. In Define Criteria and Prompts page, click Prompt check box against a property so a check mark 
 appears. You are prompted to select a value each time the graphic report runs.
 When a prompt is set, a user can display different data without having to create another chart report. For example, you can choose to display all pumps with a flow rate condition set to Less than 10 meters a second, or to
display all pumps with a flow rate condition set to Equal to or greater than 10 meters a second.
10. Click Next.
 You can use environment variables to filter the information for the current user, user organization, and so on. For more information on environment variables, see Environment variable support.
Define additional filter criteria on properties that store a date and time
Setting date and time allows you to specify additional filter criteria on properties that store a date and time.
Calendar - Allows you to select a specific date.
Today - Selects today's date. If you want to set a date that is a specific period from today, select Today plus (+) or minus (-) number of Days, Weeks, or Months.
Ignore non-working days - Allows you to ignore non-working days in the default calendar for the site when calculating the duration. The default calendar is selected using the system options. For more information on the system options,
see Configure the system options.
Define additional filter criteria on properties for blank and non blank values
Setting Has no value, Is blank, Does not exist or Has any non blank value generates reports with the specified additional filter criteria on properties of objects.
Property
Report is generated when
Has no value
a property value is cleared later
Is blank
a property value is empty
Does not exist
a property value does not exist or is not created
Has any non blank value
a populated property value exists
Specify grouping
You can display the chart report results grouped by the physical objects and their properties. There are three grouping options in the Specify Grouping page.
Single Grouping uses a single property using a set criterion to form a specific grouping.
Multiple Grouping uses multiple properties, which allows the user to create more complex groupings.
Using Run Time Values grouping uses a single property to form a specific grouping, which is only generated at the report run time.
Using a single property
1. In Specify Grouping page, select Using Single Property option.
2. Click Create Grouping 
 to open the Grouping Administrator page.
3. In the Create Grouping list, select the property required.
4. In Grouping Definition list, type the criteria to add to the property in the Add new criterion box.
5. Select the condition to add to the criteria from the Condition menu.
 The property criteria you use and conditions displayed are dependent on the property type selected.
6. Click Add Criteria 
 to add the current criteria and condition to the Grouping Definition list.
7. Click Finish to add the groupings into the Grouping selection list in the Specify Grouping page.
8. Click Finish to run the report.
 If you want to specify series grouping, click Next.
Using multiple properties
1. In Specify Grouping page, select Using Multiple Properties option.
2. Click Create Grouping 
 to open the Grouping Administrator page.
3. Type the Grouping name and Description in the boxes provided.
 The Grouping name box is mandatory. A warning message displays if nothing is typed in the box.
4. In the Create Grouping list, select the property required.
5. In the Criteria Definition page, type the criteria to add to the property in the Add new criterion box.
6. Select the condition to add to the criteria from the Condition menu.
 The property criteria you use and conditions displayed are dependent on the property type selected.
7. Click Add Criteria 
 to add the current criteria and condition to the Grouping Definition list.
 You can repeat this action to add further criteria and conditions to other properties.
8. Click Create to create the groups and add each property criteria and condition to the Grouping Name list.
9. Click Cancel to close the Grouping Administrator page.
10. Click Finish to run the report.
 If you want to specify series grouping, click Next.
Using Run Time Values
1. In Specify Grouping page, select Using Run Time Values option.
2. Click Create Grouping 
 to open the Grouping Administrator page.
3. In the Create Grouping list, select the property to base the groupings on. For example, Category.
The dialog validates that an eligible property has been selected and only allows you to progress if the selection is valid.
After you select a property, you cannot add any further properties to groupings.
Click Calculate Groupings 
to display information on how many groupings will be created at run time for the selected property. If the property selected has a large number of distinct values, this would create a large number
of groupings, which may be unwanted.
4. Click Finish when you have selected the property.
 A message appears in the Grouping Selection dialog box stating that groupings will be generated at run time using the property selected.
5. Click Next to specify any series grouping, or click Finish to close the Specify Grouping page and run the report.
Specify series grouping
Specifying the series grouping allows you to display chart report results in a column chart and further group these results by a relevant series grouping. This provides a mechanism to display the data with greater granularity, adding an
additional dimension of data to a chart report. There are three series grouping options in the Specify Series Grouping page.
Single Grouping uses a single property with set criterion to form a specific series grouping.
Multiple Grouping uses multiple properties, which enables the user to create more complex series groupings.
Using Run Time Values grouping uses a single property to form a specific series grouping, which is only generated at the report run time.
Using a single property
1. In Specify Series Grouping page, select Using Single Property option.
2. Click Create Grouping 
 to open the Series Grouping Administrator page.
3. In the Create Series Grouping list, select the property required.
4. In the Grouping Definition list, type the criteria to add to the property in the Add new criterion dialog box.
5. Select the condition to add to the criteria from the Condition menu.
 The property criteria you use and conditions displayed are dependent on the property type selected.
6. Click Add Criteria 
 to add the current criteria and condition to the property. The series groupings created displays in the Criteria Definition list.
7. Click Create to create the series grouping.
8. Click Finish to display all the property series groupings in the Specify Series Grouping list.
9. Click Finish to create the report.
Using multiple properties
1. In Specify Series Grouping page, select Using Multiple Properties option.
2. Click Create Series Grouping 
 to open the Series Grouping Administrator page.
3. Enter the Grouping name and Description in the boxes provided.
 The Grouping name box is mandatory. A warning message displays if nothing is typed.
4. In the Create Series Groupings list, select the property required.
5. In the Criteria Definition page, type the criteria to add to the property in the Add new criterion box.
6. Select the condition to add to the criteria from the Condition menu.
 The property criteria you use and conditions displayed are dependent on the property type selected.
7. Click Add Criteria 
 to add the current criteria and condition to the property. The series groupings created displays in the Criteria Definition list.
 You can repeat this action to add further criteria and conditions to other properties.
8. Click Create to create the series grouping and display all the property series groupings created in the Specify Series.
9. Click Finish to display all the series groupings created in the Criteria Definition list.
10. Click Cancel to close the Series Grouping Administrator page.
11. Click Finish to close the Specify series Grouping page and create the report.
Using Run Time Values
1. In Specify Series Grouping page, select Using Run Time Values option.
2. Click Create Series Grouping 
 to open the Series Grouping Administrator page.
3. In the Create Series Groupings list, select a property to base the groupings on. For example, Category.
The dialog box validates that an eligible property has been selected and only allows you to progress if the selection is valid.
After you select a property, you cannot add any further properties to groupings.
UoM fields are restricted, therefore UoMs cannot be used with run time values.
Click Calculate Groupings 
to display information on how many groupings will be created for the selected property at run time. If the property selected has a large number of distinct values, this would create a large number
of groupings, which may be unwanted.
4. Click Finish when you have selected the property.
 A message appears in the Series Grouping Selection dialog box stating that series groupings will be generated at run time using the property selected.
5. Click Finish to close the Specify Series Grouping page and create the report.
Edit a chart report
1. Once a report is made, you can edit the results. Click the chart report to highlight it in the Reports pane.
2. Click Edit Report 
. The Business Intelligence Report Wizard displays the original chart report criteria used to create the report. You can then follow the wizard and edit any details. For more information, see Generate Chart
Reports.
 Only users with administration rights can edit a chart report.
Expose summary reports in Web Client feature sets
Summary reports provide a convenient way to view key business metrics and engineering data in the form of charts. You can relate the summary report to any feature set for displaying in the Web Client.
1. In the Desktop Client, click Find > Administration > Feature Sets.
2. Right-click the feature set, and click Manage Summary Reports.
3. In the Manage Summary Reports dialog box, select the Summary Report and relate it to the feature set. The summary report appears as a tile in the feature set.
Modify the display of summary reports in a feature set
Sort the display order of summary reports in a feature set
1. In the Desktop Client, right-click the feature set you want to sort, and click Manage Summary Reports.
2. In the Manage Summary Reports dialog box, type a value in the Order Value column to specify the order each summary report is displayed.
Group the summary reports in a feature set
1. In the Desktop Client, right-click the feature set you want to add to a group, and click Manage Summary Reports.
2. In the Manage Summary Reports dialog box, type the same group name value in the Group Name column to group the required summary reports together in the same feature set.
Load integration reports
SmartPlant Business Intelligence is delivered with several reports that you can use on new sites. If your site was upgraded from a previous SmartPlant Foundation version, you must first load the Business Intelligence reports through the
SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client Loader utility.
 Only follow this process if your site is upgraded from a previous version of SmartPlant Foundation.
 You must have administrator privileges to perform this process.
1. Download 03-DATA_DW-IntegrationBIReports.xml from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. Log on to SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
3. Click File > Loader.
4. Browse for and select 03-DATA_DW-IntegrationBIReports.xml.
5. Click Process.
 A green checkmark next to an XML file indicates that the file has been processed successfully.
6. Click Close.
Delete a chart report
1. Click the chart report in the Reports pane.
2. Click Delete 
 to delete the selected chart report from the Reports pane.
 Only users with administrator privileges can delete a chart report.
Integration Business Intelligence Reports
SmartPlant Business Intelligence has several delivered reports out-of-the-box that you can use on new sites. If your site was upgraded from a previous SmartPlant Foundation version, you must first load the integration Business
Intelligence reports through the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client Loader utility. For more information, see Load integration reports.
 You can only view the Business Intelligence integration reports if you are assigned the DWEditor or DWViewer roles. For more information on assigning roles, see Role configuration.
Report
Description
Published Documents by DocType
Displays the number of published documents, sorted by document type, such as PBS documents and equipment datasheets.
Published Objects by Domain
Displays the number of published objects from each domain, such as the PBS domain and the GenD domain.
Published Versions by DocType
Displays the number of versions published, sorted by document type, such as PBS documents and equipment datasheets.
Retrieved Documents by DocType
Displays the number of documents that were retrieved by the tools, sorted by document type, such as the number of versions retrieved through
PBS documents and equipment datasheets.
Business Intelligence Model
The interfaces used in SmartPlant Business Intelligence are shown in the following model.
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these interfaces used in the Business Intelligence module are given in the following sections.
ISPFViewDef
This interface is responsible for identifying the view definition that is related to the report.
ISPFColumnSet
This interface is responsible for identifying which column set will be used for the report drill down.
ISPFAccessGroup
This interface is responsible for identifying who can access and run the report.
ISPFOwningGroup
This interface is responsible for identifying who can administer the report definition.
ISPFBIReport
This interface contains the structure and properties for the report. The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFBIRptTitle
Report title
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
SPFBIRptIgnoreZeroCounts
Ignore zero count groupings/series groupings.
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
SPFBIDDEnabled
If drill down to list view is enabled on the report
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
SPFBIRptPageSize
The number of records to retrieve when drilling down
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
SPFBIRptXAxisLabel
The X-axis label
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
SPFBIRptYAxisLabel
The Y-axis label
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
ISPFBIReportPrompt
This interface indicates if a prompt is required for a property. The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFBIRptPrmptViewDefProp
The name of the property.
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
ISPFBIReportCriteria
This interface contains the structure and properties to build criteria for the report. The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
ISPFBIReportCriteriaS
Describes the shared interface which contains the criteria.
Code.
ISPFReportGroupItem
This interface contains the label that is displayed as part of the report grouping/series grouping. The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFBIRptGrpItmLbl
The label for the grouping/series grouping.
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
ISPFReportGroupItemDetail
This interface contains the label that is displayed as part of the report grouping/series grouping. The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFBIRptGrpItmDtlUseRTValues
Determines if groupings/series groupings should be created at run time.
Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
ISPFBIReportCriteriaS
Describes the shared interface which contains the criteria for the grouping.
Code.
ISPFBIReportCriteriaS
This interface contains the common structure used to define report criteria. The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description

Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
SPFBIRptCrtrValue

Create/Edit Report Definition wizard.
SmartPlant Report Administration
In SmartPlant Foundation, reports allow you to search for a type of object in the database and use the properties to filter the results found. Report definitions determine both the type of object that is searched for and the properties of those
objects that are returned. For example, you can create a report definition to search for tags based on their properties and return not only properties particular to the tags, but also properties of related objects, such as the names of related
units.
Report definitions are equivalent to relational database views, displaying the requested objects, their properties, and their relationships to other objects in the database. Report definitions are the basis for both the ad-hoc and Business
Intelligence reporting functionality in SmartPlant Foundation, as well as the basis for creating the data sheet and data list definitions.
The SmartPlant Report Administration module includes an easy-to-use graphical interface that allows you to configure report definitions even if you do not have a detailed understanding of the schema and its terminology. The report
definitions are made up of two parts:
View definition - Defines the properties to be extracted for the returned object and any related objects. The view definition also allows you to change the display values for properties, so that you can configure how they will appear in the
final report.
Graph Definition - Defines a collection of properties, for both the returned object and any other types of objects in the database to which it is related, from which you can choose those that will be included in the report.
You can create or modify report definitions in the SmartPlant Report Administration module using the Report Administration Wizard. This wizard allows you to view properties of an object and navigate the relationships between that
object and others in the database, exposing properties of the objects on the other end. The resulting collection of properties is called a graph definition.
The process of creating a new report definition with this wizard includes the following steps:
Create a new report definition and select an example object from a list
Refine the access options
Expand the relationships in a tree view to navigate to related objects
Select the properties of related objects found on each relationship expansion
Save the report definition hierarchy result and view the relationships displayed in the graph definition
Object types for reports
To be able to create a report for an object type, that object type must already be configured as a Quick Find item in the Desktop Client. In SmartPlant Report Administration, those Quick Find items are listed as the example object types you
choose from when creating a report definition.
However, the list of example object types that appear in SmartPlant Report Administration might be different than the list of Quick Find items that appear on the Desktop Client toolbar. You might notice these differences:
The display name for an object in the list of Quick Find items is different than its display name in the list of example object types - The display name is the SPFGUIDisplayAs property for the GUI item related to the method by the
SPFGUIAccessControlMethod relationship. If the SPFGUIDisplayAs property is not configured, the display name is the Quick Find item description, name, or UID, in that order.
An object that appears in the list of Quick Find items does not appear in the list of example object types - If more than one Quick Find item has been configured for the same object type, the object type will appear in the list of Quick
Find items multiple times. However, the object type will appear only once in the list of example object types, and it will be the first one found.
An object that appears in the list of example object types does not appear in the list of Quick Find items - If there are no GUI items related to the method by the SPFGUIAccessControlMethod relationship, the object type does not
actually appear in the list of Quick Find items. However, in SmartPlant Report Administration the method itself is listed as an example object.
In SmartPlant Report Administration, the list of example objects appears on the Search pane of the Create a New Report Definition dialog box in the Example object box.
For more information about configuring an object as a Quick Find item and setting the object display name, see Add a new item to the Quick Find menu.
For help with creating a report definition in SmartPlant Report Administration, see Create a report definition.
Other considerations when using the SmartPlant Report Administration module
The SmartPlant Reports Administration module was designed to make the creation and management of reports easier. When you create or modify report definitions with this module, you are creating or modifying graph definitions and view
definitions like those delivered in the SmartPlant Foundation schema. The user interface allows you to navigate the existing relationship definitions for an object graphically to create your graph definition and your view definition, which the
Desktop Client or the Business Intelligence module uses to create reports.
The changes you make with this module are written directly to the applicable SmartPlant Foundation database, removing the need for you to manually edit the schema objects in the Schema Editor and load the schema changes into the
database using the Schema Import Wizard or Loader. However, unlike when you make changes to the graph definitions or view definitions using the Schema Editor, this module does not write changes back to the schema file.
 If you use the SmartPlant Reports Administration module to modify a delivered view definition, or a view definition previously created in a schema file using the Schema Editor, reloading the schema file that contains the view
definitions could result in the loss of the changes made with the SmartPlant Reports Administration module. Carefully review the differences during the comparison stage of the Schema Import Wizard to be sure you do not revert the view
definition to its previous state.
 For easier reporting, SmartPlant Report Administration does not support the use of edge definitions.
Using the SmartPlant Report Administration Module
In the SmartPlant Report Administration module, you can define and assemble a report definition by navigating to objects, exposing their relationships to find additional properties, and building a report definition for your reporting purposes.
The Report Administration Wizard helps you create and modify report definitions used in reporting and data list generation. It provides a user-friendly interface where these changes can be made without requiring you to open the
Schema Editor to modify schema objects directly. In order for a report definition to be managed with the Report Administration Wizard, the following conditions must be met:
Through your role assignment, you must have a relationship to at least one access group that has edit access to the report definition (or the view definition). This relationship can be created in the Desktop Client.
The graph definition and view definition that define the report must have the same name.
The container ID of the report definition (or view definition) must be blank or some container other than Core.EFSchema or SchemaComponent.SchemaComponent.
In the Maintain or Create Report Definitions dialog, you can view all the report definitions saved in the system to which you have edit access. You can create a new report definition or edit an existing report definition by expanding
relationships to expose different properties and modify the report definition.
To search for an existing report definition, scroll through the list of report definitions, or type some portion of the name of the report definition in the Select a report definition to modify or view box, and click Search 
.
At any point, you can return to the SmartPlant Dashboard from a module by clicking Fdn > Home. You can also expand the sidebar menu by clicking Expand . The sidebar menu displays all the application modules to which you
have access. You can click on any icon to close the SmartPlant Report Administration module and open another.
Create a report definition
1. To create a new report definition, click Create New Report Definition 
 on the toolbar to open the Report Administration Wizard.
2. In the Basic Properties tab of the Create a New Report Definition dialog box, provide a name and description for the new report definition in the appropriate boxes.
3. On the View Access tab, select one or more access groups that will have permission to view the new report definition. For more information on access groups, see Access group configuration.
4. On the Edit Access tab, select one or more access groups that will have permission to edit the new report definition.
5. In the Search pane, select the type of object to be returned by this report from the Example object box, for example, Method.
For more information on which objects are available in the Example object box, please see Object types for reports.
You can also search for an example object using the search box to the right of the Example object box. Type the name of an object on which to base your report definition in the search box, and click Search 
. You can also
use the * as a wildcard.
Access to methods is controlled using specific access groups. Methods that are not applied to a specific access group may not display in the Example object box. Contact your administrator for more information on your access
group permissions.
6. Click Next to open the Expand Relationships and Select Properties dialog.
7. In the Navigation pane, select an interface from the Report Scope list box, for example, Method, ISPFMethod.
 Every report definition needs a starting interface, which is based on the example object selected. Selection of the starting interface is important because it is the primary class definition for the report definition. Alternatively,
the interface selected could be a shared interface, in which case the report pulls data from multiple class definitions.
8. In the Expand Relationships section, expand the nodes by the relationship definitions and related objects. Select a related object to display the properties of that object in the Properties pane. For example, expand Access Groups
and select a related object, like SystemAdmin, to see a list of the properties available from that object.
9. In the Properties pane, select the check boxes besides each property that you want to add to your report to add it to the properties list in the Selected Properties tab. For example, select the check boxes beside Name and
Description to add those properties from the related access group.
You can change the order of the properties in the Selected Properties tab by using the up 
or down 
arrows. The order in which the properties appear in this list is the default order of the properties in the report.
If necessary, select a property from the Selected Properties list and click Delete 
 to remove it from the report, or click Reset to completely clear the Selected Properties pane, removing all of the properties.
You can click the Report Diagram tab to display the properties exposed by the report definition in a graph definition.
Use the Resize 
 command to resize the graph objects to zoom into details.
Use the standard Move 
 and Fit 
 commands to edit the view of the graph definition.
Click Print 
 to send the graph definition to a printer or save it as an XPS file type.
10. Click Finish to create the new report definition and add it to the Maintain and Create Report Definitions list.
The report definition can now be selected to create reports in SmartPlant Business Intelligence or to make ad-hoc reports in the Desktop Client. For more information on making reports, see Select a view definition and Create a new
report.
New report definitions are saved to container IDs using your default container IDs set on the Change User Preferences dialog box in the Desktop Client. If you have not specified a default container ID, the new report definitions will
have no container ID assigned.
Edit a report definition
The following procedures explain how to modify an existing report definition.
Add properties to or remove properties from a report definition
This procedure explains how to add properties to a report definition, remove properties from a report definition, or change the order in which the properties appear by default in the report definition.
1. From the Maintain or Create Report Definitions dialog box, select the report definition you want to edit, and click Edit to open the Report Administration Wizard 
.
If an example object already exists for the report definition, it will appear in the Navigation pane on the left. You can expand the object to display the relationships between the example object and other objects.
If the example object for a report definition is deleted for any reason, properties cannot be added to the report definition. However, properties can still be removed from a report definition that has not example object.
The grid at the top of the Selected Properties pane includes all the properties of the object selected in the Navigation pane that are currently included in the report definition.
2. Select an object from the tree in the Navigation pane.
3. From the list of properties that appear in the Properties section of the Selected Properties pane, select the box beside properties you want to include in the report definition.
You can change the order of the properties in the report definition by selecting a property in the grid at the top of the Selected Properties pane and clicking Up 
 or Down 
 to change its placement in the grid.
To remove a property from the report definition, select the property from the grid at the top of the Selected Properties pane, and click Delete 
.
To change the default label associated with a property in the report definition, find the property in the grid at the top of the Select Properties pane, and edit the value for that property in the Caption column.
4. Click Finish.
Modify report definition properties and access rights
This procedure explains how you can modify information about the report definition, such as the description, the access groups to which it is available, and the access groups that have permissions to edit it.
1. From the Maintain or Create Report Definitions dialog box, select the report definition you want to edit and click Edit to open the Report Administration Wizard 
.
2. Click Back to open the wizard screen with a tab for basic properties and view and edit access information.
3. Modify the report definition using one or more of the following steps:
Click on the Basic Properties tab and edit the description of the report definition.
Click on the View Access tab and use the check boxes to specify which access groups have permission to see and use the report definition.
Click on the Edit Access tab and use the check boxes to specify which access groups have permissions to edit the report definition.
 Report definitions must have at least one access group selected on the Edit Access tab to be edited in the Report Administration module.
Delete a report definition
Use the following steps to delete a report definition:
1. Select a report definition from the Maintain or Create Report Definitions list.
2. In the main toolbar, click Delete the selected report definition 
.
3. Click Yes on the warning dialog box to confirm that you want to delete the report definition.
When a report definition is deleted, both the view definition and a graph definition are deleted. If the graph definition is being used by another object, then only the view definition is deleted.
If a report definition is deleted, any saved ad hoc reports or business intelligence reports based on that report definition are deleted.
You can delete only one report definition at a time.
Export a report definition to an .xml file
The following steps describe how to export a report definition from the SmartPlant Report Administration module to an .xml file.
1. Select a report definition from the Maintain or Create Report Definitions list.
2. In the main toolbar, click Export the selected report definition 
.
3. In the Save As dialog box, choose the file name and location to which to export the report definition, and click Save.
You can export only one report definition at a time.
When a report definition is exported, the .xml file contains the following objects:
The view definition
The graph definition used to create the view definition
The relationship definition tags to the primary interface for the view definition
The relationship definition tags to the primary interface for the graph definition
Any access groups granted view access for the report definition (if applicable)
The access group or groups granted edit access for the report definition
Third-Party Connectors
Can I connect to third-party software?
The following describes how to install, configure, and use the SDx Connectors for third-party software:
HxGN SDx Connector for TruView
HxGN SDx Connector for Plant Maintenance
HxGN SDx Connector for OSIsoft PI
SPO/E Core
This section contains information about how the user can perform tasks of SPO/E Core.
Using SPO/E Core
The software helps Owner Operators manage the technical complexities and work processes of the virtual plant asset, during all phases of the plant life cycle.
Using the software, Owner Operators can significantly:
Lower capital expenditures (CAPEX)
Achieve production startup earlier
Lower operating expenditures (OPEX) to reduce revenue loss due to unplanned outages
Minimize health and safety risks
Ensure demonstrable compliance with laws and regulations
Each SPO/E solution includes business packages that provide default pre-configured work processes and integrations with third-party systems.
About SPO/E Core
SPO/E Core includes key work processes relevant to the entire plant life cycle, such as:
Management of the plant breakdown structure and work breakdown structure
Tag management
Document management
Microsoft Office integration
Transmittals
Electronic dossiers
Risk-Reducing Measures (RRM)
Action Item and Issue Tracking (AIT)
Data loading
Getting Started
Exploring SPO/E Core
Understanding the SPO/E Core work processes is essential to getting started with SPO/E Core.
Get to know the SPO/E Core
SPO/E Core includes the following work processes:
Action Item and Issue Tracking - This acts as a communications platform for various interfaces within a particular scope of work. It manages the party and relationship details, and also provides reports.
Consolidated Data Warehouse - The main entities defined in this work process are the basic definitions for common objects and relationships that are used by the PBS, DMS, TAG, WBS, XMTL, and MAM work processes. CDW
provides all the basic definitions for the consolidated data warehouse to be aligned with the product schema.
Collaboration - The main entities defined in this work process are the basic definitions for common objects and relationships that are used by the PBS, DMS, TAG, WBS, XMTL, and MAM work processes.
SPO/E Core Basic Definitions - This work process enables the bulk load of the PBS data (system, area, unit, and so on) from Microsoft Excel.
Document Management - This contains a comprehensive set of work processes for document management, including the central allocation of document numbers, capture of document metadata, file archiving, distribution/review,
subscription, check in/check out, and online approval and transmittal of documentation internally and externally.
Engineering and Design - This work process maintains the dynamic design basis in accordance with planned design modifications during the plant life cycle.
Manufacturer, Asset, Model - This work process manages the relationships between tags, models, assets, and manufacturers.
Office Integration - This work process enables you to save or retrieve documents to or from SPO/E without using a SPO/E client. An "add-in" to Microsoft applications (Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel, and Microsoft PowerPoint)
allows you to save or retrieve documents to or from SPO/E.
Plant Breakdown Structure - This work process enables the bulkload of the PBS data (system, area, unit, and so on) from Microsoft Excel.
Risk Reducing Measures - The main entities defined in this work process are the basic definitions for common objects and relationships that are used by the PBS, DMS, TAG, WBS, XMTL, and MAM work processes.
Tag Management/Central Tag Allocation - This provides the centralized allocation of tag numbers in SmartPlant Foundation, in accordance with a pre-defined engineering numbering system, maintenance of tag data, data sheets,
and the publishing of tag details to SmartPlant Enterprise design tools (as supported by existing SmartPlant adapters).
Test Tracking Object - This work process acts as a communications platform for various interfaces within a particular scope of work. It manages the party and relationship details, as well as providing reports.
Work Breakdown Structure - This work process manages development, turnaround, and modification projects as well as the breakdown of work into packages.
Transmittals - This work process manages receipt of incoming documentation, distribution of internal documents within the Owner Operator, and distribution of external documents to third parties.
Each work process is unique and is relevant throughout the plant life cycle.
Familiarize Yourself with SPO/E Core
This section describes the most common SPO/E Core objects and provides an overview of major features in the user interface. In SPO/E Core, you can create a variety of objects depending on your role assignments and user permissions.
Learn about SPO/E Core objects
Understanding the SPO/E Core objects (including tags, documents, manufacturers, dossiers, suppliers, assets, and models) is essential to getting started with SPO/E Core.
The SPO/E Core objects appear in the Tree View window and on the tool bar of the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client only when site is created with delivered SPO/E Core database.
The following table lists the icons and their descriptions for the most commonly used objects in SPO/E Core.
Icon
Description
Opens the Search dialog box SPO/E Core objects. This common search dialog box combines both the Quick Find and Query features into a single dialog box.
Allows you to create Non-Integrated tag types.
Allows you to create Central Tag Allocation (CTA) tag types
Allows you to create a supplier.
Allows you to create a manufacturer.
Allows you to create an asset.
Allows you to create a model.
Search for SPO/E Core objects
There are multiple ways to search for SPO/E Core objects in the Desktop Client, based on detailed information about the object, the name of the object, and the object type.
In addition to the Quick Find and Query feature, you can also use the Binocular Search 
 feature for searching SPO/E Core objects. Binocular Search 
 combines both the Quick Find and Query features into a single dialog box.
1. Click the Binocular Search 
 icon on the toolbar.
2. Select an object you want to search for from Select item from the list.
3. Type the object's name and description into the Name and Description boxes respectively.
4. Click Send to new list to display the search results as a new list or click Send to active list to append the search results to an existing search results list.
5. Click Find to display the search results.
 To search objects using additional search criteria, click Advanced Query.
Create and Modify SPO/E Core objects
You can create a variety of objects in SPO/E Core, depending on your role assignment and user permissions within a project. You can use a wizard or a dialog box to create these objects.
Some types of objects (such as tags and documents) can be created using a wizard, which you can access by clicking File > New and clicking the type of object that you want to create.
 Not all objects on the File > New submenu have wizards associated with them. You can create these types of objects using dialog boxes in one of two ways:
If the object appears on the File > New menu, click File > New > [Object Name] to create the object.
Browse to the type of object you want to create in the Tree View, right-click the object type, and then click Create [Object Type].
Documents
A document is a specialized database object that follows a controlled life cycle and usually has files attached. Revisable documents are composed of a document master, revision, and version.
Create a document
You can create a variety of documents in SPO/E Core. Some of these document types include engineering, management, vendor, procurement, project control, email, and admin, you need to add the ISPFDocumentMaster interface before
creating a document.
You can create a variety of documents in SPO/E Core. Some of these document types include engineering, management, vendor, procurement, project control, email, and admin, you need to add the ISPFDocumentMaster interface before
creating a document.
1. Click File > New > Documents > Engineering Documents.
2. In the New Engineering Document dialog box, expand the document class to select a document type.
3. Click Next.
4. In the Main Details section, type a description in the Description box.
5. In the Revision details section, select a revision scheme from the Revision scheme list.
6. In the Owning group section, select an owning group from the Item owning group list.
7. In the Document Classification section, select values from the Discipline Codes, Originator Codes, and Book Reference Codes lists.
8. In the Plant/Area/Unit/System section, select a value from the Plant, Area, Unit, and System list.
9. Click Finish. The engineering document is created and displayed in the New Items window. 
 Use the following steps to create an engineering document from the Tree View window:
1. In the Tree view window, expand the Document Types to select a document type.
2. Right-click the selected document type, and click Create Document > Engineering Document.
Create a reserved document
The Reserved Documents feature enables you to reserve document numbers for single or multiple documents such as engineering, vendor, procurement, and project control documents. When you create a reserve document, you create
a document master. The document revision and version are created when the reserved document is activated.
The following steps show how to create a reserved engineering document:
1. Click File > New > Documents > Reserved Documents > Reserved Engineering Documents.
2. In the New Reserved Engineering Document dialog box, expand the document class to select a document type.
3. Click Next.
4. Enter the information in the required boxes.
5. Click Finish to reserve the engineering document.
 You can also reserve an engineering document from the Tree View window. Right-click the document type in the Tree view, and click Reserved Documents > Reserved Engineering Documents.
When you create a reserved engineering document, the status of the document master is set to RESERVED_INACTIVE.
You can create other reserved documents such as reserved project control documents, reserved vendor documents, and so on, in the same way.
Activate a reserved document
When should I activate a reserved document?
You need to activate a reserved document if you want to use it.
1. Right-click the reserved document that you want to make active, and click Activate.
2. In the Activate dialog box, select the document type.
3. Click Next.
4. Select an owning group from the Item owning group list.
5. Select a revision scheme from the Revision scheme list, and set the Major revision and Minor revision values.
6. Click Next.
7. If required, attach a file to a document by clicking Add File.
8. Browse to the file that you want to attach, and click Open.
 If opening the Attach File dialog box for the first time, the Delete local files option is selected by default. If you clear the Delete local files option, the selection is saved to the local system as the default.
9. Click Finish. The status of the document changes from RESERVED_INACTIVE to RESERVED.
 You can attach multiple files to the reserved document by repeating these steps.
NLF load format for documents
You can use an NLF load file to create documents, and load it using the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client Loader.
When creating documents such as engineering, management, project control, and vendor documents using an NLF load file, you must add the ISPFDocumentMaster interface to the NLF instructions.
The following is an example of an NLF file with instructions to create an engineering document:
.SPXEngineeringDocMaster
.Name|EngDocMaster-Basic-001
.Description|Engineering Document from NLF
.SPXDocumentClass|MD|SPFPrimaryClassification_21
.SPFDocState|RESERVED
.SPFRevisionScheme|RevA01|SPFDocRevisionRevisionScheme_12
.SPFMajorRevision|A
.SPFMinorRevision|01
.SPFRevState|WORKING
.SPFProject|0000|DocumentProject_12
.SPXOriginator|AB|DocumentOriginator_12
.SPXDiscipline|A|DocumentDiscipline_12
.ISPFDocumentMaster
For more information on NLF format and loading files in the SmartPlant Foundation database, see NLF format and Loader.
 For these SPO/E documents, ISPFDocumentMaster interface is not a mandatory realize relationship. This interface is made optional for these documents to support the document reservation feature. Hence, you must add the
ISPFDocucmentMaster interface to the NLF instructions.
Cancel a document
What happens when I cancel a document?
When a document is canceled, it gets removed from the document life cycle routine. The status of the document is changed to Canceled. After a document is canceled, you can still view the canceled document's information and the
attached files but it can no longer be revised, checked out, and so on.
Right-click the document in the Tree View or New Items window, and click Document > Cancel.
Only a document with a Revision state of WORKING can be canceled.
To undo the Cancel, right-click the canceled document in the Tree View or New Items window, and click Document > Undo Cancel.
You can select multiple documents for Cancel and Undo Cancel operations.
Relate objects to documents
You can relate objects such as systems, work packages, areas, units, PO Contracts, files etc. to a document.
1. Drag the selected object onto the document.
2. Click OK in the New Relationships dialog box.
 You can also drag files directly from Windows Explorer or Microsoft Outlook onto a document to attach files.
Update a document securely
In SPO/E Core, administrators can restrict modifications to documents by applying secure privileges. This way, only privileged users can check out, check in, re-classify, and update a document securely.
If you need to edit a document securely in the Desktop Client, use the Check Out (Secure) command to check out the document. After you have edited the document, you can check in the document to the Desktop Client by using the
Check In (secure) command.
Check out a document securely
1. Right-click the document you want to modify, then click Edit > Check Out (Secure).
2. In the Validate dialog box, type the purpose for checking out the document in the Purpose box.
3. Type the password in the Password box, and click Save.
4. In the Check Out dialog box, browse to select a location you want to check out the document.
5. Click OK. The document is checked out, and the purpose for checking out the document is saved and is displayed under the ISPXIntentionOfChange section in the Properties window.
Cancel a check out
Using the Undo Checkout (Secure) command, you can relinquish your claim to a document you have checked out so that other users can check it out to modify.
1. Right-click the document for which you want to cancel the checkout operation and click Edit > Undo Check Out (Secure).
2. In the Undo Check Out (Secure) dialog box, select the document and files for which you want to cancel the checkout operation.
3. Click OK.
Only the user that checked out the document can undo the checkout.
When you cancel a checkout, the software deletes the latest working copy (for example, Rev A Version 3) and resets the previous version (such as Rev A Version 2) as the latest working document. It also changes the revision state
for the previous revision from superseded to blank, and deletes any markups for files attached to the checked out document.
Check in a document securely
1. Right-click the document you want to check in, and click Edit > Check In (Secure).
2. In the Validate dialog box, type the purpose for checking in the document in the Purpose box.
3. Type the password in the Password box, and click Save.
4. In the Check In dialog box, select the document to check in.
5. Click OK. The document is checked in, and the purpose for checking in the document is saved and is displayed under the ISPXIntentionOfChange section in the Properties window.
Dossiers
A dossier is a collection of structured documents that contain sections or chapters containing other documents. You can create various types of dossiers such as Vendor Databook, Fabrication Record, Systems Dossier, and so on.
Create a dossier chapter
You can create two types of dossier chapters:
Current Revision - This chapter displays the latest version of the attached document.
Specific Revision - This chapter displays the specific version of the attached document.
1. Right-click the dossier, then click Manage Chapters.
2. In the Manage Chapters dialog box, click Create.
3. In the Item dialog box, enter the chapter name in the Name box.
4. Select a chapter type from the Chapter Type list. By default, Current Revision is displayed as the chapter type. To relate, drag the object onto the dossier, and select the object in the New Relationships dialog box, then click OK.
5. To attach a document to the Current Revision chapter type, click Find. All of the documents are displayed in the Document Master box.
6. Select the check box in the Document Master box to attach the selected document to the dossier chapter.
7. Click Finish.
8. In the Manage Chapters dialog box, click OK. The chapter is created and displayed in the New Items window.
You can attach a document to the Specific Revision chapter type the same way.
You can attach a document to a chapter by dragging it onto the chapter type.
To attach files to a chapter, select File from the Attach list. Right-click the chapter in the New-Items window, and click Attach file.
Reorder dossier chapters
1. Right-click the dossier, and click Manage Chapters.
2. In the Manage Chapters dialog box, use 
or 
 to reorder the chapters. All of the chapters are displayed in ascending order according to their Order value. By default, 10 is set as the Order value.
Copy dossier
1. Right-click the dossier, and click Copy Dossier.
2. In the Dossier Copy dialog box, type the description in the Description box. A copy of the dossier is displayed in the New Items window.
Documents, tags, and discipline related to a dossier are not copied.
The copy of the dossier name is displayed in the New Items window as Dossier Name_Copy.
To view the copied chapters, right-click the dossier copy, and click Show Dossier Chapter.
Relate objects to dossier, dossier documents, and dossier chapters
You can relate objects (such as tags, discipline, model, asset, PO Contract, task, etc.) to a dossier, dossier document, and dossier chapter. To relate an object to a dossier, use the following steps:
1. Drag the selected object onto the dossier.
2. Click OK in the New Relationship dialog box.
 You can drag files directly from Windows Explorer onto existing dossier chapters in the List view.
Generate a PDF of a dossier
 To support the multi-level hierarchy of dossiers and dossier chapters, parameter 1, GraphDef to navigate to the files and parameter 2, Specify dynamic expression for merge order, of the
SPXGenerateMergedRendition client API are not supported with SPO/E Core methods SPXDossierCurrentPDF and SPXDossierCurrentWorkingPDF.
You can combine PDFs of all the documents and files attached to a dossier and its chapters into a single merged PDF.
 PDF generation of a dossier will be faster if you first generate the individual PDF for each file attached to the documents or dossier chapters. For more information on generating PDF for a file, see Generating PDFs.
How are documents attached to a dossier organized?
When you generate a PDF of a dossier, the documents and files attached to dossier chapters are organized according to the Order Value of the dossier chapters.
1. Right-click the dossier, and click Generate Merged PDF > Current Documents.
2. Select the option for the documents and files you want to include in the PDF of dossier:
Select Current Documents to include only signed off documents. This means all documents with a revision state of Current are included in the PDF.
Select Current and Working (if NO Current) Documents to include both signed off documents and the latest drafts of other documents that are not yet signed off. This means all documents with a revision state of either
Current or Working are included in the PDF.
A scheduler task for PDF generation is created.
 After the scheduler task is successfully processed, you can view the PDF. To do so, right-click the dossier and click View Merged Rendition.
Add a watermark to the PDF of a dossier
1. Click Find > Administration > Method.
2. Find the SPXDossierCurrentPDF method, right-click, and click Update.
3. In the Update – Current Documents dialog box, select the Include watermarks in PDFs check box.
4. In the Watermark name box, type the name of the configured watermark, and click OK. For more information on creating a watermark, see Create a new watermark.
You can update the SPXDossierCurrentWorkingPDF method to include a watermark in the PDF generated for Current and Working documents.
If you generate PDFs for all files individually prior to generating a single merged PDF for a dossier, the watermark set on the dossier takes precedence over and replace any watermarks set on individual files.
Configure bookmarks in the PDF of a dossier
You can configure the display of bookmarks in the PDF. By default, the bookmarks are displayed using the [Document name & revision details: Attached File name] format. For example: [0000-AB-A-0003 01A: File A.txt]. In this example,
“0000-AB-A-0003” represents the document name, “01A” represents the document revision details, and “File A” represents the name of the attached file.
To configure a different format for displaying the bookmarks in the PDF, follow these steps:
1. Click Find > Administration > Method.
2. Find the SPXDossierCurrentPDF method, right-click it, and click Update.
3. In the Specify dynamic expression for bookmark box on the Update – Current Documents dialog box, type your preferred format for bookmarks.
 You can update the SPXDossierCurrentWorkingPDF method to configure bookmarks in the PDF generated for Current and Working documents.
Export dossier structure
You can export a dossier structure, as well as the files attached to a dossier, dossier chapters, and sub-chapters, in a CSV format from SPO/E Core to the target system using the Exchange Data functionality. For more information on
configuring the Exchange Data functionality, see Configure the Exchange Data functionality.
The .csv file generated must be validated using the Intergraph Data Validator before exporting it to the target system. For more information on setting up Data Validator for validating data, see Setting up Data Validator for Data Validation.
Generate a .csv file to export the dossier structure to the target system
1. Find the DossierExportConfig report configuration object, right-click it, and click Update.
 If you are creating a new report configuration object, you must select the Is CSV repeated check box. For more information on creating a new report configuration object, see Report configuration.
2. In the Update DossierExportConfig dialog box, in the Export directory path text box, specify a location to save the .csv file in, and click OK.
3. Right-click the dossier, and click Exchange Dossier data. The .csv file and the files attached to dossier, dossier chapters, and sub-chapters are saved in the location specified in the DossierExportConfig report configuration object.
You can export multiple dossiers from SPO/E Core to the target system simultaneously.
The delivered recursive relationship definition, SPXDossierChapterComposition, is used to expand objects recursively. You can also configure a custom recursive relationship definition and associate it with the report configuration
object. For more information, see Manage view definition options.
The target system and source system should have the same ENS configuration.
Validate data using Data Validator and export it to the target system
1. Log on to the Data Validator Job Management module and create a new job. To create a new job and run it, see Create a job.
 When creating a new job, select the Suppress ENS check box to suppress the creation of objects with ENS in the target system for this job.
2. Download and view the validation report and take any necessary corrective actions. To download the validation report, see Download a validation report.
3. If validation is successful, click the Approve Workflow Step icon to approve the job and export data to target system. For more information on approving the validation report, see Approve the validation report.
4. View the exported data in the SPO/E Core authoring domain in the target system.
Tags
You can create two types of tags in SPO/E Core:
Central Tag Allocation Tag Types - These tag types are supported for Central Tag Allocation process, and can participate in integration work processes with other Smart Plant engineering tools.
Non Integrated Tag Types - These tag types do not participate in integration work process.
Create a tag
You can create a CTA tag using the following steps:
1. Click File > New > Central Tag Allocation Tag Type.
2. In the Create Tag dialog box, expand the tag classes to select a tag.
3. Click Next.
4. On the Main details tab, select a value from the Plant, Area, and Unit lists.
5. Enter the information in the required boxes.
6. Click Finish.
 You can create a Non Integrated tag in the same way by clicking File > New > Non Integrated Tag Type.
 You can also create CTA tags and Non Integrated tags from the Tree View window. To create, expand the required tag class to select a tag. Right-click the selected tag, and click Create Tag.
Manage tags
1. Right-click the selected tag, and click Tag > Update Tag.
2. In the Update Tag dialog box, edit the information in the required boxes.
3. Click Finish.
4. To copy a tag, right-click the selected tag, and click Tag > Copy Tag.
5. In the Copy Tag dialog box, enter the information in the required boxes.
6. Click Finish.
7. To delete a tag, right-click the selected tag, and click Tag > Delete Tag.
8. Click OK.
9. To terminate a tag, right-click the selected tag, and click Tag > Terminate Tag.
10. Click OK.
Relate tags to documents, work packages, and other tags
You can relate a tag to a document, work package, or to other tags. To relate a tag to a work package, follow these steps:
1. Drag the selected tag onto the work package.
2. Click OK in the New Relationship dialog box.
 You can relate a tag to a document or to other tags in the same way.
Export tags
In an integrated environment, when SPO/E Core is integrated with other software (such as Smart P&ID and Smart Instrumentation, you can easily export tags from SPO/E Core to other software.
 When you export tags from SPO/E Core to Smart P&ID, an .xml file is generated because Smart P&ID supports .xml files in a pre-defined format to import tags. In case of Smart Instrumentation, when tags are exported from
SPO/E Core, an .xlsm file is generated.
Export tags to Smart P&ID
1. Right-click the work package to which you have related tags, and click Publish/Export > Export Tags for SmartPlant P&ID.
2. Browse to select the location where you want to save the .xml file.
3. Click Save.
Export tags to Smart Instrumentation
1. Right-click the work package to which you have related tags, and click Publish/Export > Export Tags for SmartPlant Instrumentation.
2. Browse to select the location where you want to save the .xlsm file.
3. Click Save.
Retrieve tags in the SPO/E authoring domain
Central Tag Allocation (CTA) must be installed and configured for publishing and retrieving tags from various Smart Engineering tools such Smart P&ID, Smart Instrumentation, and Smart Electrical.
Tag classifications which are mapped in SPO/E Core can only be retrieved from the various Smart Engineering tools.
You can retrieve tags published from various Smart Engineering tools such as Smart P&ID, Smart Electrical, and Smart Instrumentation, in the SPO/E authoring domain.
After retrieving the tags, you can update them. You can add additional tag properties and relationship definitions to the authoring tags, relate them to a work package, then publish the work package. The Smart Engineering tools can then
retrieve these updated tags from the published document.
1. Click SmartPlant > Central Tag Allocation > Retrieve.
2. In the Retrieve dialog box, select the document type you want to retrieve from the Document type list.
 To filter the list of documents, select one of the check boxes:
If you are retrieving a published document for the first time, then select the New documents check box.
If you are retrieving a new version of the published document, then select the New versions of retrieved documents check box.
If you are retrieving a document version you had retrieved earlier, but did not make modifications to, then select the Unchanged documents check box.
3. Select the document from the Documents to retrieve section.
 Select Batch retrieve if you want the retrieve process to run in batch mode. If you select this check box, the retrieve process runs in the background. If you have configured email notification, an email message will alert you
when the process is complete.
