4. Click OK.
5. Find the retrieved document by clicking Find > Documents > CTA Retrieved Documents.
6. Right-click the retrieved document, and click Show Retrieved Authoring Tags to view all the retrieved tags attached to the document in the SPO/E authoring domain.
To view the date and time when the tags were last retrieved, check the Last Retrieved Date property in the Properties window. You can also view the Last Retrieved Date and Retrieved Documents properties as two columns in
the List View window when querying retrieved tags.
To navigate from the retrieved tag to the retrieved document, right-click the tag and click Show Retrieved Documents.
Create a property to publish and retrieve tags during integration
You can create tag properties in the SPO/E authoring domain to publish or retrieve them from various Smart Engineering tools. Identify a property to add with common interface and define it in .xlsm files.
Add a property to the SPO/E authoring domain
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm file. After logging into the Smart Community portal, select DOWNLOADS > Information Management 2019 >
Freeware Tools and Utilities > Samples to download the sample file.
 For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. Create a new or use an existing ELF of the SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm file.
3. Add a new row to the PropertyDef sheet, and define the property Name, UID, Scope By Rel, Exposes Interface Rel and Product, and save the .xlsm file.
Generate .xml files using SANTA
1. Launch SPO SANTA.
2. On the Browse tab, click Browse to locate and open the modified SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm file.
3. Select Integration from the Solution list and select the Schema Editor check box.
4. Click Generate LoadFiles to generate the .xml file for SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm.
5. You can find the santa generated SCHEMA_SPO_Integration.xml file at the same location as SCHEMA_SPO..xlsm.
 Repeat steps 3-5 while selecting SPO from the Solution list and deselect the Schema Editor check box to generate the PropertyDef_SPO.xml.
Load files in SmartPlant Desktop Client
1. Load the PropertyDef_SPO.xml delta file using the loader.
2. Add the Schema_SPO_Integration.xml delta to the pseudo file and include it in the EFSchema.
Add a property to CDW Schema
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm file. After logging into the Smart Community portal, select DOWNLOADS > Information Management 2019 >
Freeware Tools and Utilities > Samples to download the sample file.
 For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. Create a new or use an existing ELF of the SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm file.
3. Add a new row to the PropertyDef sheet, and define the property Name, UID, Scope By Rel, Exposes Interface Rel and Product, and save the .xlsm file.
4. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the DATA_CDW.xlsm file. After logging into the Smart Community portal, select DOWNLOADS > Information Management 2019 >
Freeware Tools and Utilities > Samples to download the sample file.
5. Add a new row to the PDef-PDef sheet, and define the properties UID1, and UID2, and save the .xlsm file.
 UID1 and UID2 represent the authoring property UID and CDW property UID respectively.
Generate .xml files using SANTA
1. Launch SPO SANTA.
2. On the Browse tab, click Browse to locate and open the modified SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm file.
3. Select SPO from the Solution list and select the Schema Editor check box.
4. Click Generate LoadFiles to generate the .xml file for SCHEMA_SPO.xlsm file.
5. Repeat steps 2-4 to generate load files from modified DATA_CDW.xlsm file.
Load files in SmartPlant Desktop Client
1. Load the PropertyDef_SPO.xml and PDef-PDef_SPO.xml delta files using the loader.
Map properties with Smart Engineering tools
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the SPX Tag - ToolMapSchema - SPFMappings.xls file. After logging into the Smart Community portal, select DOWNLOADS >
Information Management 2019 > Freeware Tools and Utilities > Samples to download the sample file.
 For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. Create a new or use an existing ELF of the SPX Tag - ToolMapSchema - SPFMappings.xls file.
3. Add a new row to define the SPMapPropertyDef, MapPropertyToProperty, and MapClassMapProperties, and save the .xls file.
Generate .xml files using SANTA
1. Launch SPO SANTA.
2. On the Browse tab, click Browse to locate and open the modified SPX Tag - ToolMapSchema - SPFMappings.xls file.
3. Select SPO from the Solution list.
4. Click Generate LoadFiles, to generate the .xml file for SPX Tag - ToolMapSchema - SPFMappings.xls.
5. You can find the santa generated ToolmapSchema_SPO.xml file at the same location as SPX Tag - ToolMapSchema - SPFMappings.xls file.
6. Add the ToolmapSchema_SPO.xml (ToolMapSchema_CTAVersion) delta to the pseudo file and include it in the EFSchema.
Work Packages
A work package is a collection of any information required to perform a task and is used in many ways throughout SPO/E Core and all of SmartPlant Enterprise.
Create a work package
1. Click File > New > Work Package Management > Work Package.
2. In the New Work Package dialog box, expand the work package types to select a work package, and click Next.
3. Type the required information, and click Finish.
 You can also create a work package from the Tree view. To create, expand the work package class in the Tree View to select a work package. Right-click the selected work package type, and click Create Work Package.
Create a work package type
1. Click File > New > Organization / Support Items > Work Package Type.
2. In the New Work Package Type dialog box, enter the name in the Name box.
3. Type the required information, and click Finish.
 A new work package is created and displayed in the New Items window.
Publish SPO/E Core work packages
You can publish work packages to the Consolidated Data Warehouse (CDW) domain in four ways:
Publish - When you select this option for publishing, you cannot work on the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client until the publish operation completes.
Background Publish - When you select this option for publishing, the publish operation runs in the background. This allows you to work without interruptions in the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
Schedule Publish - When you select this option for publishing, a scheduler task for the publish operation is created.
Auto Publish - You can select this option to automate the process of publishing the tags.
Publish a work package
1. Click Find > Work Packages.
2. In the Find Work Packages dialog box, type the work package name or type "*", and click OK.
3. Right-click the selected work package, and click Publish/Export > Publish Tags.
 When publishing work packages, you can enable the ExcludeIndirectTagRelsIfNotPublished option on the WPTagPublishMethod to exclude publishing indirect tag relationships when indirect tags do not exist in the CDW
domain.
4. Type your user credentials, and click OK.
5. In the Publish dialog box, select appropriate values from the Owning Group, Revision Scheme and Publish Type lists.
6. From the Operation list, select Publish, and click OK. When the work package is published successfully, a message box lets you know that the publish operation was successful.
 From SPO/E Core 2014 R3 onwards, you can publish multiple work packages by repeating the same steps. To select multiple work packages, press the CTRL key when you select the required work packages.
 To publish the work packages in the background, select Background Publish from the Operation list.
Publish a work package using Schedule Publish
 Before publishing work packages using the Schedule Publish option, you must ensure Intergraph SmartPlant .Net 2019 (COM) software is installed on the Application Server. For more information, please contact your
administrator for further assistance.
1. Click Find > Work Packages.
2. In the Find Work Packages dialog box, type the work package name or type "*", and click OK.
3. Right-click the selected work package, and click Schedule publish tags.
4. In the Create Schedule Publish Task dialog box, in the Name box, type the task's name.
5. In the Schedule Time box, select the time for running the schedule publish task.
6. In the Description box, type the description for the task.
7. Select a revision scheme from the Revision Scheme list.
8. Select an owning group from the Owning Group list.
9. Select a workflow from the Workflow list, and click OK. A scheduler task for the publish operation is created.
Publish a work package using Auto Publish
You can use the Auto Publish option to automate the publishing of tags.
Before publishing work packages using the Auto Publish option, you must make sure that the Intergraph SmartPlant .NET 2019 software is installed on the application server. For more information, please contact your administrator
for further assistance.
By default, the Auto Publish option publishes tags that you have created and updated. If you want to publish terminated tags and relationships using the Auto Publish option, then you must configure the
SPOCUpdateTombStoneFile process step as the first step of the workflow used for publishing the tags. For example, if you are using the AutoLoadPublish workflow for publishing tags, then you must configure the
SPOCUpdateTombStoneFile process step as the first step of the AutoLoadPublilsh workflow.
1. Click File > New > Work Package Management > Auto Publish > Task.
2. In the Create Automatic Publish task dialog box, type a name and description for the task.
3. In the Schedule Time box, select the time when you want the automated publish task to run.
4. Select your preferred frequency of running the auto publishing task from the Frequency list. For example, to publish tags daily, select the Publish Daily option.
5. Type the condition for selecting the tags that need to be published. For example, to select tags with the LifeCycleState of Reserved, type [obj.LifeCycleState=’ Reserved’] in the Condition box.
6. From the Revision Scheme list, select a revision scheme for the document to be created during publishing.
7. From the Owning Group list, select the owning group for the document to be created during publishing.
8. Select the workflow for publishing the tags from the Workflow list, and click OK. A scheduler task for the automated publish operation is created. The task automatically relates the tags to work packages, and then publishes the work
packages.
To disable the scheduler used for Auto Publish operation, AutoPublishScheduler, click File > New > Work Package Management > Auto Publish > Disable.
To delete a specific task created for Auto Publish, use the following steps:
a. Click the Find Schedule Tasks 
 icon.
b. Using the Find Schedule Tasks dialog box, find the task.
c. Right-click the task, and click Delete.
Publish motor and generator tags
In an integrated environment, the following rules apply to publishing motor and generator tags in SPO/E Core:
To publish a motor tag that will be retrieved by Smart Electrical, you must first create the motor tag with the EEM, Motor tag classification (Central Tag Allocation Tag Types > Electrical Equipment > Electrical Load) in SPO/E
Core. Similarly, you must create the generator tag with the EEGen, Generator tag classification (Central Tag Allocation Tag Types > Electrical Equipment> Off-site Power) in SPO/E Core.
To publish a motor tag that will be retrieved by Smart P&ID, you must first create the motor tag with the PEDEM, Electric motor tag classification (Central Tag Allocation Tag Types > Equipment > Process-equipment driver) in
SPO/E Core. Similarly, you must create the generator tag with the PEDEG, Electric generator tag classification (Central Tag Allocation Tag Types > Equipment > Process-equipment driver) in SPO/E Core.
Check work package status
You can view the work package status in the Work Package Details section in the New Work Package dialog box. By default, this status is set to Ready when a new work package is created. The various work package and publish
statuses are described in the table below:
Work package status
Publish status
Description
Ready
In Progress
The publish operation is in progress because the process
of data load or consolidation of the published document is
in progress.
Published
Successful
The publish operation was successfully completed. The
process of data load and consolidation of the published
document was successfully completed.
Failed
Failed
The publish operation failed because the process of data
load or consolidation of the published document failed.
Ready
Queued
The publish operation is queued because the process of
data load or consolidation of the published document is
queued.
Ready
Canceled
The publish operation failed because the published
document was canceled.
 You can also view the work package status if the work package is published using the Background Publish option.
Configure notification email
You can configure the user profile to receive email notifications on the success or failure of the publish operation.
1. In the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client, click Find > Administration > User.
2. In the Find User search box, type the user name, and click OK.
3. Right-click the user name, and click Update to open the Update dialog box.
4. Provide the required email address in the E-mail address box, and click Finish.
Set the work package limit
The work package limit determines the number of work packages that can be published simultaneously.
1. In the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client, click Find > Administration > Method to open the Find Work Package dialog box.
2. Type WPTagPublishMethod, and click OK.
3. Right-click on WPTagPublishMethod, and click Update.
4. Type the required work package limit value in the WorkpackageLimit box.
 The work package limit is set to 10 by default.
5. Click Finish.
Organization/Support objects
You can create a variety of Organization / Support objects such as authority, contractor, Owner Corporate, Owner field/Area, Owner Plant/Installation, Project Central Team, Project Site Team, and so on.
Create an organization/support object
You can create an Organization/Support object by clicking File > New > Organization/Support Items, and then clicking the required object.
For example, use the following steps to create an authority object:
1. Click File > New > Organization/Support Items > Authority.
2. In the New Authority dialog box, in the Organization section, enter the name in the Name box.
3. Select a value from the Category and Status lists.
4. In the Relationships section, select a value from the G/B Field Project list.
5. In the Commercial and Schedule Issues section, select a value from the Interface (IC) Deliveries list.
6. In the References section, select a value from the Originator list.
7. Enter the information in the required boxes.
8. Click Finish.
 You can create other Organization/Support objects the same way.
Create originator and discipline codes
Use the following steps to create originator codes:
1. Click File > New > Organization/Support Items > Originator.
2. In the Originator dialog box, enter the code in the Code box.
3. Enter the description in the Description box, if required.
4. Click Finish.
 You can also create a new originator code from the Tree View window by right-clicking the Originator Codes in the Tree view, and clicking Create New Originator Code.
 You can create discipline codes the same way by clicking File > New > Organization/Support Items > Discipline.
Manage originator and discipline codes
You can update, terminate, delete, and copy an originator code using the following steps:
1. Right-click the selected originator code, and click Edit > Update.
2. In the Update dialog box, update the description for the originator code.
3. Click Finish.
4. To copy an originator code, click Edit > Copy.
5. In the Copy dialog box, enter the information in the Code box.
6. Enter the description in the Description box, if required.
7. Click OK.
8. To delete an originator code, click Edit > Delete.
9. To terminate, click Edit > Terminate.
 You can update, terminate, delete, and copy discipline codes the same way.
Models and assets
You can create a variety of objects (such as models, assets, manufacturers, and suppliers) using Model and Asset Management (MAM).
Create a manufacturer
1. Open the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Manufacturer.
3. In the New Manufacturer dialog box, enter the name in the Name box.
4. Type the required information, and click Finish.
Create an asset
1. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Asset.
2. In the New Asset dialog box, expand the material classes to select an asset.
3. Click Next.
4. In the Main details section, enter the name in the Name box.
5. Type the required information, and click Finish.
 You can also create an asset from the Tree View. To create, expand the Material Types in the Tree View. Right-click on the selected material class, and click Create Asset.
Create a new model
 You must create a manufacturer before creating a model.
1. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Model.
2. In the New Model dialog box, expand the material classes to select a model, and click Next.
3. In the Main details section, enter the name in the Name box.
4. In the Model Relationships section, select a manufacturer from the Manufacturer list, and click Finish.
 You can also create a model from the Tree View. To create, expand the Material Types in the Tree View. Right-click the selected material class, and click Create Model.
Create a supplier
1. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Supplier.
2. In the New Supplier dialog box, in the Main details section, enter the name in the Name box.
3. Type the required information, and click Finish.
Collaboration objects
Using the Collaboration feature, you can create and attach notes and photos to documents.
Create collaboration objects
1. Click File > New > Collaboration > Picture.
2. In the New Picture dialog box, in the Main details section, enter the name in the Name box.
3. In the Note/Comment section, enter the information in the Long Notes box.
4. Enter the information in the Short Notes box, if required.
5. Click Finish.
You can create notes/comments the same way by clicking File > New > Collaboration > Note/Comment.
You can create a picture or note/comment object by right-clicking the document, and clicking Create CLLB.
Relate collaboration objects to documents
1. Drag the collaboration object onto the document.
2. Click OK in the New Relationship dialog box.
Transmittals
A transmittal is a controlled package of documents with an accompanying report used to perform, record, and track the distribution of project documentation between different design teams, subcontractors, vendors, and construction sites
involved in a project. It is a collection object that links a set of documents to a set of recipients with reasons for receipt.
Recipient requirements are determined by internal and external distribution matrices, and what is required of those people. A distribution matrix is a table with recipients along the top and transmittal categories down the side. Transmittal
categories can be based on document type, project, contract, and so on. Each cell inside the table is marked with each recipient's reason for receipt.
 Distribution matrices are created separately for internal and external transmittals.
The transmittal report is attached to the collection object and is e-mailed to the recipients upon issue of the transmittal.
Create an incoming transmittal
When should I create an incoming transmittal?
You can create incoming transmittals to share documents such as control documentation, safety procedures, and so on between different departments within an EPC or Owner Operator company.
1. Click File > New > Transmittals > Incoming Transmittal.
2. In the New Incoming Transmittal dialog box, in the Incoming transmittal details section, select a value from the Document type list.
3. In the Owning group section, select a value from the Item owning group list.
4. In the From details section, select a value from the From internal organization list.
5. In the To details section, select a value from the To organization list.
6. Type the required information, and click Finish.
 You can create an internal transmittal the same way by clicking File > New > Transmittals > Internal Transmittal.
Create an outgoing transmittal
When should I create an outgoing transmittal?
You must create outgoing transmittals to share documents with reviewers at other companies.
1. Click File > New > Transmittals > Outgoing Transmittal.
2. In the New Outgoing Transmittal dialog box, in the Outgoing transmittal details section, select a value from the Document type and Company lists.
3. In the To details section, select a value from the To organization list.
4. In the Owning group section, select a value from the Item owning group list.
5. In the From details section, select a value from the From internal organization list.
6. Type the required information, and click Finish.
Create SPO/E Core objects using Microsoft Excel workbooks
Create objects using Microsoft Excel workbooks
You can create various SPO/E Core objects, such as documents, dossiers, tags, models, assets, and so on, by modifying the Microsoft Excel workbooks available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . After
logging into the Smart Community portal, select DOWNLOADS > Information Management 2019 > Freeware Tools and Utilities > Samples to access the workbooks.
Documents
The sample ELFs of the ADMIN_SPO, SCHEMA_SPO, USER_SPO, and WORKFLOW_SPO workbooks are available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> .
 We recommend that you create a backup copy of the original workbooks before making any modifications to them.
You can refer to the sample USER_SPO.xlsm workbook to configure various documents such as engineering, procurement, project control, vendor document, email, and admin documents.
You can modify the USER_SPO.xlsm workbook to create new documents, and load them to the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
Create a document
The following example shows creating an Engineering document by modifying the USER_SPO workbook.
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the USER_SPO.xlsm file. For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. In the Engineering Document sheet, add a new row in the Engineering Document Master section.
3. In the Name column, type the name of the document according to the default defined ENS definition for engineering document, SPXEngineeringDocumentENS. For example, if you type [EngDoc_0000-ED-A-0007] in the Name
column, then in this example, the first four characters, “0000” represents the Project. The second two characters, “ED” represents the Originator. The third character, “A” represents the Discipline, and the last four characters, "0007"
represent the Sequence.
4. In the Description column, type the description for the document.
5. In the Plant column, type the plant to which the engineering document is related using the [PL_PlantName] format. For example, for Plant A, type [PL_PlantA] in the Plant column.
6. In the Area column, type the area using the [AR_Area No.] format. For example, for area 10, type [AR_10] in the Area column.
7. In the Unit column, type the unit using the [UNT_UnitNo] format. For example, for unit 20, type [UNT_20] in the Unit column.
8. In the Originator column, type the originator code using the [SPXOriginator_Originator Code] format. For example, for originator code "AB", type [SPXOriginator_AB].
9. In the Discipline column, type the discipline using the [SPXDiscipline_DisciplineCode] format. For example, for discipline "A", type [SPXDiscipline_A].
10. In the UID column, type the UID of the document master using the [Prefix of Documentclassification_DocumentName] format. For example for Engineering document, “0000-ED-A-0007”, type [EngDoc_0000-ED-A-0007] in the UID
column.
11. Click File > Save to save the workbook by renaming it as USER_SPO_DMS.xlsm.
Create document revisions and versions
The following example shows you how to create document revisions and versions for an engineering document:
1. In the Engineering Document sheet, add a new row in the Engineering Documents Revision section, and type the name and description of the document revision in the Name and Description columns.
2. In the Revision Scheme column, type the revision scheme details using the [RS_RevisionSchemaName] format. For example, if you type [RS_Rev01A] in the Revision Scheme column, then “01” represents the Major Revision and
“A” represents the Minor revision of the document.
3. In the Master (Rel) column, type the UID of the document master to which the document revision is related.
4. In the UID column, type the UID of the document revision using the [EngDocRev_DocumentName] format. For example, for an engineering document master "EngDoc_0000-ED-A-0007", you can define the UID for document revision
as [EngDocRev_ 0000-ED-A-0007] in the UID column.
5. To create versions, add a new row in the Engineering Document Versions section, and in the UID column, type the UID of the document version using the [EngDocVer_DocumentName] format. For example, for an engineering
document "0000-ED-A-0007" you can type [EngDocVer_0000-ED-A-0007] in the UID column.
6. In the Data Access Group column, type the Access Group using the [DAG_AccesGroupType] format. For example, if you type, [DAG_OPEN_TO_ALL] then the document version can be accessed by all the users.
7. Click File > Save to save the renamed workbook.
Attach files to a document
1. Add a new row in the Files section, and in the Name column, type the file name.
2. In the SPFLocalDirectory column, type the location where you have saved the file.
3. In the SPFLocalFileName column, type the file name as saved in the local host computer.
4. In the SPFLocalHostName column, type the local host computer name.
5. To attach file to a document version, add a new row in the SPFFileComposition section. In the UID1 column, type the UID of the file, and in the UID2 column, type the UID of the document version. For example, for attaching file to
an engineering document, "EngDocVer_0000-ED-A-0007", type [File_EngDoc] in the UID1 column, and [EngDocVer_0000-ED-A-0007] in the UID 2 column.
6. In the UID column, type the UID for defining relationship between the document version and attached file using the [UID1.UID2] format. In this example, type [FileEngDoc.EngDocVer_0000-ED-A-0007] in the UID column.
7. Add a new row in the SPFUploadInstructions section, and define the file upload instructions.
8. Click File > Save to save the renamed workbook.
Dossiers
 We recommend that you create a backup copy of the original workbooks before making any modifications to them.
The USER_SPO Microsoft Excel workbook is provided on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community. The workbook contains some default
configurations for dossiers. You can create dossiers by modifying the dossier sheet of USER_SPO Microsoft Excel workbook.
Create dossier
1. Open the USER_SPO.xlsm file, and in the Dossier sheet, add a new row in the SPXDossier section.
2. In the Name column, type the name of the dossier.
3. In the Description column, type the description.
4. In the Primary Classification column, type the dossier classification using the [SPXDossier_Dossierclassifcation] format. For example, to create a System Dossier, type [SPXDossier_SystemsDossier] in the Primary
Classification column.
5. In the UID column, type the UID of the dossier using the [SPXDossier.Name] format. For example, if D2 is the name of the dossier, then type [SPXDossier.D2] in the UID column.
6. Click File > Save to save the workbook.
Create dossier chapter
1. Add a new row in the SPXDossierChapter section, and in the Name column, type the name for the dossier chapter. For example, D2-C2.
2. In the Parent Dossier or Chapter column, type the UID of the dossier or dossier chapter to which this dossier chapter is related. For example, if this chapter is related to dossier “D2”, then type [SPXDossier.D2] in the Parent
Dossier or Chapter column.
3. In the UID column, type the UID of the dossier chapter. For example, you can type [SPXDossier.D2D2C2] in the UID column.
4. Click File > Save to save the workbook.
Attach documents to a dossier and a dossier chapter
1. Add a new row in the SPXDossierCompDocument section, and in the UID1 column, type the UID of the dossier chapter to which you want to attach the document. For example, in the UID1 column, type [SPXDossier.D2D2C2].
2. In the UID2 column, type the UID of the document revision which you want to attach to the dossier chapter. To create a Current Revision chapter, type the UID of the latest document revision. For creating Specific Revision chapter,
type the UID of the specific document revision which you want to attach.
3. To define the relationship between the dossier chapter and the document revision, in the UID column, type the UID using the [UID1.UID2] format. In this example, in the UID column, type [SPXDossier.D2D2C2. EngDocRev_0000-
ED-A-0007].
4. Click File > Save to save the workbook.
 You can attach a document to the dossier the same way.
Relate disciplines with a dossier and a dossier chapter
You can relate disciplines to a dossier and a dossier chapter using these following steps:
1. To relate a discipline to a dossier, add a new row in the SPXDossierCompDiscipline section, and in the UID1 column, type the UID of dossier. For example, “SPXDossierD2”.
2. In the UID2 column, type the UID of the discipline. For example, “SPXDiscipline_B”.
3. In the UID column, type the UID to define a relationship between the dossier and the discipline, using the [UID1.UID2] format. In this example, type [SPXDossierD2.SPXDiscipline_B] in the UID column.
4. Click File > Save to save the workbook.
 You can relate disciplines to dossier chapters the same way.
Tags
The sample ELFs of the ADMIN_SPO, SCHEMA_SPO, USER_SPO, and WORKFLOW_SPO workbooks are available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> .
 We recommend that you create a backup copy of the original workbooks before making any modifications to them.
Create a USER_SPO.xlsm file containing information on tags and their relationship with plant, area, unit etc. Depending on the tag types, these tags are defined in various sheets in the USER_SPO.xlsm file.
Create tags
You can create a new tag using the USER_SPO Microsoft Excel workbook. The following example shows you how to create a new electrical tag “Heater” using the USER_SPO.xlsm file.
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the USER_SPO.xlsm file. For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. Add a new row in the TagElectrical sheet, and in the Name column, type the name of the tag according to the default defined ENS definition, SPXAllTagsENS-UNIT. In this example, type [EE13] in the Name column.
3. In the UID column, type the UID of the tag. In this example, type [EE13] in the UID column.
4. In the Description column, type the description of the tag. In this example, type [Heater] in the Description column.
5. In the Classification column, type the tag classification. In this example, type [TAG_EEH] in the Classification column.
6. In the Plant column, type the plant to which you want to relate this tag using the [PL_Plantname] format. For example, for Plant A, type [PL_PlantA] in the Plant column.
7. In the Area column, type the area using the [AR_Area number] format. In this example type [AR_0] in the Area column.
8. In the Unit column, type the unit using the [UNT_Unit number] format. In this example, type [UNT_10] in the Unit column.
9. Click File > Save to save the workbook by renaming it as USER_SPO_TAG.xlsm.
Originators and disciplines
The sample ELFs of the ADMIN_SPO, SCHEMA_SPO, USER_SPO, and WORKFLOW_SPO workbooks are available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> .
 We recommend that you create a backup copy of the original workbooks before making any modifications to them.
You can modify the DATA_SPO.xlsm to create new originators and disciplines.
Create originator codes
The following example shows you how to create a new originator code, “AB” for “ABB-Global Engineering” organization using the DATA_SPO.xlsm file.
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the DATA_SPO.xlsm file. For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. In the Originator Codes sheet, add a new row in the SPXOriginator section, and in the Name column, type the originator code. In this example, type [AB] in the Name column.
3. In the OriginatorDrilldownTop column, type the UID of the list to which these originator codes are related. By default, the originators are related to Originator Codes list. In this example, type
[SPXOriginatorDrilldown_Originator_Codes] in the OriginatorDrilldownTop column.
4. In the Description column, type the description of the originator. In this example, type [ABB-Global Engineering] in the Description column.
5. In the UID column, type the UID of the originator using the [SPXOriginator_Originator code] format. In this example, type [SPXOriginator_AB] in the UID column.
6. Click File > Save to save the workbook by renaming it as DATA_SPO_CORE.xlsm.
Create discipline codes
The following example shows you how to add a new discipline, “Marine Engineering” using the DATA_SPO_CORE.xlsm file.
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the DATA_SPO.xlsm file. For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. In the Discipline Codes sheet, add a new row in the SPXDiscipline section.
3. In the Name column, type the discipline code. In this example, type [H] in the Name column.
4. In the DisciplineDrilldownTop column, type the UID of the list to which this discipline code is related. By default, the discipline codes are related to Discipline Codes list. In this example type
[SPXDisciplineDrilldown_Engineering_Discipline_Codes] in the DisciplineDrilldownTop column.
5. In the Description column, type the description for the discipline. In this example, type [Marine Engineering] in the Description column.
6. In the UID column, type the UID for the discipline using the [SPXDiscipline_Discipline code] format. In this example, type [SPXDiscipline_A] in the UID column.
7. Click File > Save to save the renamed workbook.
Models, assets, suppliers, and manufacturers
You can create new models, assets, suppliers, and manufacturers using the USER_SPO.xlsm Microsoft Excel workbook.
 We recommend that you create a backup copy of the original workbooks before making any modifications to them.
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the USER_SPO.xlsm file. For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. To add a new supplier, in the Supplier sheet, add a new row in the SPXSupplier section, and type values in the UID, Name, and Description columns.
3. To add a new manufacturer, in the Manufacturer sheet, add a new row in the SPXManufacturer section, and type values in the UID, Name, and Description columns.
4. To add a model, in the Model sheet, add a new row in the SPXModel section, and type values in the UID, Name, and Description columns.
5. In the Material Class (Model Rel) column, type the UID of the material type for the model.
6. Add a new row in the ModelManufacturer section. In the UID1 column, type the UID of the model, and in the UID2 column, type the UID of the manufacturer. In the UID column, define a relationship between model and manufacturer
by typing the UID using the [UID1.UID2] format.
 You can define a relationship between the model and the supplier the same way.
7. To add a new asset, in the Asset sheet, add a new row in the SPXAsset section, and type values in the Name, Description, and UID columns respectively.
8. In the Material Class (Asset Rel) column, type the UID of the material type for the asset.
9. Add a new row in the AssetManufacturer section. In the UID1 column, type the UID of the asset, and in the UID2 column, type the UID of the manufacturer. In the UID column, type the UID to define a relationship between the asset
and the manufacturer using the [UID1.UID2] format.
 You can define relationships between the asset & supplier and model & asset the same way.
10. Click File > Save to save the workbook by renaming it as USER_SPO_MAM.xlsm.
Create material classes
You can create new material types using the DATA_SPO Microsoft Excel workbook. Depending upon the material type, you can add it to the relevant MaterialClass sheet in the DATA_SPO_MAM.xlsm file.
The following example shows you how to add a material type, “Nickel-cadmium battery” to one of the MaterialClass sheet in the DATA_SPO_MAM.xlsm file .
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the DATA_SPO.xlsm file or use the DATA_SPO_MAM.xlsm file if you are creating material class after supplier. For more information, see
Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. In the MaterialClass1 sheet, add a new row in the Material Types section, and type the name of the material in the MaterialClassName1 column. In this example, type [Battery] in the MaterialClassName1 column.
3. In the MaterialClassName2 column, enter the type of the material. In this example, type [Nickel-cadmium] in the MaterialClassName2 column.
4. In the Description column, type the description for the material type. In this example, type [Battery, Nickel-cadmium] in the Description column.
5. In the UID column, type the UID for the material type. In this example, type [SPXMatC_Nickel-cadmium] in the UID column.
6. Click File > Save to save the renamed workbook.
Work packages
The sample ELFs of the ADMIN_SPO, SCHEMA_SPO, USER_SPO, and WORKFLOW_SPO workbooks are available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> .
 We recommend that you create a backup copy of the original workbooks before making any modifications to them.
You can create work package types using the DATA_SPO Microsoft Excel spreadsheet. The following example shows creating a “Construction” work package type:
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> for the sample ELF of the DATA_SPO.xlsm file. For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. In the Work Package sheet, add a new row in the Work Packages Types section, and in the WP Level 1 Name column, type the name of the work package. In this example, type [Engineering Work Package] in the WP Level 1
Name column.
3. In the UID column, type the UID for the work package type. In this example, type [SPXWPC_EngineeringWorkPackage] in the UID column.
4. In the ClassDef (WP Rel) column, type the ClassDef to which this work package type is related.
5. Click File > Save to save the workbook by renaming it as DATA_SPO_WBS.xlsm.
Generate the SPO/E Core load files (.xmldrs)
After modifying the required workbooks, use the following steps to generate the new load files (.xmldrs) to load your changes:
1. Launch SPO SANTA.
2. On the Browse tab, click Browse to locate and open the modified USER_SPO_DMS.xlsm file.
3. Select SPO from the Solution list.
4. Click Generate LoadFiles.
5. Repeat steps 2-4 to generate load files from modified USER_SPO_TAG.xlsm, DATA_SPO_CORE.xlsm, USER_SPO_MAM.xlsm and DATA_SPO_MAM.xlsm, and DATA_SPO_WBS.xlsm respectively.
6. Respective .xmldr are generated at the same location as the .xlsm files.
SANTA generates the following modified load files:
USER_SPO_DMS.xmldr
USER_SPO_TAG.xmldr
DATA_SPO_CORE.xmldr
USER_SPO_MAM.xmldr
DATA_SPO_MAM.xmldr
DATA_SPO_WBS.xmldr
Load the SPO/E Core files (.xmldrs)
1. Log on to SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
2. Set your active scope to the appropriate plant.
3. Click File > Loader.
4. Browse to select the USER_SPO_DMS.xmldr file.
5. Click Process, and wait until the loading is finished.
 A 
 next to an XML file indicates that the file has been processed successfully. A 
 next to an XML file indicates an error in the load file. To fix this error, you need to check the modifications that you made to the
corresponding workbook.
6. Repeat steps 4 and 5 to load other load files.
Document and Tag Management in SPO/E Core
The DMS and TAG are comprehensive sets of work processes to manage centralized allocation of document and tag numbers in SPO/E Core.
Document management in SPO/E Core
The Document Management System in SPO/E Core is a comprehensive set of work process for document management, including the central allocation of document numbers, capture of document metadata, file archiving,
distribution/review, subscription, check in/check out, and the online approval and transmittal internal and external documentation.
This supports full revision history and linking of documents to the PBS objects, tags, referenced documents, contracts, and disciplines. It also facilitates flexible document retrieval and manages engineering documentation/drawings, vendor
documentation, and non-revisable administrative documents, including correspondence.
Central Document Allocation
In an integrated environment, the Central Document Allocation (CDA) feature is used to centralize document numbering which controls and prevents the duplication of document numbers/names in SPO/E Core. The CDA feature applies to
only admin documents, which are non-revisable documents.
After a document number request is recorded in SmartPlant Foundation, the admin document (non-revisable document) is created, which reserves document numbers in SmartPlant Foundation. An email notification including the newly
requested document number is sent to the user of the design tool. The admin document in SmartPlant Foundation exists as long as the design tool does not publish the requested document.
There are two CDA workflows preventing the duplication of admin documents:
DMS_Autoload Publish - Retrieves documents, reports, and drawings from engineering tools. This workflow is used when publishing back from the tool.
DMS_PublishDocument - Reserves document numbers/names in SmartPlant Foundation. In this workflow, the user is notified of the reserved document number to be used in the tool.
 CDA is supported for Smart P&ID, Smart Instrumentation, Smart Electrical, and Smart 3D.
 For more information on configuring CDA, see CDA Configuration in the Technical Documentation for SPO/E Core.
The following example explains how CDA can be used:
In SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client, an admin document of type SPPID already exists and is related to a CDA work package. The work package is attached to the DMS_PublishDocument workflow.
In Smart P&ID, create a drawing using the same as an admin document that already exists in SPO/E, and publish it to SmartPlant Foundation.
By using the CDA feature, bundled with automatic workflows and a few manual system configurations, only the published admin document from the engineering tool is retained while the administrative document created in the
SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client is terminated. All of the properties and relationships are retained for the published administrative document.
Tag management in SPO/E Core
It is essential throughout the plant life cycle that tag numbers are consistently and accurately allocated in accordance with the plant’s Engineering Number System (ENS). Design properties in tag indexes must also be captured and
maintained. Managing tag number allocations during major development projects can be especially onerous and errors can be very costly to rectify, if practicable at all.
SPO/E Core uses Central Tag Allocation and enables the system to be the central place for creating and managing tags.
Central Tag Allocation
The TAG information in SPO/E Core provides the centralized allocation of tag numbers in SmartPlant Foundation, in accordance with a pre-defined Engineering Numbering System (ENS), the maintenance of tag data, and the publishing of
tag details (as supported by existing SmartPlant adapters) to SmartPlant Enterprise design tools.
The central allocation mechanism accommodates legacy data and uses this data when allocating new tag numbers.
The Central Tag Allocation (CTA) system works with the Work Breakdown System, especially with work package objects.
A work package object is a container for published tags which holds a bunch of tags. It also handles republishing of tags.
CTA is a configurable feature.
For more information on installation of CTA, see Configure Central Tag Allocation (CTA).
For more information on extending CTA implementation for new tag types and tag properties, see Extend CTA Implementation.
Office Integration
The Office Integration feature allows you to create or update SPX documents directly from any of the Microsoft Office applications, such as Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
The Office Integration feature has two components, the Microsoft Office Add-in component and the Drag-drop component.
The Microsoft Office Add-in component is a cyclical process where a document can originate in Microsoft Office, and be saved in SmartPlant Foundation, then re-opened. Alternatively, a document saved within SmartPlant Foundation can
be opened within the Microsoft Office Add-in, then checked back into SmartPlant Foundation.
The Drag-drop component enables you to relate files directly including Microsoft Outlook e-mails, by dragging them directly onto a document object in SPO/E Core.
Open a Microsoft Office file from the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client
1. Open the Microsoft Office Word file, on the Add-Ins tab, click Open from SPF.
2. In the Login dialog box, enter the information in the Username and Password boxes.
3. Select the site name from the Server list, and click Edit.
4. In the Modify Site Form dialog box, enter the information in the Name, Host Name, Virtual Dir. Name, Rel to Child Classes, Top Doc Class UID, Class to Class Def rels (1 per line), and QFind Interface (1 per line) boxes.
5. In the SPF/SPO Integration dialog box, in the Quick Find box, type the document name to find the document to which the Microsoft Office Word file is attached.
6. Select the attached Microsoft Office Word file, and click Next.
7. Browse to the location to check out the file.
8. Click OK. You can now open the file and update it.
Save a Microsoft Office file to the SmartPlant Desktop Client
1. Open the Microsoft Office Word file, and on the Add-Ins tab, click Save to SPF.
2. In the Upload File dialog box, click Yes, and browse to the location where you want to save the Microsoft Office Word file.
3. In the Login dialog box, enter the information in the Username and Password boxes.
4. Select the site name from the Server list, and click Edit.
5. In the Modify Site Form dialog box, enter the information in the Name, Host Name, Virtual Dir. Name, Rel to Child Classes, Top Doc Class UID, Class to Class Def rels (1 per line), and QFind Interface (1 per line) boxes.
6. Click OK, and then click Login.
7. In the Document Classification window, on the Classification View tab, expand the document list to select a document class.
8. Right-click the document class to select a document type.
9. In the New Document dialog box, enter the information in the mandatory boxes, and click OK.
10. In the Allocated number confirmation dialog box, click Yes. A new document is created, and the Microsoft Office Word file is attached to it.
Drag Microsoft Office files to the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client
You can use the following steps to drag Microsoft Office Word files or Microsoft Office Outlook e-mails onto documents:
1. Drag the Microsoft Office Word file onto a document.
2. In the New Relationship dialog box, click OK.
 You can also drag Microsoft Outlook emails onto existing documents the same way.
Workflows
A workflow is a set of configured steps to manage the lifecycle of a document or object. Each step defines an action that a user or the software must complete. When a workflow step requires user, that step is presented in the user's To Do
List where the appropriate commands are presented on the shortcut menu. The appearance of the action in the user's To Do List clearly shows if the step is overdue.
SPO/E Core has a variety of workflows to model and manage various business procedures.
Risk reducing measure workflow
The Risk Reducing Measure enables registration, evaluation, and review of risks in addition to risk reducing measures. Information regarding hazardous event and any proposed mitigation measures can be attached to the
SPXRRMRiskReducingMeasureWorkflow to ensure that a consistent review and approval process is followed, and provides documented evidence for auditable traceability.
 SPXRRMRiskReducingMeasureWorkflow can be configured to add or remove workflow steps.
Create a Risk Reducing Measure (RRM)
RRM captures information about potential hazardous incidents, risk reducing measures, and the tags affected due to hazardous incidents. For example, a hazard analysis determines that the risk of pipeline damage to Pump-1 in a plant
can be reduced by installing an additional pump in parallel to Pump-1. You can create an RRM to capture this information and attach it to the SPXRRMRiskReducingMeasureWorkflow workflow for review, approval, and implementation.
1. Click File > New > Risk Reducing Measure.
2. In the New Risk Reducing Measure dialog box, in the Name and Description section, type the name and description of the risk reducing measure in the Name and Description boxes.
3. In the Risk Reducing Measure section, select a value from the Contractor From, Contractor To, and Discipline lists.
4. Enter the information for the other optional boxes.
5. In the Tags section, click Find. All the consolidated tags are displayed in the Risk Reduction Tags box.
6. Select the check box in the Risk Reduction Tags box to attach the selected tag to the risk reducing measure.
7. Click Finish.
Attach workflow to RRM (for Originators)
After the RRM is created, the Originator attaches the RRM to the SPXRRMRiskReducingMeasureWorkflow workflow, and sends it for review and approval. As an Originator, use the following steps to attach workflow to the RRM:
1. In the New Items window, right-click the RRM, and click Workflow > Attach Workflow.
2. In the Attach and Edit Workflow dialog box, select the SPXRRMRiskReducingMeasureWorkflow, and click Attach. The Initiate step appears in your To Do List.
3. Right-click the Initiate step, and click Action > Completed.
 To view the workflow, select the SPXRRMRiskReducingMeasureWorkflow workflow, and click View.
Evaluate RRM (for RRM Coordinator)
The Evaluate step appears in the To Do List for the RRM Coordinator assigned to this step in the workflow.
As a RRM Coordinator, you need to evaluate the RRM. In the example from Create a Risk Reducing Measure (RRM), you need to evaluate whether or not installing an additional pump parallel to Pump-1 is an effective RRM for controlling
the excess volume flow.
When your evaluation is complete, you must right-click the Evaluate step in your To Do List, and click Action > Completed, and then type any comments for the step or messages to the recipients of the next step.
The Evaluate step is then removed from your To Do List.
Review RRM (for RRM Reviewer)
The Review step appears in the To Do List for the RRM Reviewer assigned to this step in the workflow.
As a RRM Reviewer, you need to review the work done in the previous step.
When your review is complete, you must right-click the Review step in your To Do List, and click Action > Completed, and then type any comments for the step or messages to the recipients of the next step.
The Review step is then removed from your To Do List.
Consolidate review comments (for RRM Coordinator)
The Consolidate Comments step appears in the To Do List for the RRM Coordinator assigned to this step in the workflow.
As a RRM Coordinator, you need to consolidate all the comments given by the RRM Reviewer.
When this step is complete, you must right-click the Consolidate Comments step in your To Do List, and click Action > Completed, and then type any comments for the step or messages to the recipients of the next step.
The Consolidate Comments step is then removed from your To Do List.
Recommend RRM (for Engineering Manager)
The Recommend step appears in the To Do List for the Engineering Manager assigned to this step in the workflow.
As an Engineering Manager, you need to give your recommendations for the RRM. In this example, you can recommend installing two additional pumps parallel to Pump-1 instead of one additional pump.
When this step is complete, you must right-click the Recommend step in your To Do List, and click Action > Completed, and then type any comments for the step or messages to the recipients of the next step.
The Recommend step is then removed from your To Do List.
Approve RRM (for Project Manager)
The Approve step appears in the To Do List for the Project Manager assigned to this step in the workflow.
As a Project Manager, you need to approve the RRM after viewing the details.
When this step is complete, you must right-click the Approve step in your To Do List, and click Action > Completed, and then type any messages for the step or messages to the recipients of the next step.
The Approve step is then removed from your To Do List.
Confirm RRM implementation (for RRM Coordinator)
The ConfirmImplementedClosed step appears in the To Do List for the RRM Coordinator assigned to this step in the workflow.
As a RRM Coordinator, you need to confirm the implementation of the RRM.
When this step is complete, you must right-click the step in your To Do List, and click Action > Completed, and then type any comments for the step or messages to the recipients of the next step.
The ConfirmImplementedClosed step is then removed from your To Do List.
The users assigned to the Evaluate, Recommend, and Approve steps can reject or approve the work done in the previous step. They can also reassign the step to another user or role. To reject the previous step, right-click the step
in the To Do List, and click Action > Reject, and then type any comments for the step or messages to the recipient.
To reassign a step to another user or role, click Action > Reassign.
The users assigned to Review, Consolidate Comments, and ConfirmImplementedClosed steps can only reassign the step to another user or role. They cannot reject the step.
If an Information step appears in a user's To Do List, the user is required to acknowledge this step by right-clicking the step, and clicking Action > Completed.
Action Item and Issue Tracking workflow
An action item is a task assigned to a person for resolution, which needs to be completed by a defined deadline.
An issue is a point or matter that's in question or in dispute.
You can use the Action Item Tracking workflow to get a clear overview of the statuses for all open action items and unresolved issues. It ensures that a consistent tracking, verification, and approval process is followed for task completion,
and provides documented evidence for auditable traceability.
 The Action Item Tracking workflow can be configured to add or remove the workflow steps and assignees.
Create a task (for Originators)
As an Originator, you can create an action item and tracking task, and attach relevant documents and tags. This action item and tracking task is associated to the Action Tracking workflow by default. A template workflow helps in tracking
open action items, ensures that a consistent tracking, verification, and approval process is followed for task completion, and provides documented evidence for auditable traceability.
For example, you can create an action item and tracking task to track the progress of construction activities in a plant.
1. Click File > New > Action Item and Issue Tracking > Task.
2. In the New Action Item and Tracking Task dialog box, in the Main details section, enter the name in the Name box.
3. In the Details section, select values from the Category and Priority lists.
4. Enter the information in the Requested By box.
5. In the Status section, enter the date in the Due Date box.
6. In the Relations section, select an organization by clicking Find, and then select the check box in the Organization box.
7. To select an assignee, click Find, and then select a value from the Assignee list. Similarly select a value from the Verifier list.
8. Click Finish. A new action item and tracking task is created and displayed in the New Items window.
9. The TaskCreated step appears in your To Do List.
10. Right-click the TaskCreated step, and click Action > Completed.
Perform the task (for Assignee)
The Perform Task step appears in the To Do List for the Assignee assigned to this step in the workflow.
As an Assignee, you need to first either accept the task or reject the task. If you reject the task, the task goes back to the Originator for re-allocation. To reject the task, right-click the Perform Task, and click Action > Reject, and then type
any comments for the step.
If you accept the task, you need to work on the task, and update the task's status. You can also attach files as documented evidence for task completion.
After you have completed the task, right-click the Perform Task step in your To Do List, and click Action > Completed, and then type any comments for the step or messages to the recipients of the next step.
The Perform Task step is then removed from your To Do List.
Verify the task (for Verifier)
The Verify Task step appears in the To Do List for the Verifier assigned to this step in the workflow.
As a Verifier, you need to either approve the task or reject the task. If you reject the task, it goes back to the Assignee to incorporate the suggested changes.
To reject, right-click the Verify Task step in your To Do List, then click Action > Reject, and then type any comments for the step.
The Verify Task step is then removed from your To Do List.
Reports
The reporting functionality allows you to create and run custom reports to retrieve information from the database. SPO/E Core is delivered with a number of basic, standard reports, which can be modified to include additional information.
You can also create reports of your own.
The following types of reporting are available in SPO/E Core:
Ad-hoc Reports - Show plant information. The format, or layout, of a report is fully configurable. You can save these layout definitions as custom reports that you can reuse.
Business Intelligence Reports - Summarize statistical information from your database. The information can be displayed as charts, and from those charts you can access detailed information.
Ad-hoc reports
Ad-hoc reports provide a fast way to view plant information. The format, or layout, of a report is fully configurable. You can save these layout definitions as custom reports that you can reuse.
As part of creating and running a report, you select an existing report template. The items displayed are based on the report template you select. The report template, which is configurable, includes a view definition and a Microsoft Excel
file. The view definition determines what objects, relationships, and properties are to be included in the report. The Microsoft Excel file specifies the layout of the report.
Create a new report
1. Click File > New > Report.
2. In the New Report dialog box, select a report type from the list, and click OK.
3. In the Edit Report Definition dialog box, select the check boxes for the properties that you want to include in the report.
4. Select the type of report output style required from the Report output style list.
5. Set the sort and search options for each property, as necessary.
6. For a quick view of the report, click Apply. You can view the report and return to the dialog box to make changes.
7. To save the report definition, click Save.
8. Specify details for the saved report, and click OK.
9. Click OK to run the report.
View an existing report
1. Click Find > Reports.
2. In the Find Reports dialog box, type the report name or type "*", and click OK.
 You can also click Query > Reports.
3. In the List View, right-click the report you want to view, and click Edit Report Definition.
4. In the Edit Report Definition dialog box, select the check boxes for the properties that you want to include in the report.
5. Select the type of report output style required from the Report output style list.
6. Set the sort and search options for each property, as necessary.
7. Click Apply to view the report.
Run an existing report
The Run Report command allows you to run an existing report and view the results. This command is available when you right-click a report.
1. Click Find > Reports.
2. In the Find Reports dialog box, type the report name or type "*", and click OK.
3. In the List View, right-click the selected report, and click Run Report. Depending on the type of report, and how the report is configured, the result is displayed. For example, you can run the SPO Document Revision Report to view
all revisions of a related unpublished document. To view the report, right-click the document, and click Document > SPO Document Revision Report. The document revision report for all revisions of the selected unpublished
document is displayed in the XHTML format. To view a report of all revisions of a related published document, right-click the published document, and click Document > Document Revision Report.
 The items displayed are based on the report template that you selected. The report template includes a view definition and a Microsoft Excel file. The view definition determines what objects, relationships, and properties are to be
included in the report. The Microsoft Excel file specifies the layout of the report. You can configure report template as per your requirements.
Define filter criteria for a report
When creating or editing an Adhoc report, you can filter the results to only objects with property values that match defined criteria. You can specify criteria on any number of properties.
1. In the Edit Report Definition dialog box, find a property for which you want to define the criteria to be used to filter the report results.
2. In the Criteria column, select Click Me in the row that corresponds with the property to open the Criteria Definition dialog box.
3. In the Criteria Definition dialog box, use the Operator and Value boxes to define the criteria that must be met by an object before it can be returned by the report.
4. Click Add Criterion to list the criterion in the Currently defined criteria box.
5. If necessary, click either And or Or, and define additional criteria that must be met.
 You can remove or modify added criteria by selecting them in the Currently defined criteria window and clicking Update Criterion, Delete All Criterion, or Update Selected Criterion.
6. Click OK to return to the Edit Report Definition dialog box where you can further configure the results of the report. The Criteria column displays the criteria you defined in the appropriate row.
You can define filter criteria for multiple properties on the same report.
You can define filter criteria for a property when defining the report, or you can use the Prompt option to allow the user to provide criteria when they run the report.
Terminate a report
1. Click Query > Reports.
 You can also click Find > Reports.
2. Right-click the report that you want to terminate and select Terminate.
3. Confirm that you want to terminate the report.
SPX ACAD Title Block workflow
You can use the Drawing Review and Signoff (Title Block Update) workflow to update the title block of a drawing file.
 DWGdirectX 3.2 software must be installed on the application server for editing and updating the drawing files. For further assistance, contact your administrator.
Update title block
1. Click Find > Documents > Engineering documents to find the document which has an attached drawing file for which you want to update the title block.
2. Right-click the engineering document, and then click File >View > Native. The title block of the attached drawing file is displayed.
3. Right-click the engineering document, then click Workflow > Attach Workflow.
4. In the Attach and Edit Workflow dialog box, select Drawing Review and Signoff (Title Block Update) workflow, and click Attach. The different steps of the workflow are assigned to different users for update, review, and sign-off of
the attached drawing file.
5. After all the workflow steps are completed, right-click the document, and click File > View > Native. The information in title block is updated as per the changes made to the drawing file.
SPX Propagate Properties
Property propagation refers to copying the value of a property from one object to another. You can use the SPX Propagate Properties workflow process step to propagate properties from the workflow object to its related or non-related
objects, and vice-versa.
 The workflow step class needs to be configured for Property Propagation. For more information on configuring property propagation, see Workflow Process steps in the Technical Documentation for SPO/E Core.
Propagate properties from source object to target object
1. Right-click the workflow step class, then click Property Propagation > Manage Property Propagation.
2. In the Manage Property Propagation dialog box, click Update.
3. In the Update Property Propagation Object dialog box, type the information in the Propagation Target box.
4. Type the information in the Propagation Source box.
5. Click Finish.
The following syntaxes can be used for both source and target properties:
OBJ.MyPropertyName (Represents the workflow object).
+MyRelationshipName.MyPropertyName (Represents a related object. The characters + or - represent the direction of property propagation. Both + (from object 1 to object 2) and - (from object 2 to object 1) directions can be
specified for property propagation.
REL12_MyRelationshipName.MyPropertyName (Represents a related object; REL 21 direction can also be specified).
PREVIOUS.PropertyName (Represents a property on the previous step.).
PREVIOUSUSER.PropertyName (Represents a property on the previous step’s workflow user.).
UID_<UIDofObject>.PropertyName (Properties can be propagated by specifying the UID of the objects.).
Name_<NameofObject>.PropertyName (Properties can be propagated by specifying the names of the objects.).
If the target object is related to two or more objects by the same RelDef, the property is propagated to all these related objects.
If the source object is related to two or more objects by the same RelDef, the property is propagated from any one object only.
SPX Propagate Recipients
You can use the SPX Propagate Recipients workflow process step to define a relationship between the workflow object and a login user. This way, you can assign objects to a specific user rather than assigning them to an entire role. This
feature is implemented on a Workflow Step Definition Class.
 The workflow step class needs to be configured for recipient propagation. For more information on configuring a workflow step class for recipient propagation, see Workflow Process steps in the Technical Documentation for
SPO/E Core.
Assign a recipient to a workflow object
1. Right-click the workflow step class, then click Recipient Propagation > Manage Recipient Propagation(s).
2. In the Manage Recipient Propagation(s) dialog box, click Create.
3. In the Item dialog box, in the Main details section, type the name of the recipient in the Name box.
4. In the Recipient Propagation Details section, type the RelDef in the Recipient Propagation Source box.
The RelDef must be defined between the workflow object and a login user. For example, REL12_SPXAITHasAssignee, defines a relationship between an AIT task and a SmartPlant Foundation login user.
The direction of the RelDef is specified as either REL12_, REL21_, +, or – followed immediately by the RelDef object.
5. Select a step definition from the Step Def list.
6. Click Finish. When the workflow step configured for recipient propagation is executed, the next workflow step is assigned to the recipient configured on this workflow step.
A reassignment of a task in the workflow object does not update the corresponding relationship affected by the Recipient Propagation.
It only propagates users, not roles.
Multiple propagations cannot occur off of the same workflow step. The StepDef class only takes up to one Recipient Propagation object.
Each Recipient Propagation object can have only one relationship to one login user.
Business Intelligence Reports
SmartPlant Business Intelligence is delivered and installed with SmartPlant Foundation and provides a convenient way to easily produce summary statistical information from your database. SmartPlant Foundation information can be
displayed as graphic chart reports, which you can drill down for further details. SmartPlant Business Intelligence is powered by the same viewing technology as the existing Desktop Client ad-hoc reports.
Open Business Intelligence
Click the Business Intelligence panel in SmartPlant Dashboard to start Business Intelligence.
You can select tool commands from the main toolbar to perform various actions, such as creating a new chart report or editing existing chart reports.
More than one chart report can be displayed in the graphics results pane. You can alternate between reports by clicking on each tab.
You can view, resize, print, and export the chart report in the graphics results pane, as well as drill down to see more detailed information on the properties displayed in the chart report.
Click Minimize 
 to reduce the Reports pane to a side tab. This lets the displayed chart report result fill the application window. Click the tab to expand the reports pane into the main application window.
For more information about Business Intelligence, see SmartPlant Business Intelligence.
Chart reports
The following chart reports are supplied by default in the Business Intelligence module of the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client:
Chart Name
Description
Authoring Tags by Tag Class
Displays the number of authoring tags available for each tag class.
CDW Tags per unit
Displays the number of consolidated tags available for each unit.
OutgoingXmtls by ReceivingOrg
Displays the number of outgoing transmittals for each of the receiving parties.
OutgoingXmtls not Acknowledged
Displays the number of unacknowledged outgoing transmittals for each of the receiving parties.
Transmittals by Issue Status
Displays the number of transmittals for each issue status. The valid issue statuses are Reserved, Issued, and
Completed.
Xmtls Overdue by ReceivingOrg
Displays the number of transmittals that are overdue for each of the receiving parties.
 By default, the reports are displayed as bar charts. You can view the reports in other formats, such as pie charts, line graphs, and horizontal bar charts.
View and Manage SPO/E Core Data Using the SmartPlant Foundation Web Client
 To view and manage data in the Web Client, you must first configure SmartPlant Foundation for SPO/E Core and then log on to the Web Client as a SPOWebViewer or SPOWebEditor with default password 1. To create
SPOWebViewer or SPOWebEditor roles, load the USERS_SPFWebClient_SPO.xmlldr using the Loader in the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client. The .xmldr is located at Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com>
. For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
When you log on to the SmartPlant Foundation Web Client as a SPOWebViewer you can:
View documents and related info maps, PDFs, and drawing files.
View tags, assets, and models.
View related info maps for tags, assets, and models.
View business object relationships.
When you log on to the SmartPlant Foundation Web Client as a SPOWebEditor you can:
View, create, edit, update, and delete documents, tags, assets, models, and workflows.
View documents and related info maps, PDFs, and drawing files.
View related info maps for tags, assets, and models.
View business object relationships.
Create, edit, update, or delete business object relationships.
You can use Advanced Search 
 in the SmartPlant Foundation Web Client to search for specific data.
For more information on how to navigate to the SmartPlant Foundation Web Client and performing relevant tasks, see SmartPlant Foundation Web Client Help.
Configuring SPO/E Core
This section contains information that was previously available in the SPO/E Core How To guides.
Configuring SPO/E in a CDW Environment
Mapping relations used in CDW
ClassDefToClassDef
This is a RelDef that maps a published ClassDef to a CDW ClassDef.
Each SPO/E Core published class must be mapped with a CDW class, as shown in the following figure:
InterfaceDefToInterfaceDef
This is a RelDef that maps a published InterfaceDef to a CDW InterfaceDef.
Each SPO/E Core published interface must be mapped with a CDW interface, as shown in the following figure:
PropertyDefToPropertyDef
This is a RelDef that maps a published PropertyDef to a CDWPropertyDef.
Each SPO/E Core published property must be mapped to a CDW property, as shown in the following figure:
RelDefToRelDef
This is a RelDef that maps a published RelDef to a CDW RelDef.
Each SPO/E Core published relation must be mapped to a CDW relation, as shown in the following figure:
SPFAuthoringRelDefToCDWRelDef
This is a RelDef that maps a RelDef in the Authoring domain to a RelDef in the CDW domain.
Each relation that includes a SmartPlant Foundation authoring object must be mapped to a relation in the CDW domain, as shown in the following figure:
 This RelDef does not exist in the CDW domain, but has a relation to the authoring object for that authoring RelDef.
WPTagPublish method and Rels to go into CDW
The WPTagPublishMethod can publish relations associated to the tags in a common rel file.
Any relation can be published to CDW if:
The corresponding RelDef is defined.
The RelDef is updated in the WPTagPublishMethod and ToolMapSchema file.
In SPO/E Core, the relations associated to the tags highlighted in red in the following figure can be published into CDW. These tags are displayed in a common rel file.
 For more information on WPTagPublish client API, see WPTagPublish in the Technical Documentation for SPO/E Core.
To publish these tags into CDW, you must add mappings to the tags in the ToolMapSchema_CTAVersion adapter document. This document contains the mappings to publish the relations into CDW.
In the following illustration, the rels associated to area, unit, and plant tags are published in a common rel file attached to the CDW domain. For example, the area tag, when published into CDW, appears as SPXCDWAreaTag in the rel file.
Similarly, the other tags, such as plant, unit, and so on, are displayed in the same format.
SPFMapFunctionDef and MappingToSPFMapFunction
The SPFMapFunctionDef ClassDef is a definition of a map function that can be called during the consolidation process. The only instance of the SPFMapFunctionDef ClassDef in the configuration is SPFClassificationFromTyping. This map
function converts the typing enumerations used in SmartPlant Integration to the classification relations used in SmartPlant Foundation.
The MappingToSPFMapFunction RelDef is a relation between any of the above mappings and a map function. The end 1 interface is IMapRel, allowing ClassDef, InterfaceDef, PropertyDef, and RelDef mappings to have map function.
However, all instances of this relation in the configuration provided are PropertyDef mappings.
This map function also adds CDW tags with relation to the classification tag type in the Tag types tree, as shown in the following figure:
PrimarySPFMethodDef, SecondarySPFMethodDef, Performs, and SecondaryMethodDefs
The Primary/SecondarySPFMethodDef ClassDefs are a description of methods implemented on interfaces in the SmartPlant Foundation server. The Performs RelDef maps a PrimarySPFMethodDef to the InterfaceDef on which it is
implemented. The SecondaryMethodDefs RelDef switches between a primary and secondary reldef.
Domains Architecture
The following figure describes the CDW mappings:
Legend
Red - Mapping between the SPO/E domain and the CDW domain. The XML map files must be loaded using the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client's Loader feature. The CDW objects are automatically created in the consolidation
task.
Blue - Relation.
Integration Environment – CDW View
You can publish properties from the following tools to CDW:
Smart Electrical
Smart P&ID
PDS
SmartPlant Isometrics
Smart Instrumentation
The main improvements of publishing from the tools is as follows:
The ability to determine which property value goes into the CDW domain, even though the same property is published from different tools.
The ability to determine how the properties are displayed in SmartPlant Foundation using ViewDefs.
To understand the publish process and limitations before using CDW, the following figure describes the two scenarios:
Blue (Scenario1) - Publish properties from different tools into the shared domain. These properties are published with separate mapping, values, and UIDs. The properties having the same meaning are separately published to the
DWshared domain.
Red (Scenario2) - Publish properties from different tools into the similar properties of the shared domain. This indicates a "Last in wins" concept, where the last tool that published a property gets the value in the DWShared domain.
To use a different method for publishing and retrieving, the CDW uses mapping files as PropertyDefToPropertyDef to determine which tool is the owner of the property in the CDW domain.
The following illustration describes the publish and retrieve operation in CDW.
A property can be published from more than one tool, enabling CDW to determine the owner of that property. The PropertyDefToPropertyDef mapping can be loaded with the appropriate mapping, and the consolidated task takes place only
for the owner tool.
The result is that when a shared property is published from more than one tool, the property is updated into the CDW domain only if the property to which the tool belongs has published it.
 ViewDefs define how the Property grids look and which properties are displayed in SmartPlant Foundation.
SPX CDW Delivered Files
To get the required CDW functionality, SPO/E delivers the CDW schema, administration, and data mapping files.
 SCHEMA_SPO for CDW spreadsheet contains a sample spreadsheet to edit CDW Classes, Interfaces, and Reldefs.
The following Reldefs are used by the Adapter and published into the CommonRels file:
SPXCDWUnitTag
SPXCDWAreaTag
SPXCDWPlantTag
SPXCDWSystemTag
SPXCDWTagDocument
SPXCDWTagAssembly
SPXCDWTagConnection
SPXCDWProjectTag
SPXCDWTagModel
SPXCDWTagAsset
Each RelDef that needs to be published into SPO/E must be defined in the WPTagPublishMethod method. Argument 7 (Rels to go into CDW) contains all the required published relations. Any custom RelDef that has to be published must
be defined here:
ADMIN_SPO for CDW spreadsheet contains a sample spreadsheet for CDW configurations, such as Methods, Menus, Access Groups, and so forth.
You can configure the relationship definition from the Rels to go into CDW field to view the corresponding relationship definition of SPX tag on the CDW tag.
You can now create self tag relationships only by using loader.
DATA_CDWMapping
