This allows you to consistently manage your data, as well as collaborate with specialists, contractors, and suppliers in managing your plant design, configuration, and engineering documentation.
 The Web Client Server can be configured to handle static server data, such as images.
Web Client Interoperability
The interoperability of the SmartPlant Foundation Web Client and HxGN SDx module Web Clients with other applications helps bridge gaps in functionality and provides integration with the latest technologies. Extensibility allows you to
add custom views from applications that can be displayed in an iframe inside the Web Client, and Embedded mode provides the ability to embed the Web Client into third-party applications.
Extensibility
Extensibility for the Web Client provides integration with any web development paradigm using the latest technologies. The Web Extensibility API can be used in the Web Client to help bridge gaps in functionality, such as displaying
external applications in the Web Client through a URL connection in order to share data. The Extensibility framework provides:
The capability to embed external web applications and integrate the data from those applications into the Web Client experience.
An emphasis on the user and developer experience.
An introduction to Web Development Best Practice.
A clean, modern API.
For more information on using extensibility for the Web Client, see Web Client Extensibility.
Extensibility samples, with a full set of services for interacting with the Web Extensibility API and SmartPlant Foundation Web APIs, are available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more
information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
Embedded mode
Embedded mode is an event-driven communication mechanism that allows the Web Client to send and receive certain events when the Web Client is embedded in another parent application. In Embedded mode, the Web Client is placed
in an iframe of the parent application. Communication events can be securely sent between the Web Client and the parent application using a wrapper library that is based on the HTML5 postMessage API.
For more information on using embedded mode in the Web Client, see Embedded mode.
Embedded samples are available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
Integration Architecture
SmartPlant components interact with the standard SmartPlant Foundation client and server components to make integration possible. The following SmartPlant components are part of integration:
Authoring Tool Components
SmartPlant Adapters
Common User Interface
SmartPlant Client
Schema Component
The following illustration provides an overview of the integrated architecture of the SmartPlant Foundation components and tool authoring software when publishing.
Authoring tool components
The publish and retrieve mapping between the tool database and the SmartPlant schema is defined in the tool map schema file. Basic mapping is delivered with each authoring tool.
You can modify this mapping using the Schema Editor, which is optionally installed with the SmartPlant Schema Component. The Schema Editor also allows the SmartPlant Foundation administrator to edit the plant breakdown structure
(PBS) in the SmartPlant schema. Authoring tool components include:
Tool application
Tool database
Adapter
Tool map schema
SmartPlant adapters
Each authoring tool that is part of SmartPlant Enterprise has its own authoring tool SmartPlant adapter. Each adapter facilitates the sharing of data between the authoring tool and SmartPlant Foundation. The adapters generate XML files
for publish operations and consume XML when tools retrieve documents.
The following list shows the SmartPlant authoring tools that have an adapter that can publish to SmartPlant Foundation:
PDS®
Intergraph Smart® 3D
Intergraph Smart Construction
Intergraph Smart Electrical
SmartPlant® Foundation
Intergraph Smart Instrumentation
SmartPlant Isometrics
Intergraph Smart P&ID
SmartPlant Spoolgen®
SmartSketch®
Intergraph Smart Interop Publisher
 SmartPlant Foundation is included in the list because it has several of its own authoring tools, such as Line List and Plant Breakdown Structure that can publish to a SmartPlant Foundation data warehouse. Smart Construction is
included as it participates as an authoring tool but does not publish.
Common user interface
The SmartPlant Common user interface (UI) is an ActiveX component (.DLL) that is called by the authoring tool software. When a user in one of the authoring tools clicks one of the integration commands, the authoring tool interacts with
the SmartPlant common user interface component to connect to SmartPlant Foundation. The Common UI also interacts with the SmartPlant Client component when it requires information from SmartPlant Foundation.
SmartPlant Client
The SmartPlant Client is a layer below the Common UI on the client workstation. It accepts requests from the Common UI and communicates with the SmartPlant Foundation server. SmartPlant Client also supplies requested information to
the Common UI.
SmartPlant Schema Component
The SmartPlant Schema Component is a suite of ActiveX components that provide functionality surrounding the creation, parsing, validation, and comparison of SmartPlant schema and data. The tool adapters interact with the SmartPlant
Schema Component to read the SmartPlant schema to create data to publish as well as to consume the retrieved data.
SmartPlant Foundation components
The following list contains the SmartPlant Foundation components and describes how they are used in a publish and retrieve mapping environment:
SmartPlant Foundation database - The SmartPlant Foundation database stores document metadata for documents that are published and retrieved. Document metadata contains the name of the document, document type,
document revision and version, and information about published files, including data XML, metadata XML, tombstones XML, and view files. Additionally, other data for published documents, such as the object data published in the
data XML file by the tool, is also stored in the SmartPlant Foundation database.
SmartPlant Foundation vault - The SmartPlant Foundation vault is used to store XML files and view files published by the authoring tools.
SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client - The Desktop Client allows you to create a new plant breakdown structure (PBS) for publish and retrieve, review published data and viewable files, and compare object data across domains.
Additionally, the Desktop Client can participate in integration as an authoring tool – retrieving data published by other tools and creating, updating, and publishing data of its own.
SmartPlant Foundation Server Manager - Server Manager allows an administrator to configure the SmartPlant Foundation server sites. Server Manager provides configuration for server settings that are necessary for SmartPlant
Foundation and integration.
Intergraph Smart Licensing - SmartPlant Foundation 2019 Update 10 and later uses Intergraph Smart Licensing for license management. For more information, see Intergraph Smart Licensing Help.
Authoring tool integration commands
Each authoring tool contains the following commands for integration on the SmartPlant menu:
Register
Publish
Retrieve
Find Documents to Publish
When a user in one of the authoring tools clicks one of these SmartPlant commands, the authoring tool interacts with the SmartPlant Common UI component. Depending on the command, the authoring tool sends an appropriate message
to the Common UI component, which does the processing and displays the dialog boxes for user input.
 For the purposes of this document, this is an overview of the authoring tool process. For more detailed information, see SmartPlant Integration.
Register
The Plant Breakdown Structure (PBS) must be registered with SmartPlant Foundation so it can be used with any integrated tools. Once the PBS is registered, each authoring tool must be registered with the SmartPlant Foundation plant
before you can publish and retrieve documents. When you register an authoring tool, this allows the authoring tool to display the SmartPlant commands and registers the authoring tool with the SmartPlant Foundation plant. After the plant
is registered, you can publish and retrieve documents.
The following table displays the main interaction stages using the Register command.
Action
Result
User clicks SmartPlant > PBS > Register.
Type the node name and virtual directory of the SmartPlant Foundation database to which you want to register your
PBS.
User clicks SmartPlant > [Application Type]> Register.
Type the node name and virtual directory of the SmartPlant Foundation database to which you want to register your
authoring tool application.
The Register command maps the authoring tool's plant and its projects to the SmartPlant Foundation plant using a URL.
Each authoring tool contains the following commands for integration on the SmartPlant menu:
Register
Publish
Retrieve
Find Documents to Publish
When a user in one of the authoring tools clicks one of these SmartPlant commands, the authoring tool interacts with the SmartPlant Common UI component. Depending on the command, the authoring tool sends an appropriate message
to the Common UI component, which does the processing and displays the dialog boxes for user input.
Publish
The following table displays the main interaction stages in the action for Publish.
Action
Result
User clicks SmartPlant > Publish.
The authoring tool sends the publish command to the Common UI with the document list selected in the authoring
tool.
The Common UI displays the Publish dialog box for user input.
User clicks Engineering Tool in the Publish dialog box.
The Common UI calls the specific SmartPlant authoring tool adapter to display the specific tool user interface for
document selection.
User makes the appropriate document selection. The adapter returns selections to the Common UI.
User clicks OK in the Publish dialog box.
The Common UI retrieves data from the adapter to publish for each document and interacts with the SmartPlant
Client component.
The SmartPlant Client component sends published data to the SmartPlant Foundation server.
SmartPlant Foundation server uses the file service to put published XML files and view files in the SmartPlant
Foundation vault.
Document reaches load step in workflow.
SmartPlant Foundation loads objects in the data XML file into the SmartPlant Foundation database.
Retrieve
The following table displays the main interaction stages in the action for Retrieve.
Action
Result
User clicks SmartPlant > Retrieve.
The authoring tool sends retrieve command to the Common UI.
The Common UI displays documents that can be retrieved in the Retrieve dialog box.
User clicks OK after selecting documents to retrieve.
SmartPlant Client communicates with SmartPlant Foundation to get published XML files from the appropriate
SmartPlant Foundation vault.
SmartPlant Foundation supplies the XML file.
SmartPlant Client passes the retrieved XML file to the Common UI.
The Common UI calls the tool's SmartPlant adapter to consume the retrieved XML.
Adapter stores retrieved data in tool database.
The tool adapter generates actions that are displayed in a To Do List so that users can choose which actions to
perform on the database.
Find Documents to Publish
The Find Documents to Publish command can work in synchronous or asynchronous mode, depending on whether the authoring tool keeps track of the list of documents to publish.
If the authoring tool knows what documents to publish, it passes the list of documents to a command in the Common UI component. The Common UI displays the tool document list in a dialog box for user selections. The Common UI
communicates with the tool's SmartPlant adapter to determine if documents exist in the authoring tool. If the document exists in SmartPlant Foundation but does not exist in the authoring tool, the Common UI lists the deleted documents for
termination in SmartPlant Foundation. After the user makes the selection in the Find dialog box, the Publish dialog box is displayed with the selected documents for user input.
Licensing for SmartPlant Foundation
 As of December 31, 2024, we no longer generate license keys for SmartPlant License Manager. You must upgrade to Intergraph Smart Licensing. For more information, see Intergraph Smart Licensing Installation and Setup.
Intergraph® Smart Licensing is the next-generation software licensing product for Hexagon AB. For more information, see Intergraph Smart Licensing Help.
Smart Licensing Client is a small application that must be installed on a license server, accessable by machines running Intergraph tools.
For more information about how to setup a license server for SmartPlant Foundation, see License server setup for SmartPlant Foundation.
Setting up SmartPlant Foundation to use Intergraph Smart Licensing
Before you can use Intergraph Smart Licensing to manage your connections to SmartPlant Foundation, make sure it is installed and configured using the following steps:
1. Install the Intergraph Smart Licensing Client on any machine that will be acting as a license server. For more information, see Intergraph Smart Licensing Installation and Setup.
2. Load a configuration connection info (.cci) file with device option, if it was not loaded during installation. See Client Configurations (see ).
3. Start your licensed application.
For more information on getting started with Intergraph Smart Licensing, see Getting Started with Intergraph Smart Licensing (ISL).
Obtaining a license from Intergraph Smart Licensing
Intergraph Smart Licensing supports a number of configurations and types of licenses. For more information about configuring this environments, see Configurations, projects, and keystores.
When you log into SmartPlant Foundation, it evaluates your ability to access the following types of licenses and tries to pull:
A site seat (FST). Any user who attempts to log in to SmartPlant Foundation can pull this license seat.
A perpetual seat (FPP). Only users listed in the SPF_perpetual group in the perpet.dat file can pull a perpetual seat.
A perpetual seat allows a user in that group to have unlimited access to SmartPlant Foundation.
Perpetual licenses are tied to the username and password of the user and do not expire.
While a perpetual license can be used on any client machine in the network, it can be used on only one client machine at a time.
The perpet.dat file is stored in the license site virtual directory.
A SmartPlant Foundation single seat (FSU). Only users listed in the SPF_single group in the perpet.dat file can pull a single seat.
A Basic Integrator seat (FBI). Only users listed in the SPF_perpetual group in the perpet.dat file can pull a Basic Integrator seat.
These seats are used only by users who have Basic Integrator, rather than SmartPlant Foundation.
A daily token (FDY). Any user who tries to log in to SmartPlant Foundation can pull one of these tokens.
Daily tokens are good for 12 consecutive hours of access, starting with the initial log in, and are tied to the SmartPlant Foundation user, not the location where the license is used.
When you acquire a daily token to use SmartPlant Foundation, the quantity of the daily tokens in the keystore is reduced by one.
Perpet.dat file
This file, stored in the license site virtual directory, lists the user names of any users allowed to use a perpetual seat (FFP). The file should contain one Group SPF_perpetual line for each perpetual seat purchased, and each line should
indicate the user allowed to use that perpetual seat.
 If the number of users defined in the Group SPF_perpetual section is less than the number of perpetual seats purchased, no one will have access to those additional seats.
If you are using Basic Integrator (FBI) license, define the users who can access tokens in the Group SPF_perpetual section.
If you are using a SmartPlant Foundation single seat (FSU), define the user who can use licenses in the Group SPF_single section.
Migrating from SmartPlant License Manager to Intergraph Smart Licensing
 As of December 31, 2024, we no longer generate license keys for SmartPlant License Manager. You must upgrade to Intergraph Smart Licensing. For more information, see Intergraph Smart Licensing Installation and Setup.
While you can configure your Intergraph Smart Licensing environment to behave exactly as licensing worked with SmartPlant Foundation, Intergraph Smart Licensing is more powerful and provides options that were not previously
available, such as site- or project-based configurations.
This article describes how to migrate from SmartPlant License Manager (SPLM) to Intergraph Smart Licensing (ISL). This process can take anywhere from a day to a couple of weeks, depending on the complexity of your installation.
If you need assistance, please do not hesitate to contact us. Provide the following information:
What SPLM server are you migrating to ISL?
What is your end use customer name and site ID? (If you do not know your site ID, provide your end use site address or most recent SPLM key.)
How many users are you migrating to ISL? What products are they using, and how many licenses for each product do you need to migrate?
Prepare for the migration
1. Consider your SPLM installation and determine what customizations (if any) need to be transitioned. Examples include:
If you used location data in SPLM journal files, consider using custom fields in ISL.
If you used projects in SPLM environment variables, consider using projects as part of a project-based configuration in ISL.
2. Contact your local order administrator to request a temporary ISL key.
 You should consider requesting this key about mid-month, so you have time to do internal testing before converting to your production ISL key for the next month and reducing the licenses on your SPLM key.
3. When your local order administrator notifies you that your temporary ISL key is ready, log on to the Licensing Portal.
4. Select Configure ISL > Configurations, and find your configuration, which has the same name as your end use site ID.
 Your temporary ISL key is set up under a site-based configuration. You can change this configuration to a project-based configuration later.
5. Select the configuration, and download the CCI file. Save this file in a safe place; you send it to your users in the next section.
Migrate clients
 If you are configuring Intergraph Smart Licensing for SmartPlant Foundation, Smart Licensing Client must be installed on the application server and must be updated to the latest version. You might need to upgrade the
application server to support a later version of Windows Server®. Before updating the license server during the migration process, we recommend performing an iisreset.
All of the steps in this section take place on the client computers that are part of your Intergraph Smart Licensing installation. A license machine or server is not needed in Smart Licensing.
 You can automate client deployments to quickly install and configure Smart Licensing Client on many computers at once. For more information, see the Smart Licensing Installation Guide delivered with Smart Licensing Client.
1. Tell users to close all instances of SPLM licensed applications.
2. Tell users to ensure their computers meet the hardware and software recommendations for Smart Licensing Client.
3. Tell users to install Smart Licensing Client.
 The Smart Licensing Client installation uninstalls SmartPlant License Manager, both the SPLM client and server components, if they are present.
 If you are setting up licensing for a web-based application, tell users to perform an iisreset from the command prompt to ensure all changes are implemented.
4. Send the CCI file to all users, and instruct them to load it in Smart Licensing Client.
5. Tell users that they can open their licensed application(s).
Migrate licenses from SPLM to ISL
1. Contact your local order administrator to generate a reduced SPLM key before the temporary ISL key runs out on the last day of the month.
2. Install the reduced SPLM key and send the audit code to your local order administrator.
3. Once your local order administrator confirmed the receipt of your audit code, generate your ISL production key.
 ISL keys expire on the last day of the month at midnight UTC, unlike SPLM, which expires on the second day of the next month. So you must ensure that the ISL production key is installed prior to the beginning of the next
month.
Decommission SmartPlant License Manager server
Follow this procedure once all clients have been migrated to ISL and there are no active licenses left on the SPLM server.
1. Verify that no one is using the SmartPlant License Manager server. Use the Display Status of Reserved Licenses command in SmartPlant License Manager on the server.
2. Do one last scavenge on the server.
3. Upload the journal files, if applicable. These files are located on the server.
 Not all customers upload journal files, so this might not apply to you.
4. Uninstall SPLM on the server, and decommission the server.
 After migrating to ISL, the SPLMDepDll folder no longer exists in the Server Manager Settings nor in its location on the disk. This is expected behavior and does not require further action.
For more information about migrating your system to Intergraph Smart Licensing, see Migrate to Smart Licensing. For additional assistance, you can contact your licensing manager or the order desk, or you can file a support case with
Intergraph Smart Licensing support.
Troubleshooting SmartPlant Foundation
This section contains information about troubleshooting various portions of the Desktop Client.
Log Files
The links below provide direct access to additional information about setting up and using log files for SmartPlant Foundation and SmartPlant Markup Plus.
SmartPlant Foundation
License server logging
Setting up SmartPlant Foundation client logging
File server error log example
Maintaining log output files
Set up a batch print log file
Generate markup rendition files in batch
Trace logging for server, cache service, remote services, file service, and configuration service
SmartPlant Markup Plus
Setting up SmartPlant Markup Plus log files
SmartPlant Foundation Security
This section contains troubleshooting information about security and logging on to the SmartPlant Foundation installation.
Unable to log on to SmartPlant Foundation
You may receive one of the following errors when you try to logon to SmartPlant Foundation software components.
Error Message
The error message that appears when you try to log on contains the following text: "Client found response content type of 'text/html; charset=utf-8', but expected 'text/xml'. The request failed with an empty response."
Solution
On the server, open a web browser and enter the following address (or equivalent for your site) http://localhost/SPFServer/ServerRequest.asmx.
This action should produce an error screen. The Description should point to where the error is.
Possible Causes
Incorrect version of .NET.
You may need to reregister IIS with the Microsoft .NET Framework. To reregister IIS, use the following command:
C:\WINDOWS\Microsoft.NET\Framework\version\aspnet_regiis.exe –i
Where version is your .Net framework version.
For more information, see your IIS and .NET Framework documentation.
Error Message
The error message that appears when you try to log on contains the following text: "The RSA key container could not be opened."
Solution
If you get this error on logging in to the client, it indicates a permissions problem for the user account that is trying to access the registry key that contains the security information. The user account that is configured as the identity running
your site's application pool in IIS, needs to be able to access this security key in the registry. You can perform either of the procedures below to resolve this problem.
To give access to the local user account:
aspnet_regiis -pa "NetFrameworkConfigurationKey" Local_User_Account_Name
(Run this from C:\WINDOWS\Microsoft.NET\Framework\v4.0, and substitute your actual account name for Local_User_Account_Name above)
-Or-
Set the user account to have system access to the Registry. (Normally this is only done if you are running IIS with application pools and can set up a dedicated user account for the SmartPlant Foundation application pools).
Error Message
The HTTP 500.21-Internal Server Error message may be encountered when you try to log on or browse SmartPlant Foundation and contains the following text:
HTTP 500.21-Internal Server Error: Handler "PageHandlerFactory-Integrated" has a bad module "ManagedPipelineHandler" in its module list.
Solution
This occurs when .NET is installed before IIS or the application role has been added after the Web Server Role. To fix this issue, open a command prompt and go to the .NET directory, for example:
%windir%\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319
Open a command prompt and type the command: aspnet_regiis –iru
For more information, see your IIS and .NET Framework documentation.
Error Message
The error message appears that the system is in a Faulted State. There are many reasons for this.
Solution
Check the trace logs for more detailed error information and note the new location of the trace logs.
Error Message
The error message appears that there is an Add Error reading from the pipe.
Solution
This is a database issue as there are invalid or missing tables. Check the trace logs for more detailed information.
Cannot log on after several failed attempts
Problem
After several failed log on attempts, you cannot log on and receive the following error.
Account temporarily blocked due to the number of failed login attempts
Solution
SmartPlant Foundation can be configured to temporarily lock an account when a set number of failed login attempts is reached. However, after a set period of time, the account is automatically unlocked. This has been instated to protect
the system from malicious bulk password attacks. For more information on these system settings, contact your system administrator.
Unable to log on to SmartPlant Foundation site with Windows Authentication
Problem
After a site has been created and a local user account has been created to run as the identity for the SmartPlant Foundation application pool, trying to access the site results in an HTTP 401 error (unauthorized access error).
Solution
The SmartPlant Foundation application pool identity must be set to a domain user account, not a local user account, and this domain user account must be a member of the SPFUsers group on that server.
Cannot log on with FIPS enabled
Problem
If you have a system enabled to use Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) but the system does not have IWA enabled, when you attempt to log on to the Desktop Client, the following error message appears:
Invalid username or password
Solution
If you are using system that uses Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) without IWA enabled, you must ensure that your users passwords are created and conform to the FIPS standards.
SmartPlant Foundation Installation
This section contains troubleshooting information about the installation of SmartPlant Foundation.
Unable to publish documents after hot fix installation
After installing a hot fix package for SmartPlant Client, publishing authoring tool documents results in failure errors.
Solution
Ensure all pre-requisite hot fix packages have been applied to the SmartPlant Foundation installation on the application server. When applying hot fixes to an installation of SmartPlant Client, you must also apply any pre-requisite hot fix to
the SmartPlant Foundation installation on the application server. The SmartPlant Client readme will reference any pre-requisite SmartPlant Foundation hot fixes.
Error during uninstall of SmartPlant Foundation after removing Schema Component
Problem
During uninstall of SmartPlant Foundation, after having already uninstalled Schema Component, the following error is displayed:
Error 1905. Module C:\Program Files (x86)\SmartPlant\Foundation\COMPlus Applications\SPFSCWrapperComPlus\SPFSCWrapperComPlus\Schemacomponent.tlb failed to unregister. HRESULT -2147319780. Contact your support personnel.
Solution
When SmartPlant Foundation and Schema Component are installed together on the same machine, you must always uninstall SmartPlant Foundation before uninstalling Schema Component.
Error occurs when browsing File Service after installing SmartPlant Foundation Update 25
Problem
After installing SmartPlant Foundation Update 25, the following error occurs when trying to browse the SmartPlant Foundation File Service:
"Server Error in '/<Sitename>FileService' Application
Could not load file or assembly PDFNet or one of its dependencies. An attempt was made to load a program with an incorrect format."
Cause
This error occurs after installing SmartPlant Foundation Update 25, if the "Visual C++ 2013 x86 redistributable" package is not installed in the File service machine.
Solution
To avoid this error, we recommend installing the "Visual C++ 2013 x86 redistributable" package before installing the Update 25 of SmartPlant Foundation. For more information, see SmartPlant Foundation Application Server platform
recommendations.
Canceling uninstallation leaves an entry in Programs and Features list
Problem
If you run the uninstallation for SmartPlant Foundation and you choose to cancel the uninstall of the software before it has finished, the software is still listed in the Control Panel's Programs and Features window, and you are unable to
uninstall the program.
Solution
You must manually remove the item from the Control Panel's Programs and Features window.
1. In the Programs and Features window, right-click SmartPlant Foundation and click Change.
2. Select Add or Remove Features and click Next.
3. Select a feature (for example, the Desktop Client), and click Update.
4. In the Programs and Features window, right-click SmartPlant Foundation and click Change.
5. Select Uninstall and click Next.
6. Click Finish when the software has been removed.
 This issue may occur for any software when its installation wizard is canceled near the end of the process. If this occurs, the software can be removed using this same procedure.
Unable to load SPFDatasheetDefiner.vsto because a compatible version of .NET Framework is not installed
Problem
When you attempt to install SmartPlant Foundation Data Sheet Definer, the error “Unable to load the SPFDatasheetDefiner.vsto because a compatible version of .NET framework is not installed" may be displayed.
Solution
The Visual Studio 2010 Tools for Office Runtime may be missing from the server. This software must be installed in order to install the SmartPlant Foundation Data Sheet Definer.
 If Visual Studio 2010 Tools for Office Runtime is not installed by default, install it before you install the SmartPlant Foundation Data Sheet Definer.
Unable to view Data Validator staging schema in site settings property
Problem
After upgrading to IM 2019 or later updates, when you load the Data Validator staging schema, the database layer is not appearing in the DatabaseLayers property in the Site Settings node.
Solution
This happens if you have not loaded the Data Validator staging schema before starting the upgrade process.
 For more information, see the Knowledge Base article <https://intergraph-ppm.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka03Z000000ojnPQAQ/view> .
After applying an Update you receive runtime errors using the Web Client
Problem
After performing the upgrade process to install a new Update, when you try to access your site using the Web Client, you receive runtime errors in the browser.
Solution
Under some isolated conditions, the browser's cache may need to be manually cleared to remove references to files that have been updated. If you are experiencing this problem with the Web Client, try clearing the browser's cache on the
device or workstation.
 For information about clearing your browser's cache, refer to your browser's documentation.
Database Connections
This section contains troubleshooting information about database connections.
You may encounter errors due to the SmartPlant Foundation application server not connecting to the SmartPlant Foundation database.
Unable to connect to Oracle database after setting up new Windows Server
This solution applies to Windows Server installations where the Oracle 64-bit client has been installed on the application server.
Problem
After installing the 64-bit Oracle client software on a Windows Server application server, you receive an Oracle error, "Unable to connect to the database" on trying to test your connection.
Solution
After installing the 64-bit Oracle client on Windows Server, the server must be rebooted.
Unable to connect to the database server from the Desktop Client
When you try to connect to the Desktop Client, you may see the following error message immediately after you click OK on the Login Information dialog box. This may be either a faulted state or reading from the pipe error.
The remote server returned an error: (500) Internal Server Error.
To see more details about the error, click Show Html Error.
Solution
If the error indicates that the Oracle Client cannot be found, do the following:
1. On the application server, browse to the directory where Oracle is installed.
2. Right-click the Oracle directory and then click Properties.
3. Click the Security tab.
4. Add the SPF_Server application pool identity user to the list of users for the directory and give it read access.
5. Click Advanced.
6. Select Replace permission entries on all child object with entries shown here that apply to child objects.
7. Click OK.
8. Click OK.
Unable to connect to an Oracle database
The following are common Oracle database connection errors; many of these are caused by typographical errors during initial installation or a problem in the Oracle connection. For more information on Oracle errors, please see the Oracle
documentation.
Error Messages
ORA-12154: "TNS:could not resolve service name"
ORA-12198: "TNS:could not find path to destination"
ORA-12203: "TNS:unable to connect destination"
ORA-12224: "TNS:no listener"
ORA-12571: "TNS:packet writer failure"
Solutions
You can do the following to try to resolve these errors:
1. Check that you can connect to the database server and the data database using the following Oracle utilities:
tnsping <service name>
sqlplus =<data database user>/<password>@<service name>
 Replace the strings in italics with the appropriate information for your database.
2. Use the Oracle Net Configuration Assistant utility to check and test the <service name>. For more information, see the Oracle documentation.
3. Recycle your Internet Information Services (IIS) application pools on the SmartPlant Foundation application server. If the Oracle server has been recently rebooted, or if the instance used by SmartPlant Foundation has been
restarted, IIS connections to the instance need to be reset.
Unable to load file or assembly Oracle.DataAccess
Error Message
Could not load file or assembly Oracle.DataAccess.
Problem
The Oracle.DataAccess dll file is not registered in the Global Assembly Cache (GAC) or the default version is different from the installed version.
The default assembly version of the DLL in the SPFAppServer.config file is different from the Oracle version installed on the SmartPlant Foundation server.
Solution
1. Register the Oracle.DataAccess dll file by moving the file from <Oracle_install_directory>\product\<Oracle_version>\client_1\ODP.NET\bin\2.x to C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\assembly.
Or
2. Update the Oracle.DataAccess version in the SPFAppServer.config file of the SmartPlant Foundation site with the version available in the C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\assembly folder.
Timeout errors on SQL server
Error Message
Timeout expired. The timeout period elapsed prior to completion of the operation, or the server is not responding.
Problem
Timeout errors occur on a SQL Server site when loading published documents and executing queries.
Solution
Run the following SQL on your database. Replace <Databasename> with the name of your database.
ALTER DATABASE <Databasename> SET READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT ON.
If the errors persist, then increase the value for the DBCommandTimeoutSeconds setting for your site. This property is available in SmartPlant Foundation Server Manager, on the Settings node under each site node.
 This issue is specific to SQL Server.
Oracle error 1502 occurs connecting to the database
When connecting to the database, the following error is displayed, indicating an index for the database is unusable:
ORA-01502: index 'XXXX_Data.PK_SCHEMAOBJDETAIL' or partition of such index is in unusable state
Solution
Perform the following procedure to make the index usable:
1. Run IIS reset on the selected site.
2. Log in to the site database and run the following SQL commands.
 Each command should be executed one at a time, rather than in a batch.
select status from user_indexes where index_name ='PK_PUBOBJPRDETAIL'; (the status should be unusable)
alter table "PUBOBJPRDETAIL" drop constraint "PK_PUBOBJPRDETAIL";
drop index PK_PUBOBJPRDETAIL;
alter table "PUBOBJPRDETAIL" add constraint "PK_PUBOBJPRDETAIL" primary key ("OBJPROBID");
select status from user_indexes where index_name = 'PK_PUBOBJPRDETAIL'; (the status should be usable)
Oracle error 942 during database dump file import
Problem
When importing a database dump file that was created on Oracle 10g R2 to a database running Oracle 11g R2, the following error may occur:
--IMP-00058: ORACLE error 942 encountered
--ORA-00942: table or view does not exist .. rows imported
This error is observed in the Server Manager logs <ServerManager Root Path>Temp\ServerManager or when performing the import manually.
Solution
This error can be ignored. This is a known Oracle issue. The data is imported but may end up in the wrong tablespace. There is a workaround, in that you must make sure you create the prdetails tables before import.
Oracle error 3114 occurs when connecting to a site
Problem
Oracle error 3114 (not connected to Oracle) occurs while attempting to connect to a SmartPlant Foundation site. This error can occur in isolation or in succession during several seemingly unrelated operations, such as while logging in to
the Desktop Client or during a scheduled operation.
Solution
This indicates a potential performance issue with the Oracle database. Performance analysis should be undertaken by a qualified Oracle engineer. If it was due to an Oracle server restart, you must recycle the application pools for all sites
that connect to the database.
Unable to update site after upgrade as path access denied
Error Message
When updating a site in Server Manager after upgrading SmartPlant Foundation, the following error message displays:
Unable to update the site due to: Access to the path sqlceme40.dll is denied
Problem
This problem is because the SmartPlant Foundation configuration service application pools are still running, which blocks access to the relevant file to update the site.
Solution
You must stop the application pools using Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager before updating any sites. Ensure the application pools are restarted after the update.
 Alternatively, run an IISReset before any upgrade.
Multi-byte character set data exceeds field size limit
Problem
When using a multi-byte character set (such as Chinese, Japanese, Russian, etc.) against an Oracle database, creating an object results in Oracle error ORA-12899: value too large for column. The data cannot be saved because the size
limit of the property has been exceeded.
Solution
If your implementation uses multi-byte languages, such as Chinese, Japanese and Russian, and you have upgraded to the current version of SmartPlant Foundation from version 2009 R3 SP3 or earlier, you will need to run a script to
update the definition of certain columns in the database in order to accommodate the full length of some properties.
Configuration Service and Cache Service
The existing SmartPlant Foundation Server architecture allows the SmartPlant Foundation server application pool to leverage multiple worker processes for concurrent tasks and significantly improve the scalability of SmartPlant
Foundation.
Two services, Cache Service and Configuration Service, have been developed to support the SmartPlant Foundation server.
The Cache Service moves the SmartPlant Foundation In Process application cache to an Out of Process application cache. By moving the caching mechanism outside of the SmartPlant Foundation server, the system can better
use the multiple worker processes feature available on the application pool. The server communicates with the cache service to get any information about the current configuration, such as schema and administration data.
The Configuration Service provides a centrally located place for all of the installation and configuration settings for SmartPlant Foundation. To take the burden of polling away from the application server, the configuration service
now has prime responsibility for polling to check if the configured schedulers need to start processing. The poller determines which SmartPlant Foundation site needs to service the scheduler request and sends a request to that site
to start polling for tasks.
The maximum number of worker processes that can be configured for the cache and configuration services application pools is limited to 1. Multiple worker processes are not supported for these application pools.
Due to the introduction of this cache service, the previous cache synchronization mechanism, using a distributed cache, is not supported.
To achieve the architecture changes, some fundamental changes have been made to some well-known and frequently used APIs; some of these have been removed. For more information and a full list of the class and API
changes, see the SmartPlant Foundation Concurrency Changes document, which is delivered with the developer's toolkit. This document is only intended for users who have done SmartPlant Foundation server side
development or customization.
Configuration service
The communication object System.ServiceModel.Channels.ServiceChannel cannot be used
DefaultSPFServerURL
Error message
The communication object, System.ServiceModel.Channels.ServiceChannel, cannot be used for communication because it is in a Faulted state.
This is a general message that you can get for a number of reasons. If you get this message then browse the config or cache service and run the asmx to get a more specific error.
HTTPS site
You will see this message display when you set the site to HTTPS in Server Manager but you have not updated the DefaultSPFServerURL in the server site's settings to include HTTPS. The error in the log file gives further detail:
Failed to connect to the default SPFServer specified in the configuration file ---&gt; SPF.Server.SPFException: Failed to connect to default SPFServer at
'http://localhost/SPFServer/ServerRequest.asm'.
Solution
When you set a site to include HTTPS, you must change the DefaultSPFServerURL in the server site's settings in Server Manager to HTTPS.
Behavior protocols
Error message
The Communication Object System.ServiceModel.Channels.ServiceChannel cannot be used for communication because it is in a Faulted state.
The error in the log file displays information that the SmartPlant Foundation application server failed to connect to the Config service at 'net.pipe://localhost/SPFConfigService'.
Problem
The required protocols for cache service are net.pipe and net.tcp and one or both have not been enabled on the site’s virtual directory.
Solution
Configure Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager to use net.pipe and net.tcp.
 This procedure is required to support the caching service on the application server.
1. Open IIS Manager.
2. Expand the Sites node.
3. Select the Default Web Site node.
4. Under Edit Site in the Actions pane, click Bindings.
5. Select each of the following binding types, click Edit. and add the following binding information for each type:
net.pipe -- Binding information: *
net.tcp -- Binding information: 808.*
6. Click Close to finish editing bindings.
Cache service
Could not load type System.ServiceModel.Activation.HttpModule
After Windows features and additional roles are installed, you may find that you get this error:
Could not load type System.ServiceModel.Activation.HttpModule.
This is because you’ve installed some .Net 3.5 features on top of .Net 4.0 features. To resolve this issue:
Windows Developer machine
1. Open a visual studio command console.
2. Run the command aspnet_regiis.exe /iru.
3. Now connect to the cache server using named pipes.
Windows Server
1. Browse to C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319 to get into the right directory.
2. Open a command prompt and type C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\aspnet_regiis -iru.
System.ServiceModel.FaultException
System.ServiceModel.FaultException: The server was unable to process the request due to the internal error.
Solution
To resolve this, follow the steps on resolving this from Could not load type System.ServiceModel.Activation.HttpModule.
Rejected credentials
Initialization method ENSGenerationDocMgmtUnitTests.Test_Init threw exception. System.ServiceModel.Security.SecurityNegotiationException: System.ServiceModel.Security.SecurityNegotiationException: The 
server has rejected the client credentials. ---> System.Security.Authentication.InvalidCredentialException: The server has rejected the client credentials. ---> System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: The 
logon attempt failed.
You will see this error on the server.
1. If you see this error then you need to check that the ConfigServiceURL in the SmartPlant Foundation server web.config file and the CacheServiceURL in the ConfigService/SPFSharedSettings/SPFAppServer.config are all set
correctly.
2. Check the identity of the site application pools are valid. For example:
From
<add key="CacheServiceURL" value="net.pipe://localhost.ingrnet.com/SPFCacheServer/CacheService.svc" />
To
<add key="CacheServiceURL" value="net.pipe://localhost/SPFCacheServer/CacheService.svc" />
Error changing scope due to cache synchronization failure
Problem
When changing scope in the Desktop Client, you receive an error indicating that "one dataset was expected when updating an object from the database."
Solution
This error can be caused by having multiple worker processes set greater than one on the cache application pool, which leads to having multiple independent caches running on the system that get out of synchronization.
Multiple worker processes are supported only on the main SPFServer service application pool. The maximum number of worker processes that can be configured on the license, cache and configuration service application pools is limited
to one. Multiple worker processes are not supported on these application pools. For more information, see Configure maximum worker processes.
Integration
This section contains troubleshooting information about working in an integrated environment.
Problems viewing composite 3D documents
Problems
One or more of the following problems occur while viewing composite 3D documents:
Cannot select certain plant items to view the properties.
The wrong properties are displayed when selecting a plant item.
Some graphics are automatically selected that are not associated with a plant item after clicking View Item in Model and selecting a composite 3D document.
Possible Cause
These problems can occur if you are viewing composite 3D documents in SmartPlant Foundation versions 2016 or later that were created using SmartPlant Foundation versions 2014 R3 and earlier.
Solution
 You must have SmartPlant Foundation 2016 or later and SmartPlant Markup Plus 2016 or later installed.
Create a new composite 3D document:
1. Click Find > Composite 3D Documents to search for the problematic composite 3D document.
2. Right-click the composite 3D document and select Show Published Documents.
3. Click File > New > Composite 3D Document to create a new composite 3D document.
4. Select all of the published documents, then drag the documents to the new composite 3D document.
5. Verify that the new composite 3D document is properly working and delete the problematic composite 3D document.
Merge fails with SPF File Service error
Error Message
The merge process fails and the composite ZVF model cannot be viewed. The following error message is shown in the SPFFileServicesTraceRolling.log:
SPF.FileService.SPFException: File can not be found
Possible Cause
This error can occur if it takes longer than 15 minutes to merge a large published document with a composite document that contains a ZVF file. The merge cannot complete because the license token times out.
Solution
You can ensure that merging a large published document with a composite document does not timeout and completes processing by increasing the default license timeout period. This is done by increasing the timeout setting in the
LicenseTimeoutSeconds property, located in the SmartPlant Foundation Site Settings node.
For more information on the LicenseTimeoutSeconds setting, see Site Settings node properties.
Border file will not display
Problem
The border file will not display when clicking View and Markup on a file in the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
Possible Cause
This can occur after restoring a backup in an integrated environment, when the master files have been moved to a new vault and the reference files have not been moved.
Solution
Set the location of reference files, such as border files, using SmartPlant Markup Plus:
1. Click Tools > Options.
2. From the Preferences tab, click Viewer Options.
3. From the View tab, enter the UNC or local file paths for the locations of the files in the Reference file search path box.
 Use semicolons (;) to separate multiple paths.
For example: \\[Server name 1]\share\borders;\\[Server name 2]\share\files
4. Click View and Markup on the file in the Desktop Client to see the border.
License still in use after integration operation
Problem
After performing an integration command from within an authoring tool on one workstation, integration commands on another workstation fail because the license of the user is reported as still being in use on the workstation where the first
command was used.
Solution
To release the license, close the authoring tool application on the workstation where the first command was performed.
The license is taken for use on a workstation whenever any integration command is initiated, even if the operation is not completed. For example, the license is taken when you click the Retrieve command, but it is not released if you
click Cancel on the Retrieve dialog box that appears.
Once the SmartPlant Foundation license is taken to use integration commands like publish or retrieve in an authoring tool application, the license is not released for use elsewhere until the authoring tool application is closed.
Light green text labels display when viewing a Smart P&ID file
After retrieving and viewing a Smart P&ID file in SmartPlant Foundation or SmartPlant Basic Integrator, light green text displays in the label text.
Solution
The Arial Narrow font was used when modifications were made to the file in Smart P&ID, but the font is not available on your computer. To solve this problem, install the Arial Narrow font from Microsoft Office.
Unable to upload files using Excel Import
Problem
Users are unable to upload files to the SmartPlant Foundation server using Microsoft Excel import, even when using the out-of-the-box delivered templates, such as Document List or Document Load.
Solution
If you want to upload files using Microsoft Excel import, the properties SPFLocalDirectory, SPFLocalFileName, and SPFLocalHostName must be mapped in the selected template file. Contact your administrator for further information.
Missing manage integration options when setting integration defaults
Problem
When attempting to set integration default settings for authoring tools in the Desktop Client, the Manage Integration Options command is not available on the short-cut menu of the tool signature object. Even if logged on as Superuser, this
command is missing.
Solution
The logged-on user must be associated with the SmartPlantAdminIntegrator role. By default the SmartPlantAdminIntegrator role is not associated with any user, including Superuser.
Tool signature is empty when publishing PBS
Problem
If you enter a space as the first character of your plant, area or unit name, SmartPlant Foundation still allows the registration of the plant without displaying an error message. However, when you try to publish the Plant Breakdown
Structure (Publish PBS) in SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client, an error message is displayed.
Error message
Database exception error message
The database records the error as an exception detailed as below:
Solution
The plant name must begin with an alphabetical character. You must follow the plant naming guidelines when naming your plant for top-level hierarchy items used in an integrated environment.
Do not use a digit or a space as the first character for any plant name or any of the other plant elements such as area or unit.
Do not use any special characters in any part of the plant name or plant breakdown structure such as area or unit.
These limitations are imposed by the authoring tools and the database software. For further information see the Plant Naming Guidelines section of Creating an alternate plant breakdown structure (PBS).
Unable to change default publish and retrieve settings
Problem
When publishing and retrieving, you cannot override the default publish or retrieve settings. This problem occurs even when logged on as the user Superuser.
Solution
The logged-on user must be associated with the SmartPlantAdminIntegrator role. By default the SmartPlantAdminIntegrator role is not associated with any user, including Superuser.
Unable to expand a plant in tree view or publish custom PBS after upgrade
Problem
After upgrading with a custom PBS, if you are unable to expand your plant in the Tree view and you are also unable to publish your custom PBS hierarchy, this problem may be the result of having overwritten your custom PBS hierarchy
graph definition during the upgrade.
Solution
You must regenerate and reload your custom PBS Hierarchy graph definition.
1. To recreate the PBS Hierarchy graph definition used to expand the hierarchy in the Tree view, find the CMF you modified in the Desktop Client (Find > Integration > Schema Documents).
2. Right-click the CMF file version (CMF_Ver), and click Launch CMF File manager (PBS).
3. Click Load.
 One node should appear in the tree view.
4. Make sure the Inherit Clicked Item option is selected.
5. Select the PBSComponent object.
6. Select only the classdefs of your hierarchy.
7. Click Export.
8. Edit the generated file SPF_01_AuthoringSchema.xml.
9. Copy and paste the DirectedGraphDef, DeleteInstruction, and Rel object code into a new XML file.
An example of this code is shown below (yours will vary, depending on your hierarchy).
<Container>
<DirectedGraphDef>
<IDirectedGraphDef
GraphDefn="+ISPFPlant/+ISPFPlant/,SPFPlant_SPFArea_21/SPFArea/+ISPFPlant/,SPFArea_SPFUnit_21/SPFUnit/SPFArea/,SPFUnit_SPFBuilding_21/SPFBuilding/SPFUnit/,SPFBuilding_SPFBlock_21/SPFBlock/SPFBuilding/,SPF
/>
<IObject UID="DGD_PBSHierarchy" Name="PBSHierarchy" />
<ISchemaObj SchemaRevVer="04.02.05" ByCust="True" />
</DirectedGraphDef>
<DeleteInstruction>
<IObject UID="DeleteInstruction-2" Name="Delete_{E3D82BE6-7065-4B1B-8525-1B78D80FB472}" />
<IRefObject RefClass="Rel" RefUID="{E3D82BE6-7065-4B1B-8525-1B78D80FB472}" RefName="" />
<IInstruction />
<IRefRel RefDefUID="GraphDefInterfaceDef" RefUID1="DGD_PBSHierarchy" RefUID2="IPlant" />
<IDeleteInstruction DeleteTransition="Terminated" />
</DeleteInstruction>
<Rel>
<IObject UID="DGD_PBSHierarchy.ISPFPlant" />
<IRel UID1="DGD_PBSHierarchy" UID2="ISPFPlant" DefUID="GraphDefInterfaceDef" />
<ISchemaObj SchemaRevVer="04.02.05" ByCust="True" />
</Rel>
</Container>
10. Launch the Desktop Client Loader (File > Loader), and select the XML file you just created.
11. Click Process.
Unable to publish or retrieve documents
A problem can occur when either the long path to the installation location for SmartPlant Client has spaces in it, or the first eight characters of the folder name are identical to the first eight characters of the name of another folder with the
same path. If this problem exists on your computer, you may receive one of the following error messages during publish or retrieve. To fix the problem, first try reinstalling the SmartPlant Client software, described in the Solution section
below. If reinstalling the software does not work, you can attempt to edit the path in the registry, described in the Alternate Solution section.
Error messages
Publish error
The following message appears when you click Finish in the Publish Wizard:
When you view the log file, you will see the following error description:
Retrieve error
The following message appears when you click Finish in the Retrieve Wizard:
When you view the log file, you will see the following error description:
 For more information about this error, see the "BUG: COM/OLE Server Fails to Start on Windows NT 4.0 (Q185126)" article on the Microsoft Support Web site at the following URL: http://support.microsoft.com/default.aspx?
scid=kb;en-us;Q185126.
Solution
Reinstall the SmartPlant Client. The path to the installation location must meet the following criteria:
Folder names must contain no spaces.
Folder names must be no longer than eight characters.
The first eight characters of the folder name must not be identical to the first eight characters of the name of another folder with the same path.
By default, SmartPlant Client is installed in the following folder: C:\Program Files\SmartPlant\SmartPlant Client. If you experience a problem with publishing and retrieving, you must change the default installation location when you
reinstall the software.
For instructions on installing the SmartPlant Client, see Install product software.
Alternate solution
If reinstalling the SmartPlant Client software does not fix the problem, you can replace the long path to EFProgressBar.exe with the short path in the registry. Be sure to try reinstalling the SmartPlant Client software before you edit the
registry.
 Manually editing the registry incorrectly can cause problems that may require you to reinstall the operating system. Problems created by the incorrect editing of the registry may not be possible to solve. Edit the registry at your
own risk.
1. Start the Registry Editor (regedit.exe).
You should back up the registry before you edit it.
For information about how to edit the registry, see the Changing Keys and Values online Help topic or the Add and Delete Information in the Registry and Edit Registry Data online Help topics in Registry Editor.
2. Click Edit > Find.
3. In the Find what box, type EFProgressBar.ProgressBar.
4. Under Look at, make sure that only Keys is selected, and click Find Next.
5. When the software finds the key, click the Clsid folder under EFProgressBar.ProgressBar.
6. In the right pane, double-click the string to view it.
7. To make it easier to search for the class ID, copy the class ID in the Value data box.
 To copy the class ID, select the class ID, and right-click the selected text. On the shortcut menu, click Copy.
8. Click Edit > Find.
9. Paste the class ID that you copied in step 7 into the Find what box.
 To paste the class ID, right-click in the Find what box, and click Paste on the shortcut menu.
10. Click Find Next.
11. When the software finds the class ID, click the LocalServer32 folder under the key.
12. Double-click the string in the right pane to edit the path.
13. In the Edit String box, replace the long path with the short path to the installation location for EFProgressBar.exe (installed with the SmartPlant Client).
The default long path to EFProgressBar.exe is C:\Program Files\SmartPlant\SmartPlant Client\Program\EFProgressBar.exe. The short path for the default installation location might look like the following:
C:\PROGRA~1\SMARTP~1\FRAMEW~3\Program\EFPROG~1.EXE. The short path may be slightly different for different computers.
To find the elements in the short path, you can open a command prompt window and run the dir /x command on each folder in the path, starting at the drive letter and finishing with the folder where EFProgressBar.exe is
installed.
14. Click OK.
15. Click Registry > Exit to close the Registry Editor.
Unable to publish or retrieve after a domain name change
Problem
After changing the domain name of the SmartPlant Foundation server, you cannot publish or retrieve from an integrated authoring tool.
Solution
If the domain name was included in the URL of the SmartPlant Foundation server, you will need to re-register the authoring tools with SmartPlant Foundation.
Unable to publish Plant Breakdown Structure (PBS) document
If the SmartPlant Foundation or SmartPlant Basic Integrator server is not in the list of trusted sites for the web browser, you may not be able to publish the plant breakdown structure (PBS) document.
Error Message
The following message appears when you attempt to publish the PBS document:
Solution
Add the server to the list of trusted sites.
1. In the Control Panel, double-click Internet Options.
 You can also click Tools > Internet Options in Internet Explorer to access the Internet Options dialog box.
2. Click Security.
3. Click Trusted Sites.
4. Click Sites.
5. Type the name of the SmartPlant Foundation server in the Add this Web site to the zone text box.
6. Click Add.
7. Click OK.
8. Click OK.
Unable to select a listed document for retrieve
Problem
When retrieving, you cannot select documents in the list because they are disabled.
Solution
When retrieving, you can only retrieve documents for which you have proper owning group access. Even though you cannot retrieve documents for which you do not have appropriate owning group access, they are still displayed in the list
but are disabled.
To gain access to these documents, the administrator can modify the relationship between the user's role and the retrieve access group. For more information, see Using owning groups with Publish and Retrieve.
Workflow is not valid (no relation to ISPFTEFPublishedDocVersion)
Problem
Publish operation fails because a publish workflow could not be attached. The ISPFTEFPublishedDocVersion interface is not related to the selected publish workflow.
Solution
Do not remove the relationship between any publish workflows and ISPFTEFPublishedDocVersion under any circumstance. If the relationship has been removed, recreate it in SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
Unable to load published data into a project
Problem
During the loading of published data to a project, you could encounter error messages such as these:
The Relationship already exists.
Failed to process the TaggedItemGroup data: Commit has failed, Object cannot be created. The UID is not unique in this config.
The updating of the claimed item failed.
Solution
To resolve this issue, you can add a key to Server Manager, ProcessDataLoadSequentialAtProject. When this flag is set to true, the load operations for the project are restricted to use only one thread, thereby avoiding any multi-thread
issues. The flag can be turned off once the load operation is complete.
This flag is applicable only for loading data into project scenarios (ICC2).
The flag does not affect the loading of documents at the plant level, only at the project level.
You must add the flag to the Server Manager settings; it is not available by default.
Consolidation failed as process was unable to lock the object(s)
Problem
When trying to run a CDW consolidate task, the process fails with the following error message:
Consolidation failed as process was unable to lock the object(s)
Solution
If you submit a consolidate task that attempts to lock objects currently locked by another consolidate task, you receive the following message:
Consolidation was unable to lock required objects. Please retry
Retry the failed consolidation task when the first completes and the objects are unlocked. For more information about how objects are locked and an explanation of the consolidation operation, see CDW customization.
Unable to publish large data sets
Problem
When you attempt to publish a large data set, from the Excel Publish Wizard feature or any authoring tool, you receive an "HTTP status 404: Not Found" error.
Possible Cause
This error can occur when you attempt to publish a large quantity of data that results in a request that is larger than either the maximum allowed content length or the maximum request length.
Solution
You can change the maximum values to allow larger requests using the following steps on the SmartPlant Foundation application server.
Microsoft Windows uses these values for security, so you should not set the values higher than necessary to allow your publish requests to pass.
You can either increase these values incrementally, continuing to test until the publish is successful, or you can set the values to a significantly higher number, such as 2Gb, and then reduce them until you find the necessary
threshold.
The smaller of the two values will override the larger of the two.
Change the value for the maximum allowed length:
1. Click Start > Control Panel > Administrative Tools > Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager.
2. Expand the tree in the left panel of the IIS Manager dialog box to display the SmartPlant Foundation sites, and select the site you registered your authoring tools to for integration.
3. On the Features View tab in the middle panel, double-click Request Filtering.
4. Click Edit Feature Settings in the right panel.
5. In the Edit Request Filtering Settings dialog box, increase the value for Maximum allowed content length (Bytes).
Change the value for the maximum request length:
1. Click Start > All Programs > Intergraph SmartPlant Foundation > SmartPlant Foundation Server Manager.
2. Expand the tree in the left panel to display the SmartPlant Foundation sites, and select the site you registered your authoring tools to for integration.
3. Click Settings in the tree under the site.
4. Find the value provided for the Site path property, and use Windows Explorer to open that folder.
5. Running it as an administrator, use a text editing application, such as Notepad, to open the web.config file.
6. Search for maxRequestLength in the file, and increase the value provided for that setting.
 The maxRequestLength is measured in kilobytes.
Any process fails and the error message refers to invalid data or a missing property
Problems
When a process fails, you receive an error message that refers to invalid data or a missing property. For example:
<Process name> provided invalid document data. <Property name> EnumEnum property in a property of document object.
Excel provided invalid document data. No EnumEnum property in a property of document object.
You might receive error messages like this for any process such as publishing documents, publishing a document to Excel, or creating a process case.
Possible causes
These problems occur when the value of a property in a server resx file is changed and that property is one of these data types:
Class definitions
Interface definitions
Enum Lists
EnumEnums
Property definitions
Relationship definitions
UoM Enums
Edge definitions
The value of properties of these data types must exactly match the item name in the database and component schema files.
Because properties in a server resx file are altered during the localization process, the mismatch is usually caused by translation. Because item names of these data types in the database and component schema files cannot be translated,
the properties in the resx files that refer to those items must not be translated.
For example, if you change this:
<data name="99999" xml:space="preserve">
<value>RefiningAndSupply</value>
</data>
To this:
<data name="99999" xml:space="preserve">
<value>RaffinierenUndLiefern</value>
</data>
Processes that rely on data name 99999 will not complete, and you will receive an error message.
Solution
Repeat the localization process for the server resx files, making sure that none of the following data types are translated. For instructions, see Generate localization resource files.
Class definitions
Interface definitions
Enum Lists
Methods
EnumEnums
Property definitions
Relationship definitions
UoM Enums
Edge definitions
Smart 3D fails to publish when publishing from a remote server
Problem
You are publishing items in Smart 3D from a remote server and the publishing fails. This is an intermittent problem that occurs in SmartPlant Client when the remote server session is creating temporary folders. You may see the following
message.
 By default, Remote Desktop Services creates a separate temporary folder for each active session that you have on a remote computer. These temporary folders are created on the remote computer in a Temp folder under your
profile folder and are named with the sessionid.
Solution
Do not allow the remote server to create temporary folders during your remote sessions.
1. Connect to the remote server as an administrator.
2. Type gpedit in a Command Prompt window to open the Group Policy Editor.
3. Under Computer Configuration, browse to Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Temporary folders.
4. Double-click the Do not use temp folders per session setting.
5. Click Enabled to prevent the creation of a separate temporary folder for each terminal server session.
 To allow the creation of a separate temporary folder for each terminal server session, click Disabled.
6. Click OK.
Creating an Inconsistency Report in the background fails
Problem
When creating Inconsistency Reports using documents with very large sets of data or comparing data across several sources, it is possible for the inconsistency report task to time out and fail.
Solution
Find the failed task, right-click on it, and select Retry.
The process will begin again, starting with the next chunk after the task failed, as indicated by the number in the Processed chunk count property for the task.
For example, if the Processed chunk count is 52, then the task will begin again starting with chunk 53.
You can see the total number of chunks to be processed in the Total chunk count property for the task.
The Processed file list contains a list of all the files that have been compared so far.
SmartPlant Foundation Clients
This section contains troubleshooting information about the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
Client connection
Log on is temporarily blocked
Problem
After several failed log on attempts, you cannot log on and receive the following error.
Account temporarily blocked due to the number of failed login attempts
Solution
SmartPlant Foundation can be configured to temporarily lock an account when a set number of failed login attempts is reached. However, after a set period of time, the account is automatically unlocked. This has been instated to protect
the system from malicious password attacks. For more information on these system settings, contact your system administrator.
Unable to connect to the Desktop Client
Error message
The following error message appears when you click OK in the Desktop Client Logon Information dialog box.
The server ServerName is not responding.
Solution
This error is caused when the host or virtual directory is not accessible. To fix this problem, do the following:
1. Check the corresponding <ServerName> value in the ServerConnections.xml file, located in \ProgramData\SmartPlant on the client computer.
For example, make sure that the following key has the correct informationn: <server name="<ServerName>" spfserver="<HostName>" spfwebdir="<Virtual Directory>" secure="True" trusted="True" />.
2. If a local connection entry has been configured, check the settings.xml file for the user on the client computer, located in %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Roaming\SmartPlant\Foundation\Settings.xml.
For example, make sure that the following key has the correct information, as seen in this example: <server name="LocalEntry" host="<HostName>" dir="<Virtual Directory>" ssl="True" trusted="True" />.
3. Check that the host as specified (<HostName>/<Servername>) is accessible over the network.
4. Check that the virtual directory on the SmartPlant Foundation server is accessible.
Unable to connect to a server after domain name change
Problem
After changing a domain name on the network, you are unable to connect to a server on the domain via the Desktop Client.
Solution
Review all objects in the configuration that are defined using fully qualified domain names, such as hosts, server names, IIS host names, and so forth. Update the settings with the new names, and recycle the application pools on the
server.
Unable to run Desktop Client using HTTPS
If you are unable to run the Desktop Client using HTTPS, the secure server certificate might not be installed on the client.
Error message
"The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure."
Solution
Use Internet Explorer to install the server certificate on the client.
1. Start Internet Explorer.
2. In the address box, type "https:\\server_name" using the name of the secure server.
3. In the Security Alert dialog box, click View Certificate.
4. In the Certificate dialog box, click Install Certificate.
5. Click Next in each window of the Install Certificate wizard, and then click Finish.
6. In the Security Warning dialog box, click Yes.
7. Click OK at the success confirmation message prompt.
8. Close Internet Explorer.
HTTP 404 error message after installation
Error message
An HTML 404 error message displays when you attempt to run the Desktop Client.
Problems
Several situations can cause this error. Check for the following problems, in particular:
ASP .NET connections are not allowed on the IIS server.
Solution
Because an HTML 404 error can result from any number of problems, you should try to get a more specific error message from the server.
On the server, open Internet Explorer and enter the following address (or equivalent for your site):
http://localhost/spfserver/ServerRequest.asmx
This action should produce an error screen. The Description should point to where the error is.
Buffering error with IWA
Problem
When integrated Windows Authentication (IWA) is being used as the authentication method and an operation is attempted in the Desktop Client, such as checking in a schema file or attaching files to a design document, the operation fails,
and the following error is received:
"This request requires buffering data to succeed."
Solution
This error can be caused by the IIS property authPersistSingleRequest being set to True or the IIS property authPersistNonNTLM being set to False. To resolve the problem:
For resolution at the site level, set authPersistSingleRequest to False. A sample set of command lines to be run at the command prompt follows:
cd %SystemRoot%\System32\inetsrv
appcmd.exe set config -section:system.webServer/security/authentication/windowsAuthentication /authPersistSingleRequest:"False" /commit:apphost
For resolution at the server level, set authPersistNonNTLM to True. A sample set of command lines to be run at the command prompt follows:
cd %SystemRoot%\System32\inetsrv
appcmd set config /section:windowsAuthentication /authPersistNonNTLM:true
For more information, refer to http://support.microsoft.com/kb/954873 <http://support.microsoft.com/kb/954873> .
Display
Desktop Client dialog boxes appear strangely
Problem
In the Desktop Client, dialog boxes have display problems, such as overlapping text fields or misaligned column headings.
Solution
This problem can be caused by setting the text size in your Windows operating system to larger than 100%. To correct the appearance of Desktop Client dialog boxes, set the text size to100% in the Windows Control Panel Display
settings.
Client APIs and Methods
Activating and using some new commands results in an error message
Problem
When you select a document management command in the Desktop Client, the following error appears:
The [Client API Name] API is not supported.
When the error is dismissed, sometimes an editable form appears, but the form does not perform the actions indicated by the selected command.
Cause
Since the Web Client uses the Desktop Client for configuration purposes, there are a few client APIs and methods that are not supported in the Desktop Client. While these client APIs and methods can be found when searching
administrative tools, they are not configured to appear on menus or shortcut menus in the Desktop Client by default.
The error occurs when an administrator has:
Configured one of these methods to display in the Desktop Client.
Created a new method based on one of these APIs and configured it to display in the Desktop Client.
Solution
Edit the methods, and clear the Display in the Desktop Client check box.
Get workflow items no longer works after upgrade
Problem
When you have upgraded from SmartPlant Foundation 2016 (6.0) to the latest version SmartPlant Foundation 2019 (10.0) or earlier, and use the GetWorkflowStepsForUserAtStatus method definition to return workflow items for a user, the
following error appears:
NotImplementedException
Any previous customization done in SmartPlant Foundation 2016 (6.0) using the GetWorkflowStepsForUserAtStatus method definition is broken after upgrade as the method no longer exists in later versions.
Solution
The GetWorkflowStepsForUserAtStatus method definition is deprecated from SmartPlant Foundation 2016 (6.1) and was replaced with the GetToDoList method definition implemented to take full advantage of the new DAL that was
developed to improve performance.
If you were using the GetWorkflowStepsForUserAtStatus method definition before the upgrade, you will have to modify any custom code to use the GetToDoList method definition.
 The only difference is that the GetToDoList method definition returns all workflow steps that are in a Ready to start status, whereas the GetWorkflowStepsForUserAtStatus method definition returned workflow steps in any status.
Documents
Incorrect revision scheme appears when updating a document
When you update a document, the incorrect revision scheme appears.
Problem
This might occur if you have configured the update form with a new section using the text "RevScheme" in the name of the section.
If you use this text in the name of the section, the system treats it as an overridden section element and retrieves the next or initial major and minor values.
Solution
If configuring a custom update form, do not use the text "RevScheme" in the name of any custom sections. Other text, such as "RevisionDetails" is acceptable. You can include the text "RevScheme" in the name of sections used on the
create, copy, and revise forms, but not in the update form.
Problem when trying to view files with many references
Problem
Session hangs when performing the following actions on document with files having many circular and nested references:
View and Markup
Check Out
Check In
Undo Check Out
Sign Off
Cause
