QueueManagement client API
The QueueManagement client API is used to start or stop queues.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Enable the queue to process documents
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
When set to True, the queue is enabled for processing. When set to False, the queue is disabled and processing requests are
ignored.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
The following methods use the QueueManagement client API to start or stop a queue.
QueueStart
QueueStop
ReclassifyObject client API
The ReclassifyObject client API is used to create methods to change the classification of any classified object.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Show confirm changes page
Parameter 1 default
True
Parameter 1 description
If set to True, displays any changes to the objects as a result of the reclassification in a confirmation dialog box. If set to False,
the changes are committed without a confirmation dialog box.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 2
Allow ClassDef change
Parameter 2 default
False
Parameter 2 description
Indicates whether to allow reclassification if the target class definition is different from the original. If set to FALSE, the
reclassification will not be allowed. If set to TRUE, the reclassification is allowed.
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 3
Top classification
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
The top-level classification for displaying in the tree.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
ENS handling
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
Handle changes to ENS based on reclassification. For example, full renaming or retain ENS name.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
Form purpose
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
The mode in which to prompt for any missing data (usually Update).
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
Classification tree filtering
Parameter 6 default
None
Parameter 6 description
A ~ (tilde) separated list of interfaces to filter for in the classification tree.
Parameter 6 type
String
 The ReclassifyObject client API has an updated argument 6. You can restrict the availability of classification tree nodes in the hierarchy tree by selecting an interface definition in the Reclassify form that you can use to filter the
classifications. This behavior is now consistent with the create method.
The ReclassifiyDocument method uses the ReclassifyObject client API to change the classification of a document.
RegenerateAPIMetadata client API
The RegenerateAPIMetadata client API is used to request a regeneration of the OData API metadata. This client API uses no parameters.
The RegenerateAPIMetadata method uses the RegenerateAPIMetadata client API to regenerate the OData API metadata.
RegenerateMetadata client API
The RegenerateMetadata client API is used to regenerate the metadata in the system with the latest as built. This client API uses no parameters.
The RegenerateMetadata method uses this client API to automatically regenerate the metadata in the system with the latest as built from the user interface.
RegenerateSchemaFiles client API
The RegenerateSchemaFiles client API is used to regenerate the component schema files from the CMF file in the database. This client API uses no parameters.
The RegenerateSchemaFiles method uses the RegenerateSchemaFiles client API to regenerate the component schemas.
ReGenerateSharedObjs client API
The ReGenerateSharedObjs client API is used to update all the shared object relationships for a selected domain. This client API uses no parameters.
The ReGenerateSharedObjs method uses the ReGenerateSharedObjs client API to recreate and update the shared objects within a selected domain.
RegisterAndCheckIn client API
The RegisterAndCheckIn client API is used to transfer a file to the file server. The file is registered on the file server and virus checked if set up during the process.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Class name
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Class name of the file object to be created
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Reference file not found action
Parameter 2 default
PROMPT
Parameter 2 description
Indicates what should happen if the file being loaded into the system has references files that are not already in the system.
The options are CONTINUE|PROMPT|ABORT.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Delete local files configuration (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 3 default
True~True
Parameter 3 description
Includes two options, separated by a ~, to control whether local files are deleted when attached and whether to expose the
option for deleting local files on the form used for creation.
Parameter 3 type
String
The following methods use this client API to attach files to various objects.
AttachAdapter
AttachDatasheet
AttachDesignFileToVersion
AttachFileToPublishedVersion
AttachTEFSchema
AttachTemplateFileToVersion
DEVAttachFileToObject
SPFSmartConverterControlAttachFile
RegisterAuthoringTool client API
The RegisterAuthoringTool client API is used to register a tool with a selected plant. You can register the Instrument Process Design (IPD), Line List Authoring (LLA), Plant Breakdown Structure (PBS), and Process Case tools with a
selected plant.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Tool ID
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The tool ID of the registering tool, for example, PBS, LLA, IPD, and so on.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Domain
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
This is the domain of the authoring tool.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Database Code
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
The database code of the authoring tool. The database code is included in the authoring signature name. This is optional.
Parameter 3 type
String
The following methods use the RegisterAuthoringTool client API to register tools with a plant.
Register IPD
Register LLA
Register PBS
Register ProcessCase
RejectWorkflow client API
The RejectWorkflow client API provides the functionality to close an entire workflow for a document and set all the steps to the rejected status.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Comments mandatory
Parameter 1 description
Specifies whether comments must be provided in the dialog box provided to the user before they can complete the reject
operation.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 1 default
False
The RejectWorkflow method uses the RejectWorkflow client API to close a workflow for the selected document.
RelationshipManagement client API
The RelationshipManagement client API is used to create configured relationships between a source object and a set of target objects.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
RelDef/EdgeDef to related Items
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Relationship definition or edge definition (prefixed with +/-) from the source object to the related Items.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Filter Column Set UID
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The UID of the column set for displaying the filter options.
Parameter 2 type
String
RemoveDocInLoadQueue client API
The RemoveDocInLoadQueue client API is used to remove a selected document from the load queue. This client API uses no parameters.
The RemoveDocFromQueue methods use the RemoveDocInLoadQueue client API to removed the selected document from the load queue.
ReplaceFiles client API
The ReplaceFiles client API is used to replace files attached to an object with a new version of the file or to attach new files to an object.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Use next major revision for revise
Parameter 1 default
True
Parameter 1 description
Next revision control for revise. By default, it is set to True, which means that the next major revision is honored. If set to
False, the next minor revision is honored.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 2
File configuration
Parameter 2 default
SPFDesignFile~SPFFileComposition~PROMPT
Parameter 2 description
The file configuration FileClassDef~FileRelDef~ReferenceAction~SeedFileTemplateDocName. SPFFileComposition is the
default relationship definition if none is specified, and the seed file document is optional. Reference file not found actions are
CONTINUE | PROMPT | ABORT.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Sign off new revision
Parameter 3 default
False
Parameter 3 description
Signs off the new revision
Parameter 3 type
Boolean
Parameter 4
Columnset
Parameter 4 default
CS_CopyToLocalColumnSet
Parameter 4 description
UID of the column set that will be used on the Replace Files dialog box.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
RestrictedFileTypeUIDs
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
The file types are restricted from download or copy during replace files.
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
Extract Content
Parameter 6 default
None
Parameter 6 description
Content Extractions will be enabled during replace files
Parameter 6 type
Boolean
The following methods use the ReplaceFiles client API to replace files attached to a working or current document objects.
ReplaceWorkingDocFiles
UpRevCurrentDocReplaceFilesAndSignOff
NewUpRevCurrentDocReplaceFilesAndSignOf
ResetProfile client API
The ResetProfile client API is used to reset a user's window locations as saved in their user profile. This client API uses no parameters.
The ResetProfile method uses the ResetProfile client API to clear the information saved in the user's profile for the Desktop Client.
ResponseManagement client API
The ResponseManagement client API provides the functionality to open the Response Management user interface from a workflow step. The Response Management user interface allows you to manage comments and actions of other
users, typically email or paper users who are involved in the transmittal but do not have access to the application.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Step Status
Parameter 1 description
The status of the step from which the Response Management user interface was launched. The options are RS (ready to
start), NR (not ready), RJ (rejected), and TE (terminated).
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
None
The following methods use the ResponseManagement client API to open the Response Management user interface:
ResponseManagement
StepResponseManagement
WFItemResponseManagement
RestartSchedulerPolling client API
The RestartSchedulerPolling client API is used to stop the current scheduler thread polling for tasks and start a new thread. This client API uses no parameters.
The RestartSchedulerPolling method uses the RestartSchedulerPolling client API to stop the current scheduler thread and start a new one.
RetryTask client API
The RetryTask client API is used to attempt to run a scheduled task that previously failed to run successfully. This client API uses no parameters.
The RetryTask method uses the RetryTask client API to restart a scheduled task.
Revise client API
The Revise client API is used to revise documents.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Title
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The title that will appear at the top of the dialog box that appears.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Copy files
Parameter 2 default
True
Parameter 2 description
If set to True, attached files are copied to the new revision. If set to False, the files attached to the previous revision are not to
copied to the new revision.
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 3
Show hidden files
Parameter 3 default
False
Parameter 3 description
Set to true to allow selection of hidden files (Non-viewable and Non-editable files)
Parameter 3 type
Boolean
Parameter 4
File attachment configuration
Parameter 4 default
SPFDesignFile~SPFFileComposition~PROMPT
Parameter 4 description
File attachment configuration (optional) FileClassDef~FileRelDef~ReferenceAction~SeedFileTemplateDocName.
SPFFileComposition is the default relationship definition if none specified and the seed file document is optional. Reference
file not found actions are CONTINUE | PROMPT | ABORT
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
Details of secondary class (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
The details of the secondary class to create and its relationship to the parent class definition, separated using a ~
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
Events to subscribe to on cross config revise (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 6 default
None
Parameter 6 description
Comma separated list of events to create subscriptions to the origin object when revising into a sub configuration
Parameter 6 type
String
Parameter 7
Events to subscribe to when claiming related data (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 7 default
None
Parameter 7 description
Comma separated list of events to create subscriptions to the origin object when claiming related data
Parameter 7 type
String
Parameter 8
Workflow processing option
Parameter 8 default
None
Parameter 8 description
For Desktop Client - The option selected determines whether or not creation or revision wizards should include a
workflow page to choose a workflow to attach to the object. For example, None, which is the default option, indicates
that no workflow page appears and no workflow is attached to the object. Other options include Select, SelectRequired,
or a named workflow. For more information on the options, see Configure create object to attach workflow.
For Web Client - There is no workflow selection in the create pages. The Web Client chooses the applicable workflow
unless a named workflow is specified. However, if more than one applicable workflow is found, the software does not
attach any workflow to the object.
Parameter 8 type
String
Parameter 9
Update References after revise (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 9 default
True
Parameter 9 description
If set to True, reference relationships are updated after the revise operation is complete. If set to False, the reference
relationships are not updated.
Parameter 9 type
Boolean
Parameter 10
Column set to present Claimable Items (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 10 default
None
Parameter 10 description
The name of the column set to present items to be claimed.
Parameter 10 type
String
Parameter 11
Delete local files configuration (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 11 default
True~True
Parameter 11 description
Two separate options, connected with a ~, to control whether local files are deleted when attached to the new revision and
placed in the vault and whether or not the check box to control the deletion of files is exposed on the dialog box.
Parameter 11 type
String
Parameter 12
Use next major revision for revise
Parameter 12 default
True
Parameter 12 description
Revision control for revise to next major or minor revision.
If set to True, the next major revision is created.
If set to False, the next minor revision is created.
Parameter 12 type
Boolean
Parameter 13
Run in asynchronous mode for bulk revise (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 13 default
True
Parameter 13 description
If set to True, the bulk revise process runs in an asynchronous mode and a revise notification object for monitoring the revise
status gets created. If set to False, no notification object gets created.
Parameter 13 type
Boolean
Parameter 14
Revise column set for invalid documents (Not Supported in the Web Client)
Parameter 14 default
ReviseInvalidDocColumnSet
Parameter 14 description
The revise column set for invalid documents
Parameter 14 type
String
Parameter 15
RestrictedFileTypeUIDs
Parameter 15 default
None
Parameter 15 description
The file types are restricted from download or copy during revise.
Parameter 15 type
String
Parameter 16
Relationship UIDs to Copy
Parameter 16 default
None
Parameter 16 description
Enter the comma-separated list of UIDS of the document to object relationship definition which need to be copied on to the
new document revision. Ex: FDWDocRevAsset,FDWDocRevTag
Parameter 16 type
String
The following methods use the Revise client API to revise specific types of documents.
RevisionRevise
SPFDatasheetDocumentRevisionRevise
SPFLLALineListRecordDocumentRevisionRevise
TemplateRevisionRevise
RollupWithConfigOption client API
The RollupWithConfigOption client API is used to run a rollup using a configuration option object.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
ReRollup
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
Indicates whether you can re-rollup line segments to form line list records.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
The RollupSegments method uses the RollupWithConfigOption client API to roll up line segments into list records.
SchemaImport client API
The SchemaImport client API is used to launch the Schema Import Wizard. This client API uses no parameters.
The SchemaImportWizard method uses the SchemaImport client API to open the Schema Import Wizard where you can load schema changes into the database.
SDAApproveSDVJob client API
The SDAApproveSDVJob client API is used to allow users to approve the load job.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Severity to allow approval of Validation step
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Severity level for approving the Validation step
Parameter 1 type
String
 The severity level value set in Severity to allow approval of Validation step argument must be available in the Severity Levels option in the System Options wizard in Data Validator Administration.
For example, if the order of severity in the Severity Levels option in the System Options wizard is set as 1.Warning and 2.Error:
When the Severity to allow approval of Validation step argument is set to Warning, the user can approve the step if there are only warnings in the validation report but no errors.
When the Severity to allow approval of Validation step argument is set to Error, the user can approve the step even if there are warnings and errors in the validation report.
The following methods uses the SDAApproveSDVJob client API to approve the load job.
SDAApproveSDVJob
SDAApproveSDVJobOnError
SDADeleteSDVJob client API
The SDADeleteSDVJob client API allows users to delete the load job. This client API uses no parameters.
The SDADeleteSDVJob method uses the SDADeleteSDVJob client API to delete the load job. The method contains the condition, IsInActiveWorkflow. This condition checks that the load job status is not set to Completed. If the status
of the load job is set to Completed, the Delete action is not available in the Actions menu, and the user cannot delete the load job.
SDARelationshipBuilder client API
The SDARelationshipBuilder client API is used to create and manage relationships and support the termination of relationships.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Title
Parameter 1 default
Relationship Builder
Parameter 1 description
Title to be displayed on the main Relationship Builder view
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Enable Terminate
Parameter 2 default
False
Parameter 2 description
Enable the termination of relationships in the Relationship Builder view
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 3
Security Rule Definition
Parameter 3 default
Parameter 3 description
Common criteria to apply on all the entry points
Parameter 3 type
String
The following methods use the SDARelationshipBuilder client API to create and manage relationships and support the termination of relationships.
SDARelationshipBuilder
PrepareSchemaChangeRequestRelBuilder
SDASDVDownloadValidationReport client API
The SDASDVDownloadValidationReport client API allows users to download the validation report for the load job. This client API uses no parameters.
The SDASDVDownloadValidationReport method uses the SDASDVDownloadValidationReport client API to download the validation report for the load job.
SDAShowMySDVJobs client API
The SDAShowMySDVJobs client API is used to show users the My load jobs or the My Submittals pages.
 You can use Parameter 1 to specify the VTLJob class definition if you want to view the Data Validator jobs in the Web Client. These jobs are created using the Data Validator Job Management module in the Desktop Client.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Class definition UID
Parameter 1 default
SDALoader
Parameter 1 description
A comma-separated list of class definition UIDs used for getting the Data Validator jobs. Example: SDALoader,VTLJob.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Title
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The title of the Show My Load jobs page or the Show My SDV Jobs page
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Column set name
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
Name of the column set used to show the details of Data Validator jobs
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
Property name
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
Property name to receive progress percentage
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
Not used
Parameter 6
PropertyComparison
Parameter 6 description
Properties to compare for filtering
Parameter 6 type
String
The following methods use the SDAShowMySDVJobs client API to show users the summary and progress of all their load jobs or submittals, including deletion.
SDAShowMySDVLoader
ShowDataCaptureLoader
SDAViewSDVJobSummary client API
The SDAViewSDVJobSummary client API allows users to view the summary details of the load job. This client API uses no parameters.
The SDAViewSDVJobSummary method uses the SDAViewSDVJobSummary client API to display the summary details of the load job.
SectionGenerate client API
The SectionGenerate client API is used to open the automatically generate a section for an interface definition based on the exposed properties.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
FormBuilder Method Name
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The name of the method used to launch the Form Builder window to edit the new section after is has been generated
automatically.
Parameter 1 type
String
The SectionGenerate method uses the SectionGenerate client API to automatically create a new section for a selected interface definition.
SetActiveScope client API
The SetActiveScope client API is used to open the Set Active Scope dialog box where you can choose which configurations to query against, which configuration to place newly created items in, and what role you want to play. This client
API uses no parameters.
The SetActiveScope method uses the SetActiveScope client API to open the Set Active Scope dialog box.
SetAsCurrentConfiguration client API
The SetAsCurrentConfiguration client API is used to set both your query and create scopes to the currently selected configuration. This client API uses no parameters.
The SetAsCurrentConfiguration method uses the SetAsCurrentConfiguration client API to change your query and create scopes to the configuration you right-clicked on to access the command.
SetContainer client API
The SetContainer client API is used to change the container that objects are stored in. It opens the Set Container dialog box that shows what container objects of the selected type are currently placed in, and lets you choose a different
container. This client API uses no parameters.
The SPFSetContainer method uses the SetContainer client API to specify what container objects should be stored in. This method is available on the shortcut menu of schema objects.
SetDesignBasisCriteria client API
The SetDesignBasisCriteria client API uses defined criteria based on a specific design basis that will be used to return the objects. This client API uses no parameters.
The SetDesignBasisCriteria method uses the SetDesignBasisCriteria client API to define the criteria used to pull information from the database for a selected design basis query.
SetObjStatus client API
The SetObjStatus client API is used to set an object's status to exclusive claim.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Set exclusive flag
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
Set (True) or unset (False) the exclusive claim flag for the object.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
The following methods use the SetObjStatus client API to set an object's exclusive claim or clear it.
CfgSetObjAsExclusive
CfgUnsetObjAsExclusive
SetUserConfig client API
The SetUserConfig client API is used to set the configuration of the user to the configuration associated with a document. This client API uses no parameters.
The SetUserConfig method uses the SetUserConfig client API to to changed the configuration of the current user to the same configuration as the selected publish document.
ShareUrl client API
The ShareUrl client API allows you to share a URL path to selected data with other users. This client API uses no parameters.
The ShareURL method uses this client API to share a path to the selected data with other users. So when you have a results table of objects you wish to share with others, you can use this feature.
ShowInbox client API
The ShowInbox client API contains the functionality to open the To Do List for the current user.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Inbox title
Parameter 1 description
Provide a title to appear at the top of the To Do List when it is opened using this method.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 2
ColumnSet
Parameter 2 description
The name of the column set that is used for the To Do List view.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 2 default
InboxColumnSet
The WFInbox method uses the ShowInbox client API to open the To Do List.
ShowInboxForObject client API
The ShowInboxForObject client API opens the To Do List with only the tasks associated with the object from which the method was launched.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Not used
Parameter 2
Not used
Parameter 3
To Do List Column Set
Parameter 3 description
Allows you to specify the name of the column set to use for the To Do List view.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 3 default
None
The ShowInboxForObject method uses the ShowInboxForObject client API to open the inbox for objects that have instantiated the ISPFWorkflowItems interface.
ShowObj client API
The ShowObj client API is used to display a form where the details are in read-only mode.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Form Name
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The name of the form opened for viewing
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Title
Parameter 2 default
None

Title displayed at the top of the form opened for viewing
Parameter 2 type
String
The following methods use the ShowObj client API to display a form in read-only mode:
DEVTagInterfaceOwnershipTestDetailsAll
DEVTagInterfaceOwnershipTestDetailsEngOnly
GenDetail
ShowTaskFailure client API
The ShowTaskFailure client API is used to view more information about a failed scheduled task. This client API uses no parameters.
The ShowTaskFailure method uses the ShowTaskFailure client API to display information about the failed task and why it failed.
ShowWorkflow client API
The ShowWorkflow client API shows the attached workflow graphically, using a flowchart-like illustration and colors to indicate the status of each step in the workflow.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Used internally by the software. Not used in the configuration of methods.
Parameter 2
Enable full view
Parameter 2 description
Enables you to view all steps in a workflow.
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 2 default
True
The following methods use the ShowWorkflow client API to display the attached workflow graphically.
BriefViewWorkflow
ObjectViewWorkflow
StepViewWorkflow
ViewWorkflow
SignOff client API
The SignOff client API is used to issue a document. Sign off changes the status of a document revision from Working to Current, and locks the version to prevent users from checking it out or creating a new version of the associated
revision.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Allow working references
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
When set to True, all files referenced by files attached to this document are automatically signed off, too. When set to False,
the references files are not signed off.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 2
Purge superseded versions
Parameter 2 default
False
Parameter 2 description
If set to True, the system will automatically purge earlier versions for this revision that has been superseded. If set to False,
the superseded versions are maintained.
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 15
Allow signoff references
Parameter 15 default
True
Parameter 15 description
Allow signoff reference files during signoff operation. By default it is set to True, which means references files will be shown
during signoff
Parameter 15 type
Boolean
The RevisionSignOff method uses the SignOff client API to change the status of a document version.
SignOffWithForm client API
The SignOffWIthForm client API is used to issue a document using a dialog box where additional notes can be added. Sign off changes the status of a document revision from Working to Current, and locks the version to prevent users
from checking it out or creating a new version of the associated revision.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Allow working references
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
When set to True, all files references by files attached to this document are automatically signed off, too. When set to False,
the references files are not signed off.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 2
Purge superseded versions
Parameter 2 default
False
Parameter 2 description
If set to True, the system will automatically purge earlier versions for this revision that have been superseded. If set to False,
the superseded versions are maintained.
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 15
Allow signoff references
Parameter 15 default
True
Parameter 15 description
Allow signoff reference files during signoffwithform operation. By default it is set to True, which means references files will be
shown during signoffwithform.
Parameter 15 type
Boolean
The SignOffWithForm method uses the SignOffWithForm client API to change the status of a document version using a dialog box where you can add additional notes.
SmartPlantReview client API
The SmartPlantReview client API is used to view files in Smart Review. This client API uses no parameters.
The following methods use the SmartPlantReview client API to update view files in Smart Review.
SmartPlantReview
FileSmartPlantReview

The SPFCopyConfigAccess client API is used to copy role assignments for all the users in an existing configuration to the same users in a different configuration.

Description

Configuration role assignment method

None

The UID of the configuration role assignment method

String
The SPFCopyConfigAccess method uses the SPFCopyConfigAccess client API to copy users' role assignments between configurations.

The SPFExchangeData client API allows you to export data and documents to CSV files.

Description

Report configuration name

None

Name of the report configuration to be used

Create load job and attach CSV files

Workflow to attach load job

SPFNContentExtractionFromFDWDocument
SPFNCreateMasterRevision client API
The SPFNCreateMasterRevision client API is used to create a new revision if there is more than 1 version on the revision.
This client API has no parameters and uses no methods.
SPFNCreateRelationshipsForSelectedDocuments client API
The SPFNCreateRelationshipsForSelectedDocuments client API is used to create or update the relationships for the selected documents from the document properties. This client API has no parameters.
The following method is used by this client API to create or update the relationships for the selected documents from the document properties.
SPFNCreateRelationships
SPFNSetAsMasterFile client API
The SPFNSetAsMasterFile client API is used to set the selected file as the Master file. This client API has no parameters.

SPFNSetAsMasterFile
SPFNTagClassification client API
The SPFNTagClassification client API is used to manage tag classification. This client API has no parameters.
The following method is used by this client API to manage tag classification..
SPFNTagClassification
SPFNViewFilesRelatedToTag client API
The SPFNViewFilesRelatedToTag client API is used to view all the files that are related to the selected tags.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Edge Def to latest document version
Parameter 1 description
Uses the Edge Definition to display the latest document version
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
None
The following methods is used by this client API to view all the files that are related to the selected tags.
SPFNViewFilesRelatedToAliasTag
SPFNViewFilesRelatedToMasterTag
StartScheduler client API
The StartScheduler client API is used to activate a selected scheduler. This client API uses no parameters.
The StartScheduler method uses the StartScheduler client API to activate a scheduler.
StepAcceptAction client API
The StepAcceptAction client API provides the functionality behind methods that progress a document or transmittal forward in a workflow. This client API uses no parameters.
The following methods use this client API to indicate that a step is complete or successful and the workflow should progress forward to the next step defined as the Accept Step.
ApproveCorrelations
WFStepAcknowledge
WFStepCompleted
WFStepApprove
WFStepRetry
StepClaim client API
The StepClaim client API provides the functionality that allows one user to claim a workflow step that has been assigned to a role or group of users.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Reset target dates
Parameter 1 description
When set to True, the duration period for the current step should be restarted from the point where the step was claimed and
target dates for the current and future steps should be recalculated accordingly. When set to False, the dates are not reset.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 1 default
True
Parameter 2
User rel
Parameter 2 description
User relationship definition
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 2 default
None
The following methods use this client API to claim a step for the user.
WFClaim
StepReAssign client API
The StepReAssign client API provides the ability in the Desktop Client to reassign a workflow step assigned to you. This client API uses no parameters.
The WFStepReAssign method in the Desktop Client uses the StepReAssign client API to reassign a workflow step from the current user to another user or role.
 If you want configuration changes you make here to be available in the Web Client, as well as the Desktop Client, you must make the same changes to the MultiStepReAssign client API.
StepRejectAction client API
The StepRejectAction client API provides the functionality to indicate that a workflow step failed or has been rejected and that the workflow should continue with the step defined as the Reject Step. This client API uses no parameters.
The following methods use this client API to indicate that a step was rejected or unsuccessful and that the workflow should continue to the step defined as the Reject Step.
WFStepUnableToComplete
WFStepReject
StepUpdateDates client API
The StepUpdateDates client API updates the duration length assigned to a step in a workflow, as well as the target and planned dates. This client API uses no parameters.
The StepUpdateDates method uses the StepUpdateDates client API to update the duration of a step.
StepUpdateDesc client API
The StepUpdateDesc client API updates the description provided for a step in a workflow. This client API uses no parameters.
The StepUpdateDesc method uses the StepUpdateDesc client API to update the description of a step.
StepUpdateRecip client API
The StepUpdateRecip client API updates the recipients associated with a workflow step from that step. This client API uses no parameters.
The StepUpdateRecip method uses the StepUpdateRecip client API to update the recipients associated with the workflow step.
StopScheduler client API
The StopScheduler client API is used to deactivate a selected scheduler. This client API uses no parameters.
The StopScheduler method uses the StopScheduler client API to deactivate a scheduler.
SummaryViewData client API
The SummaryViewData client API is used to view summary data about a selected shared object.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Graph Name
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The graph definition that will be used to navigate from the selected object to the result object.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
InterfaceDef UID
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The interface UID that is the starting point for the graph definition from which to expand to get the result objects.
Parameter 2 type
String
The following methods use the SummaryViewData client API to display a summary of information about a selected shared object.
SummaryViewDataDocVersion
SummaryViewDataEquip
SummaryViewDataInst
SummaryViewDataPipeLine
SummaryViewDataPipeRun
SummaryViewDataPipingComponent
SummaryViewDataProcessCase
SummaryViewDataSimulatedFacility
SummaryViewDataSimulatedModel
SummaryViewDataStream
TakeWorkflowOffHold client API
The TakeWorkflowOffHold client API restarts a workflow that was previously put on hold using a method based on the PutWorkflowOnHold client API. This client API uses no parameters.
The OffHold method uses the TakeWorkflowOffhold client API to restart a workflow at the same point at which it was paused.
TaskReassignmentConfiguration client API
The TaskReassignmentConfiguration client API is used to display configurations to which a user has access.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Columnset UID
Parameter 1 description
UID of the column set to be used to display the configurations
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
CS_UserDetails.
Parameter 2
Use default query configurations
Parameter 2 description
If ALL is set, then all configurations are displayed on the Task Reassignment page. If it is blank, only the selected query
configurations are displayed.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 2 default
None
The following method uses the TaskReassignmentConfiguration client API to determine if access needs to be checked across all configurations or selected configurations.
TaskReassignmentConfiguration
TEFConsolidateDocument client API
The TEFConsolidateDocument client API is used to initiate the consolidation of a published document that has already been loaded into the database.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Load into document config ONLY
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Indicate (True or False) whether the data loaded into the database from the published document only in the the configuration
in which the document resides.
Parameter 1 type
String
The following methods use the TEFConsolidateDocument client API to attempt to consolidate a published and loaded document.
TEFConsolidateDocument
TEFRetryConsolidate
TEFLaunchExternalSchemaApp client API
The TEFLaunchExternalSchemaApp client API is used to open a delivered CMF File Manager application provided to help you manage your schema, such as the CMF File Manager for PBS or CMF File Manager for XML
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
AppMode
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The mode in which to run the external application. Current support values include: AuthSchema and XML
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
OutputPath
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The path to which to output files generated by the schema application.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
ClassPrefix
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
The prefix to be used for class definitions created by the schema application.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
SourceCompSchema
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
The name of the source component schema to be used, such as the PBSComponent component schema.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
TargetCompSchema
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
The name of the target component schema to be created, such as the PBSAuthoringComponent component schema.
Parameter 5 type
String
The following methods use the TEFLaunchExternalSchemaApp client API to open the applicable CMF File Manager application.
GenerateNewAuthoringSchema
GenerateNewPBSSchema
GenerateXMLSchema
TEFLaunchSchemaEditor client API
The TEFLaunchSchemaEditor client API opens the selected schema document file in the Schema Editor application. This client API uses no parameters.
The TEFLaunchSchemaEditor method uses the TEFLaunchSchemaEditor client API to open the file attached to the schema document in the Schema Editor application for editing.
TEFLoadPublishData client API
The TEFLoadPublishData client API is used to load published data attached to the document into the database.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Load into document config Only
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Indicate (True or False) whether the data loaded into the database from the published document should be loaded only into
the configuration in which the document resides.
Parameter 1 type
String
The following methods use the TEFLoadPublishData client API to load published data.
TEFLoadPublishedData
TEFLoadPublishedDataIntoConfig
TEFRetryLoadPublishedData
TEFMergeDelta client API
The TEFMergeDelta client API is used to merge 3D graphic elements for published documents or to resubmit a merge process to the queue. This client API uses no parameters.
The following methods use the TEFMergeDelta client API to submit or resubmit a merge process to the queue.
TEFMergeDelta
TEFRetryMergeDelta
TEFPublishDatasheet client API
The TEFPublishDatasheet client API is used to create method for generating a new datasheet.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Datasheet type
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Indicate the type of datasheet being created, such as IPD, InstrumentList, or EquipmentList
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Datasheet Def UID
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The UID of the datasheet definition used to create the datasheet.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Tool Adapter Prog ID
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
The tool adapter prog ID used in the method argument.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
Domain
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
The domain UID of the authoring tool used in the method argument.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
Adapter Code
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
Adapter code or document type that is used to identify the map file.
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
Database Code
Parameter 6 default
None
Parameter 6 description
The database code ID that can be used for the data warehouse.
Parameter 6 type
String
The TEFPublishDatasheetIPD method uses the TEFPublishDatasheet client API to generate a datasheet.
TEFReApplySameAsObjs client API
The TEFReApplySameAsObjs client API allows you to reapply Same As relationships for published data to ensure the order of SameAs relationships. This client API uses no parameters.
The TEFReApplySameAsObjs method uses the TEFReApplySameAsObjs client API to reapply the same as relationships to published data.
TEFSelectCorrelations client API
The TEFSelectCorrelations client API allows you to select correlations for the published objects as part of a workflow attached to a published document. This client API uses no parameters.
The SelectCorrelations method uses the TEFSelectCorrelations client API to select correlations for published objects.
TEFShowFailure client API
The TEFShowFailure client API shows the error associated with a published document version. This client API uses no parameters.
The TEFShowFailure method uses the TEFShowFailure client API to show the publish failure message associated with a document version.
TerminateObj client API
The TerminateObj client API terminates the selected object. This client API uses no parameters.
 Many methods use this client API, and the list can be viewed in the SmartPlant Foundation. To see the methods in your system that use this client API, find the client API, and select Show Methods.
TerminateWorkflow client API
The TerminateWorkflow client API is used to terminate the relationship between a document or transmittal and an instance of a workflow definition. All history pertaining to work already completed is retained in the database. However, the
relationship is marked terminated and is therefore no longer valid from the point of termination forward.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Comments mandatory
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
Specifies whether comments must be provided in the dialog box provided to the user before they can complete the terminate
operation.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
The TerminateWorkflow method uses the TerminateWorkflow client API to terminate the relationship between the selected document or transmittal and an attached instance of a workflow definition.
ToolVersionGraphicCompare client API
The ToolVersionGraphicCompare client API is used to compare a tool document to a published document, graphically.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Tool data file
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The path to the tool data file that will be used in the comparison.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Tool view file
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The path to the tool view file that will be used in the comparison.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Display mode
Parameter 3 default
FRAME
Parameter 3 description
If set to OPEN, the comparison dialog box will open in a separate browser window. If set to FRAME, the comparison dialog
box will open in an frame within the Desktop Client.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
Include identical properties
Parameter 4 default
TRUE
Parameter 4 description
If set to TRUE, all properties will be included in the comparison, even properties with identical data between the two
documents. If set to False, only properties with different values between the two documents will be included in the
comparison.
Parameter 4 type
Boolean
No delivered methods use the ToolVersionGraphicCompare client API.
UnCancelDocument client API
The UnCancelDocument client API is used to revert the document to the state it was in when it was cancelled. This client API uses no parameters.
The UnCancelDocument method uses this client API to reverse the cancellation of the document in a workflow and return it to the previous state.
UnClaimObj client API
The UnClaimObj client API is used to remove the claim of an object in a project or lower-level configuration.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Column set to present results
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The name of the column set that will be used to display the results.
Parameter 1 type
String
The UnClaimObj method uses the ClaimObj client API to claim an object down into a lower-level configuration for editing.
UndoCheckOut client API
The UndoCheckOut client API is used to cancel a checkout operation. The new version create during checkout is deleted and the previous version, which had been marked superseded, is changed to working and marked checked in.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Get checked out versions
Parameter 1 default
True
Parameter 1 description
Allows you to update nested reference files to the latest version.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 15
Allow references
Parameter 15 default
True
Parameter 15 description
Allow reference files during undocheckout operation. By default it is set to True, which means references files will be shown
for undocheckout
Parameter 15 type
Boolean
The UndoCheckOut method uses the UndoCheckOut client API to cancel a previous check out operation.
UndoEditFiles client API
The UndoEditFiles client API is used to revert the editing on the files.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Delete Revision
Parameter 1 description
Options are YES, NO, or PROMPT.
YES – If multiple versions exist for a revision, then it deletes all versions along with the revision.
NO – If multiple versions exist for a revision, then it deletes latest version.
PROMPT – Prompts the user whether to delete the revision or not.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
PROMPT
 You do not see the Undo Edit Files action for any document revision with only one version, as this action is controlled by a condition in the method so the system will not delete the revision.
The following methods use the UndoEditFiles client API to revert the editing on the files.
UndoEditFiles
InvalidUndoEditFiles
UndoSignOff client API
The UndoSignOff client API allows you to change a signed off document back to the Working state. This client API uses no parameters.
The UndoSignOff method uses the client API to revert a signed off document to the Working state.
UnlockMarkup client API
The UnlockMarkup client API is used to control user access to the commands to unlock markup layers. This client API uses no parameters.
The following methods use the UnlockMarkup client API to unlock markups layers.
UnlockFileMarkups
UnlockMarkupFile
UnlockMarkups
UnlockUserAccount client API
The UnlockUserAccount client API allows administrators to unlock a temporarily locked user account when it has been locked for a period of time after a number of failed log on attempts. This client API uses no parameters.
The UsrAdmUnlock method uses the UnlockUserAccount client API to unlock the user account.
UpdateGraphicsMapFiles client API
The UpdateGraphicsMapFiles client API is used to update graphics map files that include objects to support viewing items in drawings.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Graphics map file criteria
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Criteria to filter graphics map files. If no value is provided, all files will be updated.
Example: +SPFFIleComposition~IObject~Name~=~1409* can be used to update all the files attached to documents with
names that begin with "1409".
Parameter 1 type
String
The UpdateGraphicsMapFiles method uses the UpdateGraphicsMapFiles client API to update older WebGL files so that they will display correctly.
UpdateMarkup client API
The UpdateMarkup client API is used to control user access to the commands for editing existing markup layers. This client API uses no parameters.
The UpdateMarkup method uses the UpdateMarkup client API to enable users to edit existing markup layers.
ViewConsolidatedDataForObject client API
The ViewConsolidatedDataForObject client API is used to view the data in the CDW domain for a consolidated object. This client API uses no parameters.
The ViewConsolidatedDataForObject method uses the ViewConsolidatedDataForObject client API to display information about a selected consolidated object.
UpdateReferences client API
The UpdateReferences client API is used to manage the version of referenced files.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Column set to present data
Parameter 1 default
CS_UpdateRefsColumnSet
Parameter 1 description
Column set used to display items in the Update References dialog box.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Column item for target references
Parameter 2 default
CI_TargetReferencesRev
Parameter 2 description
The editable column item for selecting the target reference document. Allows you to specify whether you want to maintain a
relationship to the latest working version of a reference file, the current version, or the original version to which the relationship
was initially created.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Skip Max Nested Level Validation
Parameter 3 default
False
Parameter 3 description
When set to True, the maximum nested reference level validation is skipped.
Parameter 3 type
Boolean
The following methods use the UpdateReferences client API to manage what version of references file a document is related to.
FileUpdateReferences
UpdateReferences
UpdateReferredBy client API
The UpdateReferredBy client API is used to update out-of-date referred by relationships.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Column set to present data
Parameter 1 default
CS_UpdateRefsColumnSet
Parameter 1 description
Column set used to display items in the Update References dialog box.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Column item for target references
Parameter 2 default
CI_TargetReferencesRev
Parameter 2 description
The editable column item for selecting the target reference document.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Skip Max Nested Level Validation
Parameter 3 default
False
Parameter 3 description
Configuration to skip max nested reference level validation
Parameter 3 type
Boolean
The following methods use the UpdateReferences client API to manage what version of references file a document is related to.
FileUpdateReferredBy
UpdateReferredBy
UpgradeDataCaptureTagDocRels client API
The UpgradeDataCaptureTagDocRels client API is used to update the interface definition property Is Data Capture Rel to True for the Data Capture relationships created between documents and tags. This client API uses no
parameters.
The UpgradeDataCaptureTagDocRels method uses the UpgradeDataCaptureTagDocRels client API to update the document to tag relationships created by the Data Capture.
UploadDocument client API
The UploadDocument client API is used to query for the existing document, retrieve the metadata of the selected document, create a new revision of that document, attach any selected files to this new revision, and upload the document
along with the files.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Title
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The title that appears at the top of the dialog box.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Entrypoint set name to query the documents
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
Entrypoint set name to query the documents. For more information on how to configure the entry point, see Configure API
Entry Points.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
File attachment configuration
Parameter 3 default
SPFDesignFile~SPFFileComposition~PROMPT
Parameter 3 description
File attachment configuration (optional) FileClassDef~FileRelDef~ReferenceAction~SeedFileTemplateDocName.
SPFFileComposition is the default relationship definition if none specified and the seed file document is optional. Reference
file not found actions are CONTINUE | PROMPT | ABORT
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
RelDef to navigate to the ClassDef
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
RelDef to navigate to the ClassDef - Used to derive the class definition to create from the classification object.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
UID of classification
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
UID of classification
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 8
Workflow processing option
Parameter 8 default
None
Parameter 8 description
The Web Client chooses the applicable workflow unless a named workflow is specified. However, if more than one applicable
workflow is found, the software does not attach the object to any workflow. The options include Select, SelectRequired, or a
named workflow.
Parameter 8 type
String
The following method uses the UploadDocument client API to query for the existing document based on the given arguments.
UploadDocument
UserPreference client API
The UserPreference client API is used to open the Change User Preferences dialog box. This client API uses no parameters.
The UserPreference method uses the UserPreference client API to open the Change User Preferences dialog box.
ValidateConditions client API
The ValidateConditions client API is used to validate all conditions in the database. This client API uses no parameters.
The following method is used by the ValidateConditions client API to validate all conditions in the database.
ValidateConditions
ValidateSPFSite client API
The ValidateSPFSite client API is used to validate a SmartPlant Foundation site connection when you add it to the SmartPlant Foundation Log In page. This client API uses no parameters.
The ValidateSPFSite method uses the ValidateSPFSite client API to validate a SmartPlant Foundation site.
ViewAdhocQuery client API
The ViewAdhocQuery client API is used to run a saved query. This client API uses no parameters.
The ViewAdhocQuery method uses the ViewAdhocQuery client API to run a saved query.
ViewBatchRenditionLog client API
The ViewBatchRenditionLog client API is used to configure viewing of an object, such as the batch renditions log file or a merged PDF file.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
RelDef/EdgeDef UID
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The relationship definition/ edge definition UID to the object to be viewed. For example,
+SPFNotificationBatchRenditionLogFile or -SPFMergedRenditionComposition.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
ColumnSet
Parameter 2 default
CS_SPFObject
Parameter 2 description
Name of the column set used to display the properties of viewable objects if the RelDef/EdgeDef UID parameter returns more
than one object. If not set, the default is CS_SPFObject.
Parameter 2 type
None
The following methods use the ViewBatchRenditionLog client API to view a batch rendition log or a merged rendition.
ViewBatchRenditionLog
ViewMergedRendition

View Native method for item with attached files

None

View and Markup method for item with attached files

None

View Native method for a file item
Parameter 3 default
None

String

View and Markup method for a file item

None

UID of the method for View and Markup functionality directly from a file
Parameter 4 type
String

View Alternate method for a file item

None

UID of the method for View Alternate functionality directly from a file

String

View laser-scan site method

None

String
Parameter 7
View laser-scan scan method

UID of the method for View Laser-Scan Scan functionality

String
The ViewFileDefault method uses the ViewFileWrapper client API to call the view file methods in the correct order when assigned to an object type as the default method in the Web Client.
ViewForm client API
The ViewForm client API is used display a tabbed dialog box where each tab displays a different purpose for the form. This dialog box allows you to see what dialog boxes will look like when creating, updating, querying, or viewing the
details of object that use the selected form. This client API uses no parameters.
The FormView method uses the ViewForm client API to open the dialog box with the purpose tabs.

Description

Versions EdgeDef

The edge definition used to find the latest drawing version in which the selected item was published.

String
The ViewInDrawing method uses the ViewInDrawing client API to display the selected 2D drawing item in the drawing in which it was published.

Document item to model EdgeDef

None

The document item used to model the Edge Definition

String

Document item to model and composite Graph

None

The document item used to model and composite the Graph Definition

String

Use GraphDef

False

The Graph Definition to be used

Boolean

ViewPipeRunIn3DModel
ViewLookAhead client API
The ViewLookAhead client API is used to toggle on or off the Look Ahead feature. This client API uses no parameters.
The ViewLookAhead method uses the ViewLookAhead client API to toggle off or on the Look Ahead feature.

The ViewOPC client API is used to view a selected off-page connector (OPC) in a published drawing.
Argument
Description

Versions EdgeDef

None

String

The ViewRelatedItems client API is used to show items that are related to the selected file or document.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Relationship List
Parameter 1 description
A comma separated list of Relationship Definitions (preceded with +/-) or Edge Definitions to be displayed. If left blank, all
definitions that the user has access to will be exposed off with a right-click.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
None
The following methods are used by this client API to show items that are related to the selected file or document.
FDWDOCViewRelatedItems
SDAViewTransmittalDetails
SPFNViewChildContentDiscoveryTaskDetails
SPFNDocumentsRelatedToChildTag
SPFNDocumentsRelatedToDocumentDiscoveryTask
SPFNViewDocumentsRelatedToMasterTag
SPFNViewParentContentDiscoveryTaskDetails

ViewRelatedItems

The ViewRelationshipObjects client API is used to display a list of all the relationships the selected object participates in.

Description

Relationship column set
Parameter 1 default
None

The column set used to display the relationships returned in the list view.

String
The ViewRelObjects method uses the ViewRelationshipObjects client API to return a list of relationship definitions where the selected object is at one end.
ViewSharedData client API
The ViewSharedData client API is used to show data from various domains for shared objects.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Graph Name
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The name of the graph definition used to gather properties of the selected shared object for viewing.

String

ViewSharedDataEquip

ViewSharedDataPipeLine
ViewSharedDataPipeRun

ViewSharedProcessCaseData
ViewSharedSimulatedFacility
ViewSharedSimulatedModel
VTLDeleteJob client API
The VTLDeleteJob client API is used to control permission to delete jobs created by other users. This client API uses no parameters.
The VTLDeleteJob method uses this client API to control permission to delete jobs in the Data Validator Job Management module.
 Only the users granted access to this method can delete jobs created by others.
VTLViewAllJobs client API
The VTLViewAllJobs client API is used to control permission to view jobs created by other users. This client API uses no parameters.
The VTLViewAllJobs method uses this client API to control permission to view jobs created by other users in the Data Validator Job Management module.
 Only the users granted access to this method can view jobs created by others.
WebClientExtensibility client API
The WebClientExtensibility client API is used to invoke custom pages inside the Web Client frame.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
URL to custom page
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The full URL to a custom page. May be on a separate or remote server
Parameter 1 type
String
No delivered methods use the WebClientExtensibility client API.
WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API
The WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API is used to perform an extensibility action against multiple objects.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
URL to multiple objects
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The full URL to multiple objects. May be on a separate or remote server
Parameter 1 type
String
No delivered methods use the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API.
WFDoExpirationChecks client API
The WFDoExpirationChecks client API searches for all steps that are overdue and compares their dates against the system options. If the step has been overdue for longer than the reassignment period defined in the system options, the
step is reassigned to the user or role defined on the workflow as the recipient for reassignment. If the step has exceeded the overdue period, the system will send a notification, based on the value defined for the Number of days between
emails setting. This client API uses no parameters.
The WFDoExpirationChecks method uses the WFDoExpirationChecks client API to search for overdue steps.
WorkflowDetailsReport client API
The WorkflowDetailsReport client API allows you to create methods that will run a report to display information about each step in a workflow.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
ViewDef name
Parameter 1 description
Specifies the name of the view definition.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 2
Output type
Parameter 2 description
Specifies the format in which the results of the report will be displayed. The only available option is XHTML.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 3
Template name
Parameter 3 description
Specifies the name of the template file, stored in SmartPlant Foundation, where the results must be loaded.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 3 default
None
The WorkflowDetailsReport method uses this client API to run a report with detailed information about each step of the workflow attached to the selected object.
When updating the WorkflowDetailsReport method, ensure that the following input parameters remain unchanged:
Condition: HasWorkflowHistory
Argument 1: WorkflowDetails
Argument 2: XHTML
Argument 3: Workflow Details Report Template
WorkflowEdit client API
The WorkflowEdit client API provides the functionality to view or edit the details of a workflow definition. Methods that use this client API open the dialog box that displays information about the workflow, such as names, durations, and
recipients of each step and the accept and reject paths. The information on the dialog box may be editable depending on the Mode parameter setting on the method used to open it.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Mode
Parameter 1 description
Specifies whether the dialog box that appears to the user will be editable or read-only. The available options are Edit, which
indicates that the dialog box will be editable, and Info, which indicates that the information in the dialog box will be displayed
in a read-only format.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
Info
The following delivered methods use this client API to open the dialog box for viewing or editing information about the attached workflow definition instance.
CreateWorkflowTemplate
ObjectWorkflowDetail
ObjectWorkflowEdit
StepWorkflowDetail
StepWorkflowEdit
WorkflowEditShortcut
WorkflowEdit
WorkflowDetail
WorkflowHistory client API
The WorkflowHistory client API provides the functionality to view a list of the workflows with which a document or transmittal has been associated and then choose one of those to view graphically with details about the history of each
step.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Column set
Parameter 1 description
Specify the name of the column set that will be used to display the list of all workflow definitions associated with the selected
document or transmittal.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
None
The WorkflowHistory method uses the WorkflowHistory client API to view a list of the workflows the selected object has been associated with.
XmlDiff client API
The XmlDiff client API is used to open the Compare XML Files dialog box where you can choose two XML files to compare. This client API uses no parameters.
The XmlDiff method uses the XmlDiff client API to open the Compare XML Files dialog box.
XmtlCancel client API
The XmtlCancel client API is used to cancel the transmittals attached to the workflow.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Not used
Parameter 2
Not used
Parameter 3
Not used
Parameter 4
Name of cancel workflow
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
Name of the workflow the cancelled transmittal is attached to.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
Issue states to cancel and revise
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
This argument takes list of transmittal issue states to cancel and revise with a new workflow. Multiple issue states can be
specified by comma separated values, such as ISSUED,COMPLETED. Transmittals with other issue states that are not
specified will only be cancelled.
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
Workflow Step Definition Name
Parameter 6 default
None
Parameter 6 description
Specifies the workflow step definition to identify the workflow step in the current workflow. The canceled workflow is added to
this workflow step.
Parameter 6 type
String
The following methods use the XmtlCancel client API to cancel the workflow an object is attached to.
XmtlCancelExt
XmtlCancelInt
SCLBCancelIntAction
XmtlMakeTemplate client API
The XmtlMakeTemplate client API converts a transmittal to a transmittal template that can then be reused to create additional transmittals later. This client API uses no parameters.
The XmtlStoreAsTemplate method uses the XmtlMakeTemplate client API to convert a transmittal to a transmittal template.
XmtlSetPlannedDate client API
The XmtlSetPlannedDate client API sets a planned date for a transmittal. This client API uses no parameters.
The XmtlSetPlannedDate method uses the XmtlSetPlannedDate client API to set the planned date for a selected transmittal.
XmtlSetPropToRecip client API
The XmtlSetPropToRecip client API sets a controlling recipient for a transmittal. This client API uses no parameters.
The XmtlSetControllingRecip method uses the XmtlSetPropToRecip client API to set a controlling recipient for a selected transmittal.
XmtlUndoCancel client API
The XmtlUndoCancel client API is used to revert the transmittal to the state it was in before it was canceled.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Not used
Parameter 2
Not used
Parameter 3
Not used
Parameter 4
Name of the Undo Cancel workflow
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
Name of the workflow the previously canceled transmittal is attached to when the cancel is undone.
Parameter 4 type
String
 The client API cannot be used in the Desktop Client.
XmtlUpdateRecip client API
The XmtlUpdateRecip client API updates the recipients assigned to a transmittal. This client API uses no parameters.
The XmtlUpdateRecip method uses the XmtlUpdateRecip client API to update the recipients assigned to a selected transmittal.
Dialog Boxes and Commands
The following sections includes descriptions of dialog boxes and commands that are part of SmartPlant Foundation.
Accept Replan command
The Accept Replan command replaces the plan and construction dates with those provided for replan and replan construction. This command is available from the Replan submenu on the Progress menu.
When a replan is accepted, the following changes are made to the workpack details:
The Plan Start and Plan End dates for the workpack are replaced with the Replan Start and Replan End dates.
The Construction Start and Construction End dates are replaced with the Replan Construction Start and Replan Construction End dates.
The Replan Start, Replan End, Replan Construction Start, and Replan Construction End date fields on the Plan Details tab of the Design Workpack Create/Update Form dialog box are cleared.
For each deliverable in the workpack, the following changes are made:
The plan dates for each step are replaced with the replan dates.
The forecast dates and replan dates for each step are cleared.
The Actual dates for each step are not affected.
If a deliverables new plan dates do not correspond to an available timestring, a new timestring will be created and related to it by the Accept Replan process. The new timestring will also be available on the appropriate workpack. It is
named with a prefix of "RP" and an incremental number.
Add Step dialog box
Allows you to add a step that will be part of the lifecycle of the deliverables associated with a workpack.
This dialog box appears when you click either the Before or After button on the Steps tab of either of the following dialog boxes:
New Design Workpack Template dialog box
New Design Workpack dialog box
Name - Specifies the name of the new step.
Description - Provides a brief description of the step.
Weighting - Indicates the allocated weighting of the production step. This value is a percentage, and the sum of all of the weightings for all of the steps in a workpack must equal 100.
Construction Step - Specify whether this step must be completed during the construction period (between the Construction Start and Construction End dates).
Events - Opens the Step Events dialog box, where you can define events that constitute all or some part of this step.
Activate dialog box
Used to activate reserved vendor documents, creating the first revision and version.
Name - Displays the name of the vendor document to activate.
Description - Displays an editable description of the vendor document to activate.
Title - Displays an editable title for the document.
Status - Displays the status of the vendor document. This option is display only.
Item owning group - Selects the owning group of the document.
Assign Recipients dialog box
Allows you to create the matrix of users that will be assigned as recipients to a workflow step, based on the defined conditions.
User List - Displays the list of users or roles available for assignment.
Add - Allows you to assign the selected users or roles to the distribution matrix.
Remove - Allows you to remove the current users or roles assigned to the distribution matrix.
Property columns - Displays the properties assigned to the distribution matrix. These properties are set by the system administrator according to project requirements. Typical properties include document type, contract, project, or area.
 You can use the asterisk (*) wildcard character, alone or with other characters, to associate a row with multiple values of a property. For example, if you specify *process* for the document category property, the row of the matrix
will be applicable for any document category that contains process, such as Process Documents or Process Controlled Documents.
User or Role columns - Displays the users or roles assigned to the transmittal matrix. The cells in these columns determine the actions required from the users or roles that receive transmittals for documents as determined by the
property columns. For users who receive paper documents, the cells in these columns also determine the paper format used to print the documents attached to the transmittals. The default reasons for receipt in the delivered SmartPlant
Foundation model are I (information), C (comment), and W (work). These cells are color coded according to the color palettes on the dialog box.
Context menus - Right-click items in the matrix view to display a context menu that allows you to manipulate the items in the view. Some commands are available on more than one context menu.
Column headings
The following commands are available from the context menu when you right-click on a column header in the matrix.
Move Column - Opens the Move Column dialog box where you can adjust the placement of the selected user columns.
Add Column - Opens the Add Column dialog box, which allows you to add a user or role column. You must type a valid user or role name to add the new column.
Duplicate Column - Opens the Duplicate Column dialog box where you can add a new user or role column with the same reasons for receipt as the column being duplicated. You must type a valid user or role name to add the new
column.
Clear Column - Removes all reasons for receipt from the column.
Delete Column - Removes the user or role from the display.
Change Recipient Name – Opens the Change Recipient Name dialog box, where you can provide the name of a user or role that for which you want associate the selected column, in place of the current user or role.
Row headings
The following commands are available from the context menu when you right-click on a row header in the matrix.
Duplicate Row - Adds a new row with the same properties and reasons for receipt as the selected row.
Delete Row - Removes the row from the matrix.
Individual cells
The following commands are available from the context menu when you right-click on an individual cell in the matrix in a column under a user name or role.
Reason for Receipt and Format - Opens the Reason for Receipt and Format dialog box, where you can select the reason for receipt and paper format for the user or role. Reasons for receipt are defined in the SmartPlant
Foundation Desktop Client by the system administrator.
Cut - Removes the information from the cell and places it on the clipboard.
Copy - Places a copy of the contents of the cell on the clipboard.
Paste - Writes information from the clipboard into the cell.
Delete - Removes the contents of the cell without writing the information to the clipboard.
Duplicate Row - Adds a new row with the same properties and reasons for receipt as the selected row.
Delete Row - Removes the row from the matrix.
Move Column - Opens the Move Column dialog box where you can adjusts the placement of the selected user columns.
Add Column - Opens the Add Column dialog box, which allows you to add a user or role column. You must type a valid user or role name to add the new column.
Duplicate Column - Opens the Duplicate Column dialog box where you can add a new user or role column with the same reasons for receipt as the column being duplicated. You must type a valid user or role name to add the new
column.
Clear Column - Removes all reasons for receipt from the column.
Delete Column - Removes the user or role column from the matrix.
Change Recipient Name - Opens the Change Recipient Name dialog box, where you can provide the name of a user or role that for which you want associate the selected column, in place of the current user or role.
Export to Clipboard - Places a copy of the matrix on the Clipboard, which allows you to paste the data into Microsoft Excel or other programs.
Import from Clipboard - Imports Clipboard data that was placed there from Microsoft Excel.
Matrix view outside matrix cells
The following commands appear on the context menu when you right-click in any unused portion of the matrix window.
Add Column -Opens the Add Column dialog box, which allows you to add a user or role column. You must type a valid user or role name to add the new column.
Export to Clipboard - Places a copy of the matrix on the clipboard, which allows you to paste the data into Microsoft Excel.
Import from Clipboard - Imports clipboard data that was placed there from Microsoft Excel.
Color palettes - Allows you to customize the color codes for the various cell types in the matrix view.
Default format - Specifies the paper format automatically assigned to newly added paper users. While this value appears in the new cell by default, it can be changed by right-clicking in the cell and using the Reason for Receipt
and Format command.
Default reason for receipt - Specifies the reason for receipt automatically assigned to newly added users or roles. Reasons for receipt are defined in the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client by the system administrator. While this
value appears in the new cell by default, it can be changed by right-clicking in the cell and using the Reason for Receipt and Format command.
Size columns for best fit - Resizes the columns in the matrix view.
User - Allows you to search for users to assign to the distribution matrix.
Role - Allows you to search for roles to assign to the distribution matrix.
Assign Recipients dialog box
Allows you to specify the users or roles included in the selected distribution matrix, as well as the action required from each user or role upon receipt of a transmittal. For users or roles who receive paper copies of transmittals, you can also
specify the page size for transmittals.
User List - Displays the list of users or roles available for assignment to the step.
Add - Allows you to assign the selected users or roles to the step.
Remove - Allows you to remove the current users or roles assigned to the step.
Matrix pane - Displays columns for assigned recipients. New columns are automatically populated with the default Reason for Receipt and Format values (if set).
You can also drag and drop a recipient into the matrix pane.
If an internal matrix is selected, the pane will display two columns (Doc category and Contract).
Right-click a column to display the shortcut menu options:
Move Column - Adjusts the order in which the user or role columns display.
Add Column - Allows you to add a user or role column. You must type a valid user or group name to add the new column.
Duplicate Column - Allows you to add a new user or role column with the same reasons for receipt as the column being duplicated.
Clear Column - Removes all reasons for receipt from the column.
Delete Column - Removes the user or role from the display.
Change Recipient - Allows you to change the recipient.
Right-click a row to display the shortcut menu options:
Duplicate Row - Adds a new row with the same properties and reasons for receipt as the row being duplicated.
Delete Row - Removes the row from the display.
Color palettes - Allows you to customize the cell colors.
Default format - Specifies the paper format automatically assigned to newly added paper users.
