Value - Displays the values that are set for the object or the name of the object on the other end of a relationship.
Source Domain UID - Displays the unique identifier for the source domain of the relationship.
Creation Date - Displays the date the value was provided or the relationship was created.
Creation User - Displays the user that provided the value or created the relationship.
Termination Date - Displays the date the value was replaced or the relationship was terminated.
Termination User - Displays the user that updated the value or terminated the relationship.
Export to Excel - Exports the information displayed in the Property data table to a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet.
Print - Prints the history displayed in the Property data table.
Inconsistency Report Options dialog box
Allows you to set options for the inconsistency report.
Domain(s) to compare - Allows you to select one or more domains from which to include data for comparison.
Inconsistent properties only - Displays only the properties where inconsistent data was found. If this option is not selected, then the report includes all the properties for the object including the properties with consistent data across the
domains.
Inconsistent objects only - Displays only objects with inconsistent properties. If this option is not selected, the report includes objects for which the data is consistent across the domains.
Include only mapped CDW properties - Displays only the properties that have been mapped to CDW domain properties. If this option is not selected, properties that have not been mapped to the CDW domain are included in the report.
Freeze Panes – Allows you to lock the rows and columns that contain overview information, column headers, and the first two columns of information about the objects.
View in Portrait orientation – Allows you to view the report in portrait orientation where the object data is displayed as separate rows. This is the default option. If you clear this option, the report is displayed in landscape orientation, where
the object data is displayed as separate columns.
Categorize by class definition (per worksheet) – Organizes the objects in the report by class definition and displays each class definition in a different worksheet. If this option is not selected, all the objects are displayed in a single
worksheet.
Show consolidated name and description for each property – Displays the name and description of the consolidated object for each property in the report results. The consolidated name and description are created after the data
published from the tools has been consolidated in the SmartPlant Foundation database. If this option is not selected, the name and description of the consolidated object is shown only once for each object in the report results.
 When you apply filters to one or more properties, the consolidated name and description of the object always appear along with the filtered properties, making it easier to identify the object each property is related to.
Initiate Replan command
The Initiate Replan command places all deliverables in all affected workpacks in the Replan mode. This command is available from the Replan submenu on the Progress menu.
Initiate Replan dialog box
Allows you to initiate a replan for any level in the hierarchy, down to individual workpacks. All deliverables in each affected workpack are put into Replan mode, meaning that only the Replan dates can be modified.
The default replan dates are based upon the Actual dates provided for steps. If an Actual date does not exist for a step, the replan date will be based on the forecast date of the step. If a step has no forecast date, the replan date is based
on the plan date. 
These dates must fall within their Replan Start and End dates for the workpack.
Contract - Specify the contract.
Area - Specify the top level of the hierarchy for which you want to initiate a replan.
Unit - Specify the second level of the hierarchy for which you want to initiate a replan.
Internal Matrix command
The File > New > Internal Matrix command allows you to create a new distribution matrix for internal transmittals.
List Edit Options dialog box
If multiple List Edit windows are available for this item type, you must choose which window to open.
Name - Choose an available configuration of the List Edit window to open.
List Edit UOM dialog box
Select a unit of measure (UoM). You can also enter the first letters of the UoM to jump to the UoM in the list.
The Unit of Measure dialog box converts the value you enter to the UoM you select.
If a default UOM has been set for this column, that UoM will already be selected when the UoM dialog box appears.
List Edit window
 When selecting or clearing a check box in the List Edit window, you must first click in the cell to select it and then click the cell again to select or clear the option.
Save List As New [Object] - Saves the list as a new list document if configured by your administrator.
Save List to Existing [Object] - Saves the list as an existing list document if configured by your administrator.
List Edit window (with SaveAs)
The list editing functionality can be configured to save and manage a list of objects as a document. The list is configured to display any number of instances of some type of object, along with a specified set of properties of that object. From
this list, view in the List Edit window, you can view the list and data, modify the data of existing objects, create new objects, or terminate or delete object, depending on your configuration and your user permissions. In addition to features
for managing object data, this environment may include two additional buttons for saving the list as a new list document in the database or for attaching the list to an existing list document
Once you have customized the list of items that are displayed in the List Edit window, you can save the list to a list document. You can create a new list document or attach the list to an existing document.
See List Edit window for details about using the List Edit window.
 The type of list document available varies based on how your administrator has configured your system. The name of the type of list document to which your list is attached is reflected in the option on the List Edit window.
If you have changed the list in the List Edit window, you are prompted to save those changes before attaching the list to a list document.
List Management Console dialog box
Interface Definitions
Select an interface definition to configure list editing.
Interfaces that have List Editing - Interfaces that already have at least one method configured for list editing with the List Edit control.
Additional Interfaces - Interfaces that are not already configured for list management, but are available for list management configuration.
Lists
Name - The name of the list.
Description - The description of the list.
Cascade Name - The name of the cascading menu, if any, that contains the command than launches this list.
Column Set - The column set used for this list in the List Edit control.
Related Access Groups - The access groups allowed to access the list.
Related Interfaces - The interfaces on which this list can be used.
Create - Create a new list for the interface definition selected.
Edit - Update the selected list. This opens the Edit wizard, which allows you to edit the method, the column set and the access groups for the list.
Copy - Copy an existing list. This opens the Copy wizard, which allows you to create a new list using the selected list as a template. You can also configure the column set and access groups for the new list.
Delete - Delete a list.
Edit Method Access Groups - Click to manage the access groups for the selected list. This will open the Manage Related Items form for access groups.
Column Items
Used to specify the behavior of columns that will appear in the List Edit window for the selected interface definition. Each column that will appear in the List Edit window is represented by a row in the Column Items section.
Column Item - The item in the column set which will appear in the List Edit window and the List View window.
Read Only - When selected, the column will be read-only.
Mandatory - When selected, the column will be mandatory. When a new object is created in the List Edit window, a value must be entered in this column.
Disable Edit- When selected, this column will not appear in the List Edit window.
Disable View - When selected, this column not will appear in the Desktop Client List View window.
Advanced Mode
The following features are only available in advanced mode. See List Management Console advanced mode for more information.
Edit Interfaces - Click to manage the interfaces that will use the selected list. This will open the Manage Related Items dialog box for interface definitions.
Realized Interfaces
The advanced mode of the List Management Console allows system administrators to configure lists for all interfaces realized by a class definition, not just the interfaces to which they have access to an update method.
Class Definition - Enter a class definition to view all the interfaces it realizes.
Go - Click to begin the search for the class definition's realized interfaces.
Live and Terminated Objects dialog box
Allows you search for an active and terminated objects and revive terminated objects back to an active status in the database. Once you run the query, the search results are displayed in a dialog box named for the type of object queries,
for example, Live and terminated DEVTags.
Terminated and active objects pane
Name - The name of the active or terminated object.
Description - The description of the active or terminated object in text.
Config - The configuration from which the active or terminated object is located.
Termination Date - The date the object was terminated.
Termination User - The user who terminated the object.
Creation Date - The date the object was created.
Creation User - The user who created the object.
Properties grid pane
Property - The type of active or terminated property.
Value - The value of the active or terminated property.
Creation Date - The date the active or terminated property was created.
Termination Date - The date the active or terminated property was terminated.
Loader dialog box
Allows you to load data into SmartPlant Foundation using structured files containing the input data.
Directory - Allows you to select the files for processing. Click 
 to browse to the files to load.
Selected Load files - Lists the names of the files to be loaded.
Move Selected Item Up - Moves the selected file up one position. Click 
 to move the selected file.
Move Selected Item Down - Moves the selected file down one position. Click 
 to move the selected file.
Process Load Files
Name - Displays the name of the load file.
Container - Displays the container to which objects are related.
Created objects - Displays the number of objects created.
Updated objects - Displays the number of objects updated.
Processed instructions - Displays the number of process instructions executed.
Unchanged objects - Displays the number of objects that are unchanged.
Failed objects - Displays the number of objects that failed to load.
Process Time - Displays the amount of time to load the file.
 The process time value indicates the server load time only. The actual time spent processing is often higher than this value.
Put objects in new items window - Displays new objects in the New Items window.
Process - Processes all files in the Selected load files list.
Load Results - Displays the results of the processed load files in the Load Results tree view.
Lock Layers dialog box
Allows you to lock saved markup layers to prevent further changes. This dialog box appears when you select an individual markup file and use the Lock or Unlock Markup command.
Color - Displays the name and a sample of the color used for the elements in each markup layer.
Description - Provides the brief description entered for each layer when it was first saved.
User - Displays the user name of the person who created each layer.
Date - Indicates the date on which each layer was created.
Lock - Indicates whether each layer has already been locked to prevent changes. Layers with a Yes value in this column cannot be modified or deleted.
Select All - Quickly selects all the markup layers available for the file and included in the list. Selected layers are locked when you click the OK button.
Clear All - Removes highlighting from any items in the list of available layers. Selected layers are locked when you click the OK button.
Lock or Unlock Markup dialog box
Allows you to lock or unlock a group of markup files. This dialog box appears when you select a revision or a file and use the Lock or Unlock Markup command. If you use that command after selecting an individual Markup file, use the
Lock Layers dialog box.
Current Status - Indicates whether the selected markup file is currently locked or not. To change the status, click either the Lock or Unlock button.
Lock - Clicking this button locks a markup file. This button is active only if the markup file is not yet locked, and the status in the Current Status option is Unlocked.
Unlock - Clicking this button unlocks a markup file. This button is active only if the markup file was previously locked, and the status in the Current Status option is Locked.
Logon Information dialog box
Allows you to log on to the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
User name - Type your SmartPlant Foundation user name.
Password - Type the password for your user account.
Server - Select the name of the SmartPlant Foundation site connection to which you want to connect. You can also add a new site connection or edit an existing one by clicking Options and then clicking Edit or New.
 When using Windows authentication:
You are logged on automatically if you have one site configured in your Server list and your Windows user name is valid for that SmartPlant Foundation site.
You must enter your name and password if you have multiple sites configured in your Server list.
If your system is configured for automatic logon, then the software automatically logs you on to SmartPlant Foundation using your Windows user name when you open the Desktop Client. This feature is not available if you start
SmartPlant Foundation from an authoring tool.
Alternatively, you can launch the Desktop Client from a URL. There are several optional parameters that you can add to the logon URL. These parameters should be separated with an ampersand (&).
Parameter
Description
user=
The user name assigned by the system administrator.
password=
The password assigned to the user name.
project=
The project to open when the software starts.
class=userinbox
Displays your To Do List.
For example:
http://nodename/directory/SPF.asp?user=jqpublic&password=MyPassword&class=userinbox
Maintain Attributes dialog box
Allows you to update the attribute information for a related object.
Parent Object Details
Name - Name of the parent object.
Description - Description of the parent object.
Related Object Details
Name - Name of the related object.
Description - Description of the related object.
Maintain Calendar dialog box
Allows you to define criteria for multiple calendar years.
New - Allows you to create a new calendar year.
Delete - Removes the selected calendar year.
Available calendars
Available calendars table - Displays the list of available calendars that can be assigned to a project.
Name - Displays the name of the calendar.
Description - Displays a description of the calendar.
Class - Displays the class definition.
Creation Date - Displays the date the calendar was created.
Created By - Displays the name of the user who created the calendar.
Last Updated - Displays the date the calendar was last updated.
Standard non-working days - Allows you to select the non-working days for your calendar year. Working days are displayed in bold text. Non-working days are displayed in normal text.
Calendar year - Displays the calendar in a 12-month view.
Maintain Relationships dialog box
Allows you to add, remove, and edit related items from an object. This dialog box is available from relationship tabs on an object's Update dialog box.
Available items - Displays items that are not related to the object, but are available to relate.
Related items - Displays items that are related to the object.
Find - Allows you to use quick find to locate objects.
Advanced Find - Allows you to use an object's Query dialog box to locate objects.
Apply to similar items - Select this option to apply any changes made to an item related to the object. Select this option before clicking Maintain Attributes.
Maintain Attributes - Opens the Update dialog box for the selected relationship in the Related items list. If the Apply to Similar Items option is selected, any changes you make are applied to all relationships in the Related items list.
Manage [Object] dialog box
Allows you to create, update and delete objects and their relationships.
Existing Related Objects Table
Name - Displays the name of objects that have been related.
Move Selected Item Up 
 - Moves the selected object up one position.
Move Selected Item Down 
 - Moves the selected object down one position.
Remove Selected Item 
 - Removes the selected object from the table and database. Behaves differently if used with a library pane and only removes the relationships between related objects.
All Objects Table
Name - Displays the name of objects that are available to be related.
Create 
 - Creates a new object.
Update 
 - Updates an object
Delete 
 - Deletes an object.
Relate Selected Item 
 - Adds the selected object to the related objects table.
Manage Activity Item Dates command
The Manage Activity Item Dates command allows you to update the deliverable's activity return status code and dates related to Progress. This command is available when you right-click a deliverable that is registered for Progress, or
can be registered for Progress.
Manage Activity Item Dates dialog box
Allows you to update Progress dates and the return status code for a deliverable.
Actual receipt date - The date the deliverable was received from the supplier.
Planned return date - The date the deliverable will be returned, based on the schedule defined by the deliverable review periods.
Actual return date - The date the deliverable was actually returned to the supplier.
Due date - The date the deliverable is due back from the supplier, based on the schedule defined by the deliverable return periods.
Promised date - The date the supplier promised to return the deliverable. This is not a mandatory field, and can be used as required.
Status code - The status code, or return code, for the deliverable. Status codes can represent statuses such as rejected or approved, and determine whether the deliverable can be moved to the next step in the workpack.
Set Planned Return Date - Sets the planned return date for the deliverable based on the schedule of the activity workpack. This date equals the receipt date + internal and external review periods configured for that deliverable type and
status.
Set Due Date - Sets the due date for the deliverable based on the schedule of the activity workpack. This date equals the return date + the return period configured for that deliverable type and status.
Manage Cases dialog box
Allows you to organize, modify, and remove instrument cases. Use this dialog box to specify which cases you want to display when generating a data sheet.
Selected instruments - Displays list of selected instruments.
Available cases - Displays the list of defined cases for the selected instrument.
Existing cases - Displays the cases currently associated with a data sheet. If you select a single instrument, the order that the cases appear in this list is the order in which the information appears on the data sheet. Use the Move up and
Move down buttons to change the order.
 If you select multiple instruments, only those cases which are common to all the selected instruments appear in the Existing cases list. The order in which the cases appear in this list is random and might not match the order in
which the information appears on the data sheet for the instruments.
Add - Places the selected available case in the Existing cases list.
Remove - Moves the selected case from the Existing cases list to the Available cases list.
Move up - Moves the selected existing case one level higher in the Existing cases list.
Move down - Moves the selected existing case one level lower in the Existing cases list.
Case name - Displays the name of the selected existing case, allowing you to specify whether it is the governing case and whether it displays on the data sheet.
Governing case - Select this option when you want the case displayed in the Case name box to be used as the current setting for the instruments.
Manage Engineering Numbering System command
The Manage Engineering Numbering System command allows you to manage, create, update, remove, and filter the relationships among ENS definitions and items. The Manage Engineering Numbering System command is available
when you right-click an ENS definition.
Manage Engineering Numbering System dialog box
Manages the relationships among ENS definitions and items.
Value - filters the relationships by value.
Length - number of spaces allotted for that ENS item.
Pad/Justification - justification for the pad character.
Pad Character - character used to pad the ENS item if all the allotted spaces are not used.
Delimiter - character that comes after an ENS item to visually separate it from the next ENS item.
 - Reorders the objects.
 - Reorders the objects.
 - Removes the relationship between an ENS item and an ENS definition. The ENS item remains in the database.
 - Click to add an item to a definition.
 If the dialog box is in portrait orientation, the Library pane appears at the bottom of the dialog box instead of on the right side of the dialog box. Click Relate Selected Item 
 in the Library pane to add an item to a definition.
Create - Creates a new ENS item.
Update - Updates an ENS item.
Delete - Deletes an ENS item.
Advanced - Filters the list of items by property comparison. The required format is Interface~PropertyDef~Operator~Value.
Manage Subscription dialog box
Allows you to manage the list of specific events to which you have registered to receive e-mail notification when that event occurs to that object.
Subscription Settings
[Object] - Displays the object for which a subscription exists.
Event - Lists the available events for the selected object.
Condition - Lists the conditions that are available to filter the subscription based on specific criteria.
Add - Adds the event and/or condition to the user's subscription list.
Update - Allows the user to change the event and/or condition for an existing object subscription.
Remove - Removes the subscription.
Remove All - Removes all subscriptions.
Subscription Table
[Object] - Displays the object for which a subscription exists.
Event Name - Displays the name of the event to which the user is subscribed
Condition Name - Displays the name of the condition to which the event is related.
Creation Date - Displays the date on which the subscription was created.
Item Create Config - Displays the configuration in which the item was created.
If you want to select multiple events for the object, hold down the CTRL key and click the events you want to subscribe to in the list.
If you have subscribed to events previously, those events appear selected in the list.
 If you have subscribed to other events for this object and want to modify your subscription, you must hold down the CTRL key while you select new events and clear existing events in the list. If you do not hold down the
CTRL key, all selected events are cleared.
Matrix Assignments command
The Edit > Matrix Assignments command allows you to specify the users or user groups included in the selected distribution matrix and the action required from each user or user group upon receipt of a transmittal. For users or user
groups who receive paper copies of transmittals, you can also specify the page size for transmittals. The Edit menu with this command is available when you right click a distribution matrix in the tree view.
Merge dialog box
Allows you to merge objects changed at the project level back to the as-built plant.
Mandatory objects
Name - Displays the name of the objects claimed.
Description - Displays a description of the objects.
Class Definition - Displays the class definition for the objects.
Status - Displays the current status of the object.
Rel Name - Displays the name of the relationship definition.
Name1 - Displays the name of the object that appears at the first end of the relationship definition.
Name2 - Displays the name of the object that appears at the second end of the relationship definition.
Collapse All - Collapses the object and all relationships to the highest object level for display.
Multiple Deliverable Progress Update dialog box
Allows you to update progress information for several deliverables at one time. Use the commands available from the View and Options menus at the top of the dialog box to modify the information you see in the table.
View menu - Allows you to toggle off and on the display of planned, actual, forecast, and progress information.
Options menu - Allows you to select all the displayed deliverables at once.
For each deliverable that you selected, the table on this dialog box displays a portion of the progress information, including the weighting assigned to each deliverable, the completion percentage, and dates and progress status for each
step associated with the workpack.
By activating the check box to the left of the deliverable name of each deliverable applicable, and then making a change to the data displayed, you can make the same change to a number of deliverables at once. Changes are propagated
through all deliverables with a check.
Show Workpack Plan Detail - Shows the workpack details for the workpack.
Hide Workpack Plan Detail - Hides the workpack details for the workpack.
New [Object] dialog box
Defines properties for creating an object.
Main Details
Name - Type the name of the object you want to create.
Description - Type a description of the object you want to create.
The commands available on the File > New submenu vary depending on your role and user access.
The options available on the New dialog box vary depending on the type of object you are creating.
New Activity Workpack dialog box
Allows you to create or modify an activity workpack. The tabs of this dialog box allow you to set options for the workpack and define the steps that deliverables in this workpack must complete.
This dialog box consists of the following tabs:
Workpack Details tab
Steps tab
Plan Details tab
Workpack Details tab
Allows you to create or modify details specific to this workpack. This tab is accessible from the following dialog boxes:
New Design Workpack dialog box
New Design Workpack Template dialog box
New Activity Workpack dialog box
New Activity Workpack Template dialog box
 The options that are available and enabled on this tab vary based on what you are creating.
Details
Workpack Name - Provide a name for the new workpack you are creating.
Type - Displays the type of workpack you are using, in this case Design. You cannot edit the value in this field.
Contract - Specify the contract for the workpack.
Area- Specify the top level of the hierarchy for which this workpack will be available.
Unit - Specify the second level of the hierarchy for which this workpack will be available.
Override Allocated Weighting - When enabled, allows you to override the value displayed in the Allocated Weighting field.
Allocated Weighting - Displays the sum of the weightings of the deliverables registered for progress in this workpack. To override the value displayed here, check the Override Allocated Weighting check box to activate this field.
Options
Active - Specifies whether documents can be registered in progress for this workpack.
Allow Automatic Progress - Indicates whether this workpack will allow the automatic calculation of progress (%) for registered documents in each step.
Automatic Progress - Specifies whether all documents registered for this workpack will have the auto progress feature activated by default.
Allow Downstream Forecasting - Indicates whether this workpack will allow the automatic calculation of remaining forecast dates for the registered documents in subsequent steps.
Downstream Forecasting - Specifies whether all documents registered for this workpack will have the downstream forecasting feature activated by default.
Allow Calculation Override - Indicates whether this workpack will allow the user to manually override the calculated progress value for an individual step. This option is available only when the Allow Auto Progress option is enabled.
In Replan mode - Indicates whether the workpack is in Replan mode. You will never manually activate this check box. To initiate a replan, use the Initiate Replan command. Once the replan is initiated, this check box is automatically
activated by the software.
Progress Updated - Specify whether the updating of deliverable progress has been complete for this workpack. This field is unchecked when the progress rollup process has been completed on a project cut-off dates.
Include in Rollup - Indicates whether this workpack should be included in the rollup process.
Deliverable Plan Frozen - Indicates that the plan dates for deliverables in this workpack have been locked to prevent changes.
Steps tab
Allows you to create or modify the steps that define the lifecycle of the deliverables that will be associated with this workpack.
This tab is available from the following dialog boxes:
New Design Workpack dialog box
New Design Workpack Template dialog box
New Activity Workpack Template dialog box
New Activity Workpack dialog box
Before - Opens the Add Step dialog box, where you can create a new production step that will be placed immediately before the step currently selected in the table.
After - Opens the Add Step dialog box, where you can create a new production step that will be placed immediately after the step currently selected in the table.
Sub Step - Opens the Add Step dialog box, where you can create a new production step that will be a substep of the selected step. This button is not available unless you have created at least one step, and have selected it.
Parallel - Opens the Add Step dialog box, where you can add a parallel production step to the step currently selected in the table. This button is not available unless you have created at least one step, and have selected it.
 Once you have associated parallel steps with a production step, the Allow Auto Progress and Downstream Forecasting options on the Workpack Detail tab are no longer available.
Update - Allows the selected step description, weighting, and events to be updated.
Delete - Removes the selected step from the table.
View Events - Opens the Step Events dialog box, where you can specify events and conditions for selected steps. This option is only available if you are creating a design workpack or design workpack template.
Return Codes - Opens the Return Codes dialog box, where you can specify return codes for selected steps. This option is only available if you are creating an activity workpack or activity workpack template.
Plan Details tab
Allows you to create or modify the plan details for a workpack.
This tab is accessible from the following dialog boxes:
New Design Workpack dialog box
New Activity Workpack dialog box
Plan Start - Specifies the planned start date of the workpack.
 The Plan Start and Plan End fields are required if you set the workpack to active by checking the Active check box on the Workpack Details tab.
Plan End - Specifies the planned end date of the workpack.
Float Start - Indicates the date by which the plan must be started, including any available buffer or slip time.
 The Float Start and Float End dates may be outside the defined Plan Start and Plan End dates.
Float End - Indicates the date by which the plan must be ended, including any available buffer or slip time.
Construction Start - Indicates the date on which construction is scheduled to begin.
 The Construction Start and Construction End dates must be within the defined Plan Start and Plan End dates.
Construction End - Indicates the date by which construction should be complete.
Replan Start - If necessary, enter a revised plan start date in this field rather than changing the original date entered for that value.
The Replan Start, Replan End, Construction Replan Start, and Construction Replan End fields are active only if a replan that affects this workpack has been initiated.
The Replan Start and Replan End dates are required when the workpack is in Replan mode, as indicated by a check in the In Replan mode check box.
Replan End - If in replan mode, enter a revised plan end date in this field rather than changing the original date entered for that value.
Construction Replan Start - If necessary, provide a revised construction start date in this field.
Construction Replan End - If necessary, enter a revised construction end date in this field rather than changing the original date entered for that value.
Budget - Indicates the original budget of man hours allocated to completing the workpack.
 You must enter the Budget man hours, or information about this workpack will not appear when you view progress for this configuration.
Unapproved - Indicates the changes that were made to the scope of the work (man hours needed to complete the task) that have not yet been approved.
To be Approved - Indicate the number of additional man hours that have been approved for this project. The number you enter here is subtracted from the value in the To be Approved field and added to the Approved field.
Approved - Indicates the changes that were made to the scope of the work (man hours needed to complete the task) that have already been approved.
Current - Displays the accumulated man hours derived from the sum of the budgeted hours and the approved hours. You cannot edit this field.
New Activity Workpack Template dialog box
Allows you to create or modify an activity workpack template.
This dialog box consists of the following tabs:
Workpack Details tab
Steps tab
New Datalist Definition dialog box
Allows you to edit the criteria for data list definitions.
Main details
Name - Displays the name of the data list.
Description - Displays the description for the data list.
Datalist definition details
Datalist criteria - Displays the data list criteria.
Interface Definition - Displays the interface definition related to the data list.
Data sheet definition details
Datasheet Type - Defines the data sheet type. The value is sent to the GenDataSheet API in the server. The server looks for data sheet definitions, and it will return those whose property SPFDataSheetType matches the Data sheet type
value. The DataSheet Type specified for the data list definition must match the DataSheet Type specified for the method.
Related object limit - Limits the number of related objects brought into the data sheet or data list when it is generated.
Support partially mapped data sheets - Saves unmapped data entered in the data list.
View definitions - Relates a view definition to the data list object. You can use the Find box to search for view definitions.
Template document - Relates a template document to the data list object. You can use the Find dialog box to search for a template document.
Target class definitions - Relates a class definition to the data list object. You can use the Find dialog box to search for a target class definition.
Excel publish settings details
Publish document title - Defines the document title in the Excel file.
Publish document category - Defines the document category in the Excel file.
Publish document type - Defines the document type in the Excel file.
Publish document sub-type - Defines the document type sub-type in the Excel file.
Publish document classdef name - Defines the document class definition name in the Excel file.
Document item relations - Used to specify the relationships to be created on the published objects that instantiate specified interfaces.
New Design Workpack dialog box
Allows you to create or modify a design workpack. The tabs of this dialog box allow you to set options for the workpack, define the steps that deliverables in this workpack must complete, associate a time string to calculate the dates for
steps, and define specific dates for completion of the workpack.
This dialog box consists of the following tabs:
Workpack Details tab
Steps tab
Time Strings tab
Plan Details tab
Time Strings tab
Allows you to create or modify the time strings for a workpack. This tab is accessible from the following dialog boxes:
New Design Workpack dialog box
New Design Workpack Template dialog box
Time strings indicate the number of days required to complete each production step for the workpack. This table includes columns for the name and description of the time string and for each steps defined for the workpack. The number for
each step represents the number of days expected to elapse between the end of the step and the end of the following step.
Name - Displays the name associated with a specific time string.
Description - Displays a description for a time string.
Delete Row- Deletes a row from the table.
New Design Workpack Template dialog box
Allows you to create or modify a design workpack template. The tabs of this dialog box allow you to set options for the workpack template, define the steps that deliverables in this workpack must complete, and associate a time string to
calculate the dates for steps.
This dialog box consists of the following tabs:
Workpack Details tab
Steps tab
Time Strings tab
New ENS Definition dialog box
Defines a new ENS Definition
Name - specifies the name and purpose of the ENS definition. This is a required field.
Description - specifies a description for the ENS definition. This is an optional field.
Property set by ENS - specifies the property that the ENS defines. This is a required field.
New Graphic Report dialog box
Allows you to select the item type for a new graphic report in SmartPlant Foundation.
Description - Displays the descriptions of the available item types.
New Host command
File > New > Administration > Host creates a new Host object.
New Host dialog box
Defines a new host.
Name - specifies the name of the new host. This is a required field.
Description - describes the new host. This is an optional field.
Is host secure - check box indicates the security associated with the host.
New Label dialog box
Allows you to create a new label in SmartPlant Foundation.
Name - Specifies a name for the new object.
Description - Specifies a description for the new label.
Top left label text - Specifies the text to appear in the upper left corner of the page when a document associated with the label is printed.
Label top center text - Specifies the text to appear in the upper center of the page when a document associated with the label is printed.
Label top right text - Specifies the text to appear in the upper right corner of the page when a document associated with the label is printed.
Label bottom left text - Specifies the text to appear in the lower left corner of the page when a document associated with the label is printed.
Label bottom center text - Specifies the text to appear in the lower center of the page when a document associated with the label is printed.
Label bottom right text - Specify the text to appear in the lower right corner of the page when a document associated with the label is printed.
Font name - Specifies the font to use for the label text. Select either Arial, Courier, Times New Roman, or Verdana
Font size - Specifies the size of the label text. Available font sizes range from 8 to 72 points
Font style - Specifies whether the label text will be bold, italic, or regular.
New Relationship dialog box
Allows you to view new relationships created for an object and edit the properties for relationships that support attributes.
Related Object - Displays the related object.
Relationship Name - Displays the type of related object. If there are alternative relationships to choose from, click the Relationship Name box and use the down arrow to select the appropriate relationship from the list.
Properties - Allows you to update the attributes of the object. If a relationship supports attributes, you can edit the attributes by clicking the Maintain Attributes hyperlink for the relationship.
New Relationship dialog box
Allows you to create a relationship between two or more objects. 
Child Object - Displays the name of the child object.
Relationship Name - Allows you to select the type of relationship that is created.
Properties - Displays the properties for the relationship.
Apply to similar items - Specifies whether the relationship selected in the Relationship Name column applies to all similar objects in the table. Similar objects have a different color background to help you identify them. If the Apply to
similar items check box is selected and you select a relationship, the software creates the same relationship for all objects with the background color.
New Report dialog box
Allows you to select the item type for which you want to generate a report.
Description - Displays the descriptions of the available item types.
New Server/Edit Server dialog box
Allows you to add or change a server name for logging on to the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client.
Name - Type a meaningful name for the server. This name will appear in the Server list on the Logon Information dialog box.
Web host - Type the actual server name of the Web host computer where the SmartPlant Foundation server is located.
Web directory - Type the name of the virtual directory on the Web host. You can also use the SmartPlant Foundation site name.
Secure channel (SSL) - Check this option if the server connection is to be accessed by SSL. SSL is set up on the server.
Trusted site - Select this option if you are running in a non-domain account environment. This will cause you to be prompted for domain credentials when you log on.
Delete - Select a server that you want to remove from the Server list in the Logon Information dialog box, and click Delete. (Edit Server dialog box only)
Validate - Click to test the connection to the server.
New Watermark dialog box
Allows you to create a new watermark in SmartPlant Foundation.
Name - Specifies the name of the watermark object.
Description - Specifies the description of the watermark.
Watermark text - Displays the text that appears as the watermark on the printed document.
Watermark type - Specifies the fill pattern or opacity of the watermark text.
When a SmartPlant file is printed, the watermark type can be Hatch, Outline and Filled, Outline, or Filled.
When a non-SmartPlant file is printed, the watermark type can be Light Opacity, Medium Opacity, Very Light Opacity, or Dark Opacity.
Position - Specifies the position of the watermark on the printed page. The watermark can be printed vertically, horizontally, or diagonally across the sheet.
Font name - Specifies the font to use for the watermark text. Select Arial, Courier, Times New Roman, or Verdana.
Font size - Specifies the size of the watermark text. Available font sizes range from 8 to 72 points.
Font style - Specifies whether the watermark text is bold, italic, regular, or underlined.
New Workpack or Workpack Template dialog box
Allows you to create a new design workpack, design workpack template, activity workpack, or activity workpack template. For information about options to create workpacks or workpack templates, click the name of the dialog box below:
New Design Workpack dialog box
New Design Workpack Template dialog box
New Activity Workpack Template dialog box
New Activity Workpack dialog box
Object Compare dialog box
Displays an ad-hoc report that shows the changes in the project, or lower configuration, compared to the as-built data in the plant.
Name - Displays the name of the object being compared to the as-built data and its interface definitions.
Class Def - Displays the class definition for the objects.
High config value - Displays the value of the object for the as-built plant.
Low config value - Displays the value of the object for the project, or lower configuration.
Value as Claimed - Displays the value of the object at the time it was claimed.
Status - Displays the current status of the object and in which configuration it was changed.
Ignore conflicts on unselected object deletes - Allows you to ignore the conflicts displayed for objects claimed from the plant and deleted in the project so that the deleted object can be merged.
[Object] Subscriptions for [Users] dialog box
Allows you to review and modify any of the subscription to an object or class across users.
Subscription Settings
Event - Lists the available events for the selected object.
Condition - Lists the conditions that are available to filter the subscription based specific criteria.
Add - Adds the event and/or condition to the user's subscription list.
Update - Allows the user to change the event and/or condition for an existing object subscription.
Remove - Removes the subscription.
Remove All - Removes all subscriptions.
Subscription Table
[Object] - Displays the object for which a subscription exists.
Event Name - Displays the name of the event to which the user is subscribed
Condition Name - Displays the name of the condition to which the event is related.
Creation Date - The date on which the subscription was created.
Out of Date Object dialog box
Allows users to resolve differences between property values and relationships in the List Edit window and the values in the database. Click the value you want to use. Click the New Local Value or Server Value column header to select
all values in the column.
Property - The property that is out of date.
Relationship - The relationship that is out of date.
Original Local Value - The value for the property or relationship that was current when you opened the List Edit window.
New Local Value - The value for the property or relationship that you entered in the List Edit window.
Server Value - The value for the property or relationship that is in the database.
Progress Rollup S-Curve dialog box
Shows a graphical representation of the progress information, including the planned, actual, and forecast data, when applicable. Cut-off dates make up the x-axis, and percent complete makes up the vertical axis.
Properties to Display dialog box
Allows you to add and remove step properties displayed in the View Workflow dialog box, and determine the size of the workflow step boxes.
Arrange Properties - Shows the current arrangement of properties on the workflow step boxes.
Step Width - Specifies the width of the step box.
Number of Lines - Specifies the number of lines inside the step box.
Column set - Specifies the column set to be used for the display. The column set determines which properties of the step display on the workflow step boxes.
Display Property Name - Allows you to specify whether or not you want to display the name of the step property in the workflow step box, as a label along with the property value.
Defaults - Restores the default properties for the step box, removing any changes you have made.
Published Documents dialog box
Allows you to search for an object by the object properties.
Main details
Name - Displays the name of the published document.
Description - Displays the description of the published document.
Master document details
Category - Lists the document categories available for selection.
Type - Lists the types of documents available for selection.
Subtype - Lists the subtypes of documents available for selection.
Title - Displays the title of the published document.
Status - Allows you to select if the document for which you are searching is ISSUED or RESERVED.
Document Publisher
Publisher - Lists the tools that are registered with SmartPlant Foundation. When publisher is selected, the query lists the documents published by the tool.
Revision details
Revision scheme - Lists the revisions schemes available for the document.
Major revision - Lists the major revision numbers available for the document.
Minor Revision - Lists the minor revision numbers available for the document.
Status - Allows you to select if the document for which you are searching is CURRENT, WORKING, SUPERSEDED or RESERVED.
Owning Group
Item owning group - Lists the owner of the published documents.
Related Items
Related Load Queue - Lists the load queues available for the published documents.
Query dialog box
Allows you to search for an object by the object properties.
Details
Name - Searches the name of the object.
Description - Searches the description of the object.
ContainerID - Displays the Container ID for the object and relationship.
General details
Creation date - Searches for objects that were created on a specific date. When you click this box, the Set Date and Time dialog box appears, providing additional options for setting dates and times for use in queries.
Create user - Searches for objects created by a specific SmartPlant Foundation user.
Last updated - Searches for objects by the last date it was changed. When you click this box, the Set Date and Time dialog box appears, providing additional options for setting dates and times for use in queries.
Query by Container dialog box
Allows you to retrieve objects by container for review or edit.
Domain - Selects the domain in which to query for a container.
Container - Selects the container on which to query. You can select multiple containers from the list by holding down the CTRL button.
Quick Enter Container ID instead of selecting one - Type a container ID on which to query.
Class definition - Select the class definition on which to query for a container. You can select multiple class definitions from the list by holding down the CTRL button.
Object or class name - Type an object or class name on which to query for a container.
 A container ID that is no longer associated with any objects may still appear in the list even though it is no longer used. Log off from the Desktop Client and back on to remove the unused container ID from the cache.
Reason for Receipt and Format dialog box
Allows you to select reasons for receipt and formats for the matrix.
Reason for receipt - Allows you to select a reason for receipt from the drop down list.
Format - Allows you to select a format from the drop down list.
Reassign dialog box
Allows you to reassign a workflow step to another SmartPlant Foundation user or role. You cannot reassign it to both.
Select user - From the list box, select the user to whom you want to assign the workflow step.
Select role - From the list box, select the role to which you want to assign the workflow step.
Register for Progress command
The Register with Progress command allows you to associate a deliverable with a workpack. This command is available on the shortcut menu that appears when you right-click on a deliverable. The workpack to which the deliverable is
registered is based on the properties that make up the hierarchy. In other words, the deliverable is delivered to a workpack based on the area and unit to which the deliverable is related.
Register for Progress dialog box
Allows you to add deliverables to the progress functionality by adding them to an existing workpack, according to the defined hierarchy.
This dialog box consists of the following tabs:
Details tab (Register for Progress dialog box)
Steps tab (Register for Progress dialog box)
Details tab (Register for Progress dialog box)
Allows you to specify progress details for the deliverable being associated with a workpack. This tab is accessible from the Register for Progress dialog box and the Update Progress dialog box.
Names - Displays the names of all the selected deliverables being associated with a workpack.
Contract - Displays the contract to which the workpack is associated.
Area - Indicates the top hierarchy level of the deliverable. The information in this field and the Unit field determine to which workpack the deliverable is registered.
Unit - Indicates the second hierarchy level of the deliverable. The information in this field and the Area field determine to which workpack the deliverable is registered.
Last updated - Displays the date on which the progress calculations for the selected deliverable were last updated.
Planned (%) - Specifies the expected level of completion for the deliverable as of the last date on which progress was calculated, according to the plan dates.
Actual (%) - Specifies the actual level of completion for the deliverable as of the last date on which progress was calculated.
Forecast (%) - Specifies the expected level of completion for the deliverable as of the last date on which progress was calculated, according to the forecast dates.
Weighting - Indicate the weighting assigned to the deliverable within the workpack.
Budget man hours - Specify the number of man hours budgeted for the completion of this deliverable.
Man hours to complete - Specify the number of man hours necessary to complete this deliverable.
Forecast man hours to complete - Indicates the number of man hours to complete the deliverable, based on any deviations from the plan.
Steps tab (Register for Progress dialog box)
Allows you to view or modify workpack data for the deliverable you are registering. This tab is accessible from the Register for Progress dialog box and the Update Progress dialog box.
Plan start - Specifies the planned start date of the workpack.
Plan end - Specifies the planned end date of the workpack.
Float start - Indicates the date by which the plan must be started, including any available buffer or slip time.
Float end - Indicates the date by which the plan must be ended, including any available buffer or slip time.
Construction start - Indicates the date on which construction is scheduled to begin.
Construction end - Indicates the date by which construction should be complete.
Downstream forecasting - Specifies whether the document will use the downstream forecasting feature. This functionality uses specified dates and time strings to calculate when each step is scheduled to be completed.
Calculation override - Indicates whether the users are allowed to manually override the calculated progress value for an individual step.
Reason for Override - Enter the reason for override. This option is only available in replan mode.
Show color keys - Displays a key of the colors used on the grid to indicate step statuses, such as Overdue, Pending, and so forth.
Step Name - For all of the steps associated with the applicable workpack, this table displays the name defined for each step.
Step Description - Provides a description for each step in the workpack.
Plan Date - Specifies the planned date for each step.
Actual Date - Displays, when applicable, the actual date on which a step was completed.
Forecast Date - Specifies the date on which a step is forecasted to be completed. The value displayed here is calculated using the last available Actual date and the selected time string.
Replan Date - Displays the replan date specified for each step.
Progress - Indicates the level of completion of each step defined for the workpack.
Step Status - Displays the status of each step associated with the workpack.
 The following options are only available when you are registering a deliverable for progress.
Timestrings - Displays a list of all the defined time strings that are available to be used to calculate forecasted dates. Select a time string from the list and click Apply to use that time string to complete the table.
View - Opens a window where you can view the details of the selected time string.
New - Opens the Timestring dialog box where you can create a new time string.
Delete - Removes the selected time string from the list, provided it is not used by any other deliverable.
Apply - Uses the selected time string to calculate the information in the table.
Resolve Conflicts dialog box
Allows you to select the changes in the project, or lower configuration, that you want to merge to the as-built data in the plant.
Name - Displays the name of the object being compared to the as-built data and its interface definitions.
Class Def - Displays the class definition for the objects.
High config value - Displays the value of the object for the as-built plant.
Low config value - Displays the value of the object for the project, or lower configuration.
Value as Claimed - Displays the value of the object at the time it was claimed. This value can be different than the as-built value if the object was claimed and modified in another project, but not yet merged back to the plant.
Status - Displays the current status of the object and in which configuration it was changed.
Accept All Objects - Selects the check box beside the name of each object but does not select check boxes by specific properties.
Accept All - Selects all objects and relationships to resolve for merging to the as-built data in the plant.
Clear All - Clears all objects and relationships.
Ignore conflicts on unselected object deletes - Allows you to ignore the conflicts displayed for objects claimed from the plant and deleted in the project so that the deleted object can be merged.
Resolve Conflicts dialog box
Allows you to resolve problems with objects edited or created in the List Edit window.
Name - The name of the object.
Type - The type of conflict. The two types are Validation and Out-Of-Date. The value in this column changes to Resolved when the conflict is addressed.
Message - Displays details about the conflict.
Resolve - Click to resolve the conflict.
Response Manager dialog box
The Response Manager allows the document controller to perform workflow steps for e-mail and paper users who do not have access to SmartPlant Foundation. The Response Manager behaves identically to the To Do List.
In the Response Manager, you can view and complete checklists associated with workflow steps. You can also complete voting steps and mark steps completed or unable to be completed.
The Response Manager has three parts:
Toolbar
List View
Checklist Preview Pane
Toolbar
The Response Manager toolbar contains the following buttons:
Icon
Name
Description
Refresh
Refreshes the Response Manager with new tasks. By default, the software automatically refreshes the information every
10 minutes if the value is not changed through user preferences. However, you can force a manual update by clicking
the Refresh button.
Checklist Preview Pane
Displays the checklist associated with the workflow step in a pane at the bottom of the Response Manager. When the
checklist preview pane is displayed, the icon changes to 
.
Save Checklist
Saves your changes to the checklist associated with the workflow step.
List View
The list view displays a list of the workflow steps for the transmittal from which the Response Manager was opened. All tasks appear here, regardless of the recipient to which the step is assigned, allowing a document controller to manage
responses for email and paper users. There are several types of steps, called step classes, that may appear in the Response Manager. The following icons represent these steps:
Icon
Step class
Description
Info
A step that appears for your information only. If an information step appears in the Response
Manager, you are required to acknowledge it.
Assignment
A step that assigns work to you. You may be required to complete a checklist before you can
complete an assignment step.
Approval
A step that requires you to vote. You may be required to complete a checklist before you can
complete an approval step.
 Icons with red backgrounds indicate that the step is overdue. For example, if an assignment step is overdue, the 
 icon appears beside the step.
Checklist Preview Pane
If you select a workflow step that has a checklist associated with it in the list view, the checklist preview pane displays the checklist associated with the step. You can update the checklist by right-clicking the item that you want to change in
the checklist. You can also update a checklist for a step by right-clicking the step in the list view. For the issue step, the checklist includes an item for each document attached to the transmittal.
You can view the percentage of workflow completion for each workflow step under Workflow Percentage column. The workflow status percentage is updated on acceptance or rejection of each workflow step by the user.
 For more information on configuring workflow status and workflow status percentage, see Create a step status.
Return Codes dialog box
Allows you to indicate a return status code that indicates the reason a document is being returned to its originator and reflects an approval or rejection of the document.
 Every step except the first one must have a return code.
Return code - Specify a return status code for the step.
Add - Adds the return code to the step.
Remove - Removes the return code from the step.
Revise dialog box
Allows you to revise a document in the authoring tool without publishing it.
 Boxes with a shaded background are read-only and cannot be edited.
 Engineering Tool — Displays an authoring tool-specific dialog box that allows you to select documents to add to the Selected documents list.
 Delete — Removes the selected document from the Selected Documents list.
Selected documents — Displays a list of the documents selected to be revised. You populate this list by selecting documents before activating the Revise command. You can click the Delete button to remove documents from this list.
Revision Scheme — If you have selected a new document or a document for which no revision scheme has been selected, choose the revision scheme to be applied from the list of available options. If you have selected a document with
a defined revision scheme, that scheme is displayed here in a read-only format. Only revision schemes that are applicable to the configuration (plant) or classification (document type) are available in the drop-down menu. If none of the
revision schemes are related to the configuration or classification, then all revision schemes are available. For more information on revision scheme configuration, see Configure different revision scheme strategies.
Current Revision in Tool > Major — For existing documents, this box displays the current major revision of the document, as defined in the authoring tool, in a read-only format. For new documents, this box is empty.
Current Revision in Tool > Minor — For existing documents, this box displays the current minor revision of the document, as defined in the authoring tool, in a read-only format. If the revision scheme does not use minor revision, or if the
selected document has not yet been revised, this box is empty.
 If you do not use the Minor box when revising a document for the first time, the minor revision option will never be available for that document for future revisions.
Revise in Tool > Major — From this list box, choose the next available major revision number for the document to revise it locally, without publishing the new information.
Revise in Tool > Minor — From this list box, choose the next available minor revision number for the document to revise it locally, without publishing the new information. If minor revisions are not supported for the document, no options
are available in this list.
 Administrator can disable skipping of revision numbers using the Integration options in the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client. For more information on configuring skipping of revisions, see Configure Allow skipping
revisions.
Next Major — Selects the next available major revision number for the document to revise it locally, without publishing the new information.
Next Minor — Selects the next available minor revision number for the document to revise it locally without publishing the new information.
Revision Details dialog box
Revision scheme - Select the revision scheme for the document.
Major revision - Set the major revision for the document. The software displays the first major revision number in the Major revision box.
Minor revision - Set the minor revision for the document, if necessary. The software displays the first minor revision number in the Minor revision box.
Status - Displays the status of the document. This option is display only.
Run Graphic Report dialog box
If you selected a graphic report before clicking the Run Graphic Report command, this dialog box allows you to choose a drawing or model on which to run the selected graphic report definition. If you selected a drawing or model before
clicking the Run Graphic Report command, this dialog box allows you to choose one or more graphic report definitions to apply to that model or drawing.
Criteria that appear in this table are determined by the selection you made before running the Run Graphic Report command.
Selecting a drawing or model on which to run the selected graphic report
Name of Item - Displays the name of the drawing or model.
Description - Displays the description of the drawing or model.
Class - Specifies the class of each drawing or model.
Creation Date - Indicates the date on which the drawing or model was created.
Created By - Indicates who created the model or drawing.
Last Updated Date - Indicates the date on which the drawing or model was last updated.
Selecting one or more graphic reports to run against the selected model or drawing
Name of Item - Provides the name of defined graphic reports that are available to use. You may choose one or more of these reports by selecting them from this table.
 If only one report is defined and available, this dialog box does not appear; the report is used automatically.
Classification - Specifies the classification of each defined graphic report.
Descriptions - Provides a brief description of each graphic report as provided when it was created.
 All date and time settings are stored in the database in Greenwich Mean Time (GMT), but displayed in local time, as determined by the regional settings on the local computer.
Run Editor dialog box
Allows you to check out a document and open an attached file in a native application. The application in which the file is opened is determined by your system settings for the default applications for file types. This dialog box appears when
you use the Run Editor command to check out a document and launch more than one file. If only one file was selected, this document will not appear the software will automatically launch the associated application.
Name - Displays the name of the attached files and a check box that determines whether each file will be opened in the default editor. This option has the following available states:
Checked - The file is selected for editing.
Unchecked - The file is not selected for editing.
Gray - A file nested under the file is selected for editing.
Description - Displays the description of the attached file. This option is display only.
File Local Directory - Displays the location to which the file is checked out.
Run Rollup command
The Progress > Run Rollup command uses the information defined for every deliverable in every workpack that is included in the rollup to calculate the current level of completion of each workpack, when applicable. Once the roll-up has
been performed, the information is stored for that particular cut-off date, and the information is available for viewing through the View Progress dialog box.
Progress is calculated at every level of the hierarchy, including every deliverable associated with every workpack, so for larger sets of data, this process may take some time.
 
Save Alternate Renditions dialog box
Allows you to save copies of file renditions.
Path - Displays the folder to which you want to save the files.
Name - Displays the name of the rendition file and a check box that determines whether the rendition file is to be saved to the local folder. This check box has the following available states:
Checked - The file is selected to save to the local folder.
Unchecked - The file is not selected to save to the local folder.
Description - Displays the description of the alternate rendition file. This option is display only.
Revision - Displays the document revision to which the document is attached. This option is display only.
Revision Status - Displays the revision state of the document.
Out of Date - Specifies whether the attached file or reference file is out of date. This option is display only.
Viewable - Displays whether you can view the attached file in SmartPlant Foundation. This option is display only.
Select All - Selects the viewable files to save to the local folder.
Clear All - Clears all selected files so that viewable files can be selected individually.
Expand All - Expands the Select revisions tree to display all nested reference files.
Copy all references - Selects all nested reference files to save to the local folder. This is not applicable to the alternate rendition because they do not have any reference files.
 The first time you use this dialog box, the path displayed by default is to your My Documents folder. Any time you select a different location and save one or more files to it, that path is stored in the system to be used as the default
location the next time you access this dialog box.
Save Graphic Report dialog box
Allows you to save your query criteria for the graphic report.
Name - Displays the name of the graphic report that you want to save.
Description - Displays the description of the graphic report that you want to save.
Owner - Displays the user name of the user who created the graphic report definition.
Save Query dialog box
Allows you to save a query so that you can run the query later.
Query name - Type a name for the query.
Description - Type a description for the query.
GUI display as - Type the name for the query to use on the shortcut menu.
 You can only save queries from the Query dialog box available from the main Query menu.
Save Report dialog box
Allows you to save your query criteria for the report.
Name - Displays the name of the report that you want to save.
Description - Displays the description of the report that you want to save.
Owner - Displays the user name of the user who created the report.
Save Target As dialog box
Allows you to copy any viewable files attached to the selected document and save the copied files locally to the client.
 You can control user access to the viewable files. For more information, see TBC
Path - Displays the folder to which you want to save the files.
The table provides read-only information about the documents to be copied locally:
Name - Displays the name of the attached file and a check box that determines whether the file is to be saved to the local folder. This check box has the following available states:
Checked - The file is copied to the local folder.
Unchecked - The file is not copied to the local folder.
Gray - A file nested under this file is copied to the local folder. The box for the parent file can be checked or not.
Description - Displays the description of the attached file.
Attaching Document - Displays the name of the document to which the file in the current row is attached.
Revision - Displays the document revision to which the document is attached.
Revision Status - Displays the revision state of the document.
Out of Date - Specifies whether the attached file or reference file is out of date.
Viewable - Displays whether you can view the attached file in SmartPlant Foundation.
Select All - Selects the viewable files to save to the local folder.
Clear All - Clears all selected files so that viewable files can be selected individually.
Expand All - Expands the Select revisions tree to display all nested reference files.
Copy all references - Selects all nested reference files to be copied to the local folder.
Reset Default - Changes the current save location setting to a previously defined setting. By selecting Reset Default, the Path field displays the most recently saved location. The first time you use this dialog box, the path displayed by
default is to your My Documents folder. When you select and save files to a different location, that path is stored as the default location for the next time you access this dialog box.
Schema Import Wizard dialog box
Allows you to import the schema and administration files.
Select compare context
Compare content of selected files with the database. - Compares each object in the file with the database. All new, modified, or deleted objects and their relationships are displayed in the Comparison Results dialog box.
Compare content of selected files against all database items with a matching container ID. - Compares the Container ID for the schema file with existing schema objects in the SmartPlant Foundation database that have the same
Container ID. All new, modified, or deleted objects and their relationships are displayed in the Comparison Results dialog box.
Select external schema file - Displays the path of the schema file to be loaded. Click Browse to select the file.
Compare Options
Include identical items in the compare results. - Displays identical objects in the Comparison Results dialog box.
Automatically apply any changes found during the compare. - Skips the review process and makes the changes to the database based on the loaded schema file.
Schema Import Wizard Comparison Results dialog box
Allows you to view the results of the comparison and select which changes to load.
Select changes to load
Object Class - Displays the classification of the object.
Object State - Displays whether the object is new, updated, or removed.
Object UID - Displays the object's unique identifier.
Object Name - Displays the object name.
New - Displays the number of new objects loaded.
Updated - Displays the number of updated objects loaded.
Removed - Displays the number of objects removed.
Identical - Displays the number of objects found that are identical to the database source.
Export - Exports the compared objects to a file for review.
Select All - Selects all objects to load.
Clear All - Removes all objects from the load.
Details - Displays the load results in a text file.
Added - Displays any new objects or properties in blue.
Changed - Displays updated objects or properties in yellow.
Removed - Displays any deleted objects or properties in red.
If an object fails to load, place your mouse over the object to display details about the exception.
Schema Import Wizard will reorder strings in the text file to match those in the database. These will be displayed as updated objects, but the function of the objects does not change.
Schema Import Wizard Review Changes dialog box
Displays the changes to the SmartPlant Foundation database for review.
Object Class - Displays the classification of the object.
Object State - Displays whether the object is new, updated, or removed.
Object UID - Displays the object's unique identifier.
Object Name - Displays the object name.
Load State - Displays
Changes Summary
Items Inserted - Displays the number of new items inserted into the database.
Items Updated - Displays the number of items updated in the database.
Items Deleted - Displays the number of items removed from the database.
Select Accept Steps dialog box
Allows you to specify accept steps for the current workflow.
Left pane - Displays the steps in the current workflow.
Add - Adds the selected step in the left pane to the right pane.
This step is the next step to be performed if the current step is accepted.
You can have more than one parallel accept step.
Remove - Removes the selected step from the list.
Find - Allows you to display different workflows. You can link a step in one workflow to a step in another.
Select attributes to display dialog box
Allows you to change the information displayed on the symbols in the graphical view of a workflow.
Step width - Assigns a width to the step.
Number of Lines - Specifies the number of lines to display on the step.
Column set - Allows you to change which properties are displayed on the symbols. The default is WFVColumnSet that displays step name, step description, step target date, and comments.
Display property name - Specifies that the property names be displayed.
Select Display Parameters dialog box
Provides tabs for selecting the layout, colors, and other view options for the Compare window.
Layout tab
Display tab
Auto-focus tab
Layout tab (Select Display Parameters dialog box)
Allows you to select the layout of the View and Compare window by clicking the button that corresponds to the configuration you want.
Different colors represent different parts of the View and Compare window:
Red - First drawing
Blue - Second drawing
Yellow - Properties window
Green - Comparison results table
Display tab (Select Display Parameters dialog box)
Allows you to define color settings and display indicators for the comparison results displayed in the View and Compare window.
Set Color - Allows you to set the color for the comparison result type you selected in the list.
Restore Default Color - Restores the default color for the comparison result type you selected in the list. By default, the following colors represent types of comparison results:
Green - Deleted objects
Blue - Modified objects
Red - New objects
Black - Objects that are the same in both drawings
Restore All Default Colors - Restores the default colors for all comparison results.
Set Color - Allows you to indicate whether you want color to identify comparison results of the selected type. If the check box is selected beside a comparison result type, color is used to identify that type of change in the drawings,
comparison results table, and Properties window.
Make Bold - Allows you to indicate which of the comparison results of the selected type to bold. If the check box is selected beside a comparison result type, bold is used to identify that type of change in the drawings, comparison results
table, and Properties window.
Display Titlebar in Drawing Frames - Indicates whether you want to display the name of the drawing and its major and minor revision numbers in the title bars of the drawings in the View and Compare window.
Auto-focus tab (Select Display Parameters dialog box)
Allows you to define auto-focus settings for the View and Compare window.
When selecting an item in a drawing, do you want to automatically find it in the list of changes? - Determines whether clicking an object in one of the drawing views automatically scrolls to the changes for that object in the
comparison results table. Click Yes to automatically find the changes for an object in the comparison results table; click No to turn this option off. By default, this option is set to No.
When selecting an item in the list of changes, do you want to automatically find it in the drawings? - Determines whether clicking a change in the comparison results table automatically finds the object in the drawing views. Click
Yes to automatically find the corresponding objects in the drawing views; click No to turn this option off. By default, this option is set to Yes.
When finding an item in the drawings, what method do you want to use? - Sets how you want the Compare window to behave when the software automatically locates an object in the drawings. Available options include the following:
