5. Under Select comparison version, select the previous version of the tag register with which you want to compare the current version.
 The option to select previous version is available only if Show change status column option is selected in the register definition corresponding to the selected register document.
6. Click OK to generate the tag register for the selected register document. The tag register with 10 equipment tags with its technical details is generated.
For more information, see Using Tag Registers.
Managing Authored Tags
In the engineering world, tag can mean many specific things. For example, in the design tools that make up the SmartPlant Enterprise environment, tag usually means a piece of equipment that will be placed in the plant. In SmartPlant
Master Tag Registry, a tag is a composite of technical details about objects coming from the authoring tools and an object identification number, or tag number. For example, a tag created for an instrument object will have the tag number
PI-1000 along with technical details, such as tag status, description, key engineering and references to import documents. The tag number and the technical details of the tags can be created, collected and managed with SmartPlant
Master Tag Registry.
Tags in the authoring domain
SmartPlant Master Tag Registry includes an authoring domain for gathering tags and their data from vendors and for incorporating additional data on tags published by SmartPlant authoring tools. These tags can originate in SmartPlant
Master Tag Registry to support engineering work processes where vendor tags are pre-allocated by an EPC. The technical data on the tags is provided by vendors in a Microsoft Excel data sheet and imported into the authoring domain.
The information about the tags can then be managed in the authoring environment until it is ready to be published to the published domain (MTRPublishing) and consolidated into the CDW domain with tag data from other SmartPlant
Enterprise authoring tools. Once the tags are published and consolidated, you can create a tag register for handover. For more details on authoring and published domains, see How to Configure SmartPlant Foundation Authoring.
Tag naming
To create tags, first you must choose the tag type for which you want to create tags. Tag types are object types, such as instrument, cable, and equipment that categorize the tags. Each tag type is preconfigured with an ENS (Engineering
Number System) definition that uses project-specific identification formats to automatically create names for the new tags.
The ENS definition is composed of various components, called ENS items, that can use constants and data extracted from other properties on the object or related objects. These components are configured in sequence with additional
delimiter characters to define the required format. By default, SmartPlant Master Tag Registry delivers one ENS definition for each tag type. The following ENS definitions are delivered with the software:
MTRCableAuthoring
MTRElectricalAuthoring
MTREquipmentAuthoring
MTRInstrumentAuthoring
MTRPipingSpecialtyAuthoring
MTRValveAuthoring
 The SmartPlant Foundation administrator can create and update the delivered ENS definitions, create new definitions, or terminate the relationship between the existing ENS definition and its associated class definition.
The delivered ENS definitions preconfigured for tag types use the following ENS item types:
Property - specifies the property definition.
ENS Function - automatically allocates a sequence number. Sequence defines the sequence of numbers to follow the ENS sequence start number.
For example, using the following settings, you could create the first cable tag with the name SC-1.
Property for the cable tag is SC
A ‘-‘(hyphen) set as a delimiter after the environment variables
Sequence number is set to start at 1
 Only a SmartPlant Foundation administrator can configure and update the ENS definitions for the tag types. For more information about configuring ENS, see Configure the Engineering Numbering System (ENS).
New <Tag Type Name> dialog box
Allows you to create one or more tags in the MTRAuthoring domain.
Description - Allows you to enter a description for the tag.
Unit - Allows you to select a unit with which you want to associate the new tag.
System - Allows you to select a system with which you want to associate the tag.
Tag Status - Allows you to specify the status of the authoring tag.
Preliminary - Initial status of a tag set by the EPC when pre-allocating tags for a vendor.
Active - Status of a tag set by a vendor in the Microsoft Excel datasheet to indicate to the EPC which pre-allocated tags were used by the vendor.
 Unused tags keep their Preliminary status and can be re-provisioned to other vendors
Void - Indicates that a previously used tag number is no longer required by the vendor.
Descoped - Indicates that the tag has been removed.
Supply Responsibility - Company that purchases an item for a project.
Vendor - Company that sells an item for a project.
Authoring Doc Proxies - Allows you to relate existing document proxies such as technical specification documents, drawings, commercial detail documents, warranty documents, and so on to a tag.
Number to create - Allows you to specify the number of tags to be created with the defined properties.
Start at number - Allows you to specify the starting sequence number for the tag or tags you are creating.
 Because tag names are generated automatically using ENS, you cannot provide a name for the new tag on this dialog box.
Class - Allows you to specify the class for an authoring tag.
Query For Authored Tags dialog box
Allows you to search for tags in the MTRAuthoring domain.
Number – Allows you to search for the tag by adding criteria for the tag number.
Description – Allows you to search for the tag by providing some criteria for the description of the tag.
Class – Allows you to search for a tag by the class definition of the tag.
Status – Allows you to query for tags by their status.
Supply Responsibility – Allows you to search for a tag by the supplier of the object with the tag number.
Vendor – Allows you to search for tags by the vendor for the object associated with the tag number.
Create an instrument loop
What is an instrument loop?
A group of one or more instruments or control functions arranged together to measure and control a process variable in a plant.
Why should I create an instrument loop?
To group different types of instruments together in a plant for controlling a process variable, you need to create an instrument loop and associate instruments to it.
1. Click File > New > MTR > Instrument Loop.
2. In the MTR Instrument Loop dialog box, type a name for the instrument loop.
3. Click Finish.
 You can click Find > MTR > Instrument Loops to search for the instrument loops created using this process.
Create a tag
1. Click File > New > MTR > Authored Tag, and select any of the tag types to open the corresponding dialog box.
By default, the tag name is displayed as "TBA: To be allocated using ENS".
For selected tag type, if the relationship between the Class definition and its associated ENS definition is terminated, the tag name is not displayed by default. You need to type the name for a tag manually.
2. In the PBS Details section, select a unit from the list to create a relationship between that unit and the new tag.
3. Select the system with which you want to associate the newly created tag.
4. Select the status of the tag.
5. In the Authoring Doc Proxies section, select the existing document proxies to relate to the tag.
6. In the Number to create box, specify the number of tags you want to create using the properties defined on this dialog box.
7. In the Start at number box, specify the sequence number to use for the first tag you will create.
8. Click Finish.
 When you create a tag for an instrument type, the Instrument Loop Details section is displayed in the corresponding dialog box for you to associate the instrument tag to the instrument loop.
Create a system
Why do I need to create a system?
To associate newly created tags with a system property in the MTRAuthoring domain, you must first create a system. Before you create a system, make sure that the plant has already been created. You can quickly search for required tags
using the system property and view the tag to system associations in the generated output register.
1. Click File > New > MTR > System.
2. In the New System dialog box, type a name for the system.
3. Click Finish.
 By default, the plant is displayed based on the scope set by you.
Create an authored document proxy and associate tags
What are authored document proxies?
Authored document proxies are document objects associated with a tag that can be technical specification documents, drawings, commercial detail documents, warranty documents, and so on. You can create authored document proxies
and associate them with a tag to organize and manage tag data in a plant.
When you create tags, you can create document proxies in Microsoft Excel. You can also create authored document proxies using the standard dialog box in SmartPlant Master Tag Registry and associate to a tag.
1. Click File > New > MTR > Authored Document Proxy.
2. In the New Authored Document Proxy dialog box, type a name and description for the new authored document proxy in the Name and Description boxes respectively.
3. Select the type of document that you want to associate to a tag.
4. Click Finish.
5. Click Find > MTR > Authored Tags to search for the tags that you want to associate with authored document proxies.
6. Drag one or more tags to the authored document proxy in the New Items window.
You can also search for document proxies and drag them onto a tag.
You can configure your own document number format for document proxies. Create an ENS definition for the authored document proxy and associate it with the MTRAuthDocProxy class definition. For more information on creating an
ENS definition and its relationship with class definition, see How to Configure Classification Trees and Classified Objects.
You can update an authored document proxy by finding the document proxies in the MTRAuthoring domain, right-click and click Update.
You can edit authored document proxies associated with tags using the Microsoft Excel data sheet as well as in the List Edit dialog box. You can change the existing authored document proxy for any tag. In case of a Microsoft Excel
data sheet, you must import the data sheet with changes into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry. The system creates a new authored document proxy and associates it to the tag. As a result, multiple authored document proxies of the
same type are associated to the tag. However, the generated data sheet does not support displaying multiple values for related objects. You must terminate the relationship between the tag and its authored document proxy that is no
longer required. To terminate relationship, see Terminate authored tag relationships.
In this example, you have a PID Drawing authored document proxy, and the value, PID1, is associated with a tag. You want to associate PID2 with that tag, then change the value to PID2 in the Microsoft Excel datasheet and import
the datasheet into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry. After importing, you must terminate the relationship between the tag and PID1.
Create tags with a data sheet
In addition to creating tags using the standard dialog box in SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, you can also create tags in a special Microsoft Excel spreadsheet and import them into the database and the MTRAuthoring domain. This
Microsoft Excel spreadsheet is called a data sheet and contains mapping information that is used to map specific cells in the spreadsheet with properties in the database. For more information, see Data Sheets.
 Before generating a datasheet with the tag data, make sure the required security settings for Microsoft Excel are configured on your client workstation. To configure security settings for Microsoft Excel, see Changing security
settings in Excel.
The following procedures explain how to create the data sheet, populate it with tag data, and import that data into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
Generate a data sheet template
1. Click Find > Datalist Definitions, and search for the MTR authored data list definitions.
2. Right-click a data list definition, and click Generate MTR Datasheet Template.
3. Select a location where you want to save the data sheet.
4. In the File name box, type a name for the data sheet, and then click Save.
By default, the data sheet name is saved as <Datalist definition name><(Template file name)> in .xlsm format. For example, the instrument type data sheet name is saved as MTRAuthoredInstrumentDLD
(SPMTRAuthoredInstrumentTemplate).
The data sheet can be accessed, reviewed, and edited by other users without using SmartPlant Foundation. The file can be moved as necessary.
5. Browse to the downloaded data sheet created, and open it using Microsoft Excel.
6. Enter information about the tag in the data sheet, as necessary. To populate the data sheet with tag information, see Create tags in a data sheet.
Import tags into the authoring domain
1. In SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, click File > Excel Import > Excel Import to import the modified data sheet with tags.
2. Browse to the Microsoft Excel file that contains the tag information you want to load, and click Open.
3. Click Browse 
 to open the Select Data list Template dialog box, where you can select the appropriate data list template for the specific type of tags you are importing. Click OK.
 By default, data list definitions are delivered with the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry for standard tag types. You can find the data list definitions on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more
information, see Find sample data on Smart Community. .
 To view all the data list definitions delivered with the software, click Find > Datalist Definitions.
4. Click Next to load the tags.
Click Finish.
 When you successfully import new tags from a datasheet, the Create Authoring Tags Log.txt log file is generated. This file lists tag numbers and sequence numbers (provided in the datasheet by the ENS). The log file is located at
[drive]:\Windows\temp.
If a tag's assigned sequence number in the datasheet is not available in the defined ENS, the system allocates the next available sequence number for that tag. The following log file illustrates this case.
Query for authored tags
Click Query > MTR > Authored Tags, and provide criteria as necessary to search for the tag or tags you want to find in the MTRAuthoring domain.
Edit tags in the authoring domain
 When you update properties or relationships that comprise the ENS for a tag, the name of the tag is updated automatically according to the defined ENS.
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Tags to search for the tag or tags in the MTRAuthoring domain that you want to modify.
 You can also click Query > MTR > Authored Tags and provide specific search criteria to find the tag or tags.
2. Right-click the tag or tags that you want to edit, and click List Edit <Tag Type Name> Properties.
 If you select multiple tags of different tag types to edit, right-click the tags and click List Edit Common Properties.
3. In the List Edit <Tag Type> dialog box, edit the properties that you want to update, and click OK.
You cannot edit asset related properties, or cable run related properties using the standard dialog box. This is because the List Edit does not support properties that exist across edge definitions, so they are read-only. However, you
can edit those properties in a Microsoft Excel spread sheet and import them into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
You can edit authored document proxies associated with tags using the Microsoft Excel data sheet as well as in the List Edit dialog box. You can change the existing authored document proxy for any tag. In case of a Microsoft Excel
data sheet, you must import the data sheet with changes into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry. The system creates a new authored document proxy and associates it to the tag. As a result, multiple authored document proxies of the
same type are associated to the tag. However, the generated data sheet does not support displaying multiple values for related objects. You must terminate the relationship between the tag and its authored document proxy that is no
longer required. To terminate relationship, see Terminate authored tag relationships.
In this example, you have a PID Drawing authored document proxy, and the value, PID1, is associated with a tag. You want to associate PID2 with that tag, then change the value to PID2 in the Microsoft Excel datasheet and import the
datasheet into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry. After importing, you must terminate the relationship between the tag and PID1.
Update authored tags
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Tags to search for the tag or tags in the MTRAuthoring domain that you want to modify.
 You can also click Query > MTR > Authored Tags and provide specific search criteria to find the tag or tags.
2. Right-click the tag that you want to update, and click Update.
3. In the Update <Tagname> dialog box, edit the properties that you want to update, and click Finish.
 When you update properties or relationships that comprise the ENS for a tag, the name of the tag is updated automatically according to the defined ENS.
Renumber authored tags
You can renumber tags in the MTRAuthoring domain in multiple ways. For example, you can renumber an existing tag by assigning a new number in the following ways:
Provide new tag numbers while updating the authored tags.
Provide new tag numbers when list editing the existing authored tags.
Provide new tag numbers in the Microsoft Excel data sheet and import them into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
 You can renumber the tags created with or without an ENS (Engineering Number System) definition. However, if you use ENS, make sure that the ENS definition contains the sequence number as an ENS function when you
renumber the tags.
Renumber tags using SmartPlant Master Tag Registry
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Tags to search for the tag or tags in the MTRAuthoring domain that you want to modify.
 You can also click Query > MTR > Authored Tags and provide specific search criteria to find the tag or tags.
2. Select the tag that you want to renumber, and right-click Update.
3. In the Update - <Tag name> dialog box, change the existing number to the new sequence number or tag number you want to assign for a tag in the New Sequence Number box.
4. Click Finish.
You cannot create multiple tags of same type with the same tag number. For example, PI-200 and PI-200.
You can create tags of different types with same sequence using the standard dialog box but not using the Microsoft Excel data sheet. For example, CV-200, PI-200, and PDSL-200 can be created using SmartPlant Master Tag
Registry standard dialog box.
Renumber tags using List Edit in SmartPlant Master Tag Registry
1. Click Query > MTR > Authored Tags and provide specific search criteria to find the tag or tags.
2. Select the tag or tags, right-click, and click List Edit <Tag Type Name> Properties.
3. In the List Edit <Tag Type> dialog box, type tag number in the New Sequence Number box, and click OK.
 If you want to select multiple tags of different tag types to renumber, right-click the tags and click List Edit Common Properties.
Renumber tags using Microsoft Excel
1. Generate a Microsoft Excel data sheet with the tags you want to renumber. For more information, see Generate and edit a data sheet offline.
2. In the Microsoft Excel data sheet, type the tag number you want to assign in the New Sequence Number column.
3. Save the data sheet as an .xlsm file and import it into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
Create different tags with the same sequence number using one of the following steps:
Disable ENS in SmartPlant Foundation and use the datasheet to create all new tag numbers as strings. For example, the following tags share the same sequence number and are part of a control flow loop (F-100): FT-100, FE-
100, and FI-100.
Create multiple datasheets for import using the same sequence number. (The datasheet does not support multiple tags with the same sequence number.) Import is successful if the same sequence number is used in all of the
datasheets.
When you successfully import new tags from a datasheet, the Create Authoring Tags Log.txt log file is generated. This file lists tag numbers and sequence numbers (provided in the datasheet by the ENS). The log file is located
at [drive]:\Windows\temp.
When you renumber tags, the existing sequence number of an authored tag is replaced with the new sequence number provided in the Microsoft Excel data sheet.
 After renumbering, you must create a snapshot of the authored register document or documents with which the renumbered tags are associated. Then publish the tags into the MTRPublishing domain to update the
information in CDW, so that registers can be recreated with updated data. To create a snapshot for authored register documents and publish, see Managing Authored Documents.
Delete authored tags
1. Click Query > MTR > Authored Tags, and search for the tags that were loaded in the authoring domain.
2. Select the tag or tags, right-click, and click Delete.
 Deleting tags from the authoring domain does not delete the corresponding tags automatically from the publish domain. You must delete them from the publish domain manually. For more information on deleting published
tags, see Delete published tags.
Terminate authored tag relationships
You can terminate the relationships, such as unit, or system associated with the authoring tag.
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Tags to search for the tag or tags in the MTRAuthoring domain that you want to modify.
 You can also click Query > MTR > Authored Tags and provide specific search criteria to find the tag or tags.
2. Right-click an authored tag, and click Terminate Relationships.
3. In the Terminate relationships to <Tagname> dialog box, select the tag related objects that you want to terminate.
 Terminating tag relationships in the authoring domain does not terminate the corresponding tag relationships automatically from the publish domain. You must terminate them from the publish domain manually. For more
information on removing published tags, see Terminate published tag relationships.
Terminate the relationship between a class definition and an ENS definition
 The SmartPlant Foundation Administrator role is required to terminate the relationship between the class definition and its associated ENS definition, as necessary.
1. Click Find > Schema > Class Definitions to search for the class definitions.
2. Right-click the class definition from the MTRAuthoring domain, and click Terminate Relationships.
3. In the Terminate relationships to <ClassDefinition> dialog box, select the ENS definition that you want to terminate.
Managing Authored Documents
Once the tags in the authoring domain are ready to be added into the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, you can group them together by associating them to an authored document to make it easier for you to manage the tags. The tags are
grouped together by their status, vendor, and supply responsibility property values. After tags are associated with an authored document, the document is ready to be published from the MTRAuthoring domain to the MTRPublishing
domain.
Tags are related to a specific document, then as the tag data evolves, you can create snapshots of the data as it exists at any point in time. These snapshots are managed as versions of the document. To publish tag data from the
authoring domain to the data warehouse, create a snapshot of the authored document. Each snapshot you create consists of an XML data file and contains the list of tags associated with the document as it exists when the snapshot is
created. When you publish a snapshot, the data in the XML file is loaded into the publish domain and consolidated in the CDW domain.
Once the tag information has been published, it can be included in a tag register. Over time, if the details about the tag change, you can create additional snapshots that can then be published to update the information in the CDW domain
so that registers can be recreated with updated data.
New Authored Register Document dialog box
Name - Allows you to provide a name for the authored register document you want to create.
Description - Allows you to provide a description for the authored register document.
Status - Displays the status of the document master of the register document you are creating. This option is read-only.
 When you create a document, the status of the master document is RESERVED. Once the document has been signed off, the status of the master is automatically updated to ISSUED.
Title - Allows you to provide a title for the authored register document.
Item owning group - Allows you to select the owning group for the authored register document.
Revision scheme - Select the revision status of the document.
Major revision - Set the major revision for the document. The software displays the first major revision number in the Major revision box.
Minor revision - Set the minor revision for the document, if necessary. The software displays the first minor revision number in the Minor revision box.
Status – Displays the status of the first revision of the new register document you are creating. This option is read-only.
 When you create a revision of a document, the status of that revision is Working. Once that revision has been signed off, the status is automatically updated to Current. When a newer revision is created, the status of previous
revisions is updated to Superseded.
Label - Allows you to provide text that will appear in the header or footer margins of the printed document.
Watermark - Allows you to provide text that will appear behind the main document content.
Register SmartPlant Master Tag Registry as an authoring tool
 Before you can create and publish a SmartPlant Master Tag Registry authored document, you must register SmartPlant Master Tag Registry tool for integration. This registration procedure must be performed only once for
each plant in which you want to publish documents.
1. Click SmartPlant > MTR > Register to open the SmartPlant Registration Wizard.
2. In the SmartPlant Foundation URL box of the Register dialog box, type the node name and virtual directory of the SmartPlant Foundation database to which you want to register your plant. Use the following format: http://[node
name]/[VirtualDirectory].
To register to a secure server, type https:// in your SmartPlant Foundation URL.
Replace [node name] with the name of your SmartPlant Foundation Application server.
Replace [Virtual Directory] with the name of the virtual directory for the SmartPlant Foundation site.
3. Enter your SmartPlant Foundation user name and password in the User name and Password boxes.
4. From the Plant name list, select the plant with which you want to register your authoring tool.
5. Click Finish.
Create an authored document and associate tags
 Before you create an authored document, you must register SmartPlant Master Tag Registry for integration. This registration procedure must be performed only once for each plant in which you want to publish documents.
For more information, see Register SmartPlant Master Tag Registry as an authoring tool.
1. Click File > New > MTR > Authored Register Document to open the New Authored Register Document dialog box.
2. Select the MTR Document type for which you want to create a new authored register document, and click Next.
3. Provide the required information to create an authored document. Asterisks (*) indicate required information.
4. Click Finish.
5. Click Find > MTR > Authored Tags, and search for the authored tags you want to associate with the new document.
 You can associate authored tags to an authored register document, but you cannot associate other related tag objects, such as instrument loops and cable runs.
6. Select the appropriate tags, and then drag them onto the new document, which is displayed in the New Items window.
 When you view an authored tag in the list view window, the document with which it is associated appears in the Authored Register Document column.
Find authored tags from the document
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Register Documents, and search for the document to which the authored tags are related.
2. Right-click an authored document, and click Show MTR Authored Tags.
Create a snapshot of an authored document
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Register Documents, and search for the authored document or documents for which you want to create a snapshot.
2. Right-click the authored document or documents, and click MTR > Create Snapshot.
 You can also click Create MTRSnapshot 
 on the toolbar to create snapshot of selected authored document or documents.
When you create the first snapshot of an authored register document, the software creates and attaches new electronic files that contain the data associated with the document. When you create additional snapshots, the software
creates a new version of the document and attaches updated copies of the electronic files with the current data from the database, typically .xml files with the data from the database.
The Create MTRSnapshot command is available only for an authored document or documents that are in the Working revision state.
If the tags attached to an authored register document are more than the MTRDataChunkSize set on the site Settings node, then multiple data files are generated and attached to that document. The default value for
MTRDataChunkSize is 1000. Each data file can store up to 1000 tags.
Publish tags
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Register Documents, and search for the authored document you want to publish.
2. Select the document or documents, right-click, and click MTR > Publish.
 When multiple documents are selected, only property values shared by all of the selected documents appear in the table. Changing a value in the table changes that value for all of the selected documents.
3. Type the SmartPlant Foundation user name and password if prompted.
4. Perform the following in the Publish dialog box:
On the Publish tab, select a Workflow for the document to be published.
 By default a workflow is populated if it is configured through SmartPlant Foundation Server Manager
Select the owning group from the list of owning groups available in SmartPlant Foundation for publishing.
By default, the owning group selected for the previous version, if any, is displayed.
Select one of the following publish methods from the Operation list.
Publish to publish the selected documents immediately when you click OK.
Background publish to publish as a separate process so that you can continue working in the application. When you use this feature, an e-mail message alerts you when the process is complete. To configure your email
for receiving email notifications, see Setting up e-mail for SmartPlant Foundation.
5. Click OK.
6. In the Batch Publish dialog box, perform the following:
Select Publish each document in separate process for publishing each selected document as a separate process.
If this option is selected, the bulk publish processes in the background for each document, then closes after each successful publish.
If this option is not selected, the bulk publish processes in the background for each document, but remains open to publish documents in sequence. The default is that this option is not checked.
Select Enable concurrent publish for publishing multiple documents simultaneously rather than separately. This option is only checked if Publish each document in a separate process is checked.
Type value in the Publish pool size box to specify the maximum number of documents can be published concurrently.
 Setting the pool size to 1 is similar to not checking Enable concurrent publish. This option is only available if Publish each document in a separate process is checked
7. Click OK.
8. In the Publish dialog box, click OK.
 You can also click MTR Publish 
 on the toolbar to publish the selected authored document.
The MTRPublish command is available only for an authored document for which a snapshot is already created.
When the authored document is published, SmartPlant Foundation creates a published document in the MTRPublishing domain with the same name as the authored document.
SmartPlant Foundation loads and consolidates the authored tags into a published (MTRPublishing) domain and the CDW domain, respectively. Click Find > Consolidated Data > All Consolidated Objects to check that the authored
tags were consolidated.
Loading Tags Work Process
SmartPlant Master Tag Registry enables collaboration with vendors to incorporate their tags into the tag registers generated for handover. To maintain consistent tag numbers of objects among various disciplines, an EPC can do the
following things:
Preallocate tag numbers in the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry for tags, generate a data sheet, and send it to the vendor for the tag technical details, or
Get the tag details from vendors in a Microsoft Excel file, import them into the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, and allocate tag numbers.
This section illustrates a standard work process where an EPC collects tag information from a vendor, includes the information in their authored tag data, and then publishes the authored tag data for inclusion in tag registers.
 Click a box in the diagram to go to the associated procedure.
Generate and edit a data sheet offline
If you have already created tags in your SmartPlant Master Tag Registry database, and would like to import additional information from the vendor, use the procedure below to export the tags into an editable data sheet.
1. Click Query > MTR > Authored Tags, and provide specific search criteria to find the tag or tags that you want to put in the data sheet for editing.
2. From the list of results, select the tag or tags for which you want to generate a data sheet.
3. Right-click the selected tags, and click Generate MTR Datasheet.
4. In the Generate MTR Datasheet dialog box, select the appropriate data list definition, and click OK.
5. Select a location where you want to save the data sheet so that you can open and edit it.
6. In the File name box, type a name for the data sheet, and then click Save.
By default, the data sheet name is saved as <Template file name> in .xlsm format.
The data sheet can be accessed, reviewed, and edited by other users without using SmartPlant Foundation. The file can be moved as necessary.
7. Browse to the new data sheet, and open it using Microsoft Excel.
8. Enter information about the tag in the data sheet, as necessary.
For properties that are defined by an enumerated list, the complete list of available values is included in the data sheet in a list box.
For properties that require a unit of measurement (UoM), select the UoM from the list first and then provide the applicable value.
Click Properties 
 to expand the task pane and display the properties for the active cell.
9. Click File > Save to save changes in the data sheet.
 The data sheet changes must be saved in the .xlsm version.
This Microsoft Excel file can be sent to the vendor, who can provide technical details for the tags.
When you modify or delete the value of Name property of an object that was related to the tag, the existing value for that property is displayed to you as a comment in the data sheet.
Generate a data sheet template
If the tags do not already exist in the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry database, you can use the data sheet to create the tags and provide the technical details.
1. Click Find > Datalist Definitions, and search for the MTR authored data list definitions.
2. Right-click a data list definition, and click Generate MTR Datasheet Template.
3. Select a location where you want to save the data sheet.
4. In the File name box, type a name for the data sheet, and then click Save.
By default, the data sheet name is saved as <Datalist definition name><(Template file name)> in .xlsm format. For example, the instrument type data sheet name is saved as MTRAuthoredInstrumentDLD
(SPMTRAuthoredInstrumentTemplate).
The data sheet can be accessed, reviewed, and edited by other users without using SmartPlant Foundation. The file can be moved as necessary.
5. Browse to the downloaded data sheet created, and open it using Microsoft Excel.
6. Send the generated Microsoft Excel file to the vendor for them to enter all their tag details.
Create tags in a data sheet
You can use a data sheet to create new tags and provide any technical details available for the tags. The data sheet can be accessed, reviewed, and edited by other users, including the vendor, without using SmartPlant Foundation. The
file can be moved as necessary, so that data can be provided from different sources at different locations.
By default, SmartPlant Master Tag Registry delivers sample datasheets that contain sample data of various tag types (such as Instrument, Equipment, and so on) on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For
more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community. .
How do sample datasheets help me in creating tag data?
Sample data sheets provide an easy starting point for creating your own data sheet for tag creation and management. You can review the delivered data sheets and compare the properties mapped in them to the properties that you want
to include on your data sheet. Then you can either modify the sample data sheets to meet your needs or use them as template for creating new datasheet templates.
EPCs can provide the sample datasheets (along with their actual datasheets) to vendors as a reference to enter data in the appropriate cells with correct format.
 In the sample datasheets, the Unit and System properties does not contain sample data by default because those values vary as per the implementation of SmartPlant foundation. If you want to import the sample datasheets into
SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, you can provide values for those two properties based on your SmartPlant Foundation target system
1. In the data sheet, select a cell in the Tag Number column, and click New 
 to create and enter technical details for new tag or tags.
2. In the Create tags dialog box, enter the number of tags you want to create, and click OK.
 By default, all the empty cells in the data sheet are in a locked state until you unlock each row as described in step1.
In the data sheet, the Tag Number column contains the value “TBA: To be allocated using ENS.” Do not modify the value in this column.
The top row of the spreadsheet contains predefined column headers. Do not modify this row.
3. Enter information about the tag in the data sheet, as necessary. To enter additional information about relationships the tags have with other objects in a data sheet, see Additional tag information to provide in a data sheet.
Click Properties 
 to expand the task pane and display the properties for the active cell.
For properties that are defined by an enumerated list, the complete list of available values is included in the data sheet in a list box. Click the list box, and then select the correct entry from the list.
For properties that require a unit of measurement (UoM), select the UoM from the list first and then provide the applicable value. If UoM value is not selected, you cannot import the data sheet into SmartPlant Master Tag
Registry.
For the Unit property, you must provide a value that currently exists in the list of units in the authoring domain of SmartPlant Foundation.
For the System property, use the name of one that currently exists in the list of systems created in the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, or leave the value empty.
For Manufacturer property, you must provide a value that currently exists in the Manufacturers Enum List. If the value is not available, the SmartPlant Foundation Administrator must add a new Enum list entry for Manufacturers.
For more information, refer to Publish tags.
For properties such as Warranty Expiration Date and Installation Date, the value must be in the YYYY/MM/DD format to import data into the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
For the New Sequence Number property, provide a number that you want to assign for the new tag. When you import the Microsoft Excel datasheet into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, a tag is created based on ENS
definition where the new tag number is considered as the sequence number. If no value is provided for the New Sequence Number property, the tag number is allocated automatically by the ENS when you import the Microsoft
Excel file with tag details into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
 You cannot assign duplicate tag numbers.
The following table illustrates the Microsoft Excel file with new tag numbers:
Tag number
Tag status
Instrument process function
Instrument type
New sequence number
TBA: To be allocated using
ENS
Active
Control Valve
CV
240
TBA: To be allocated using
ENS
Active
General Instrument
I/P
340
TBA: To be allocated using
ENS
Active
Flow Instrument
FI
TBA: To be allocated using
ENS
Active
Level Instrument
LSH
500
When you import the Microsoft Excel datasheet with the above data into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, the software creates new tags based on the ENS definition and New Sequence Number property. The new tags are CV-240,
I/P-340, FI-341, and LSH-500 respectively. Because there is no value provided for the instrument process function Flow Instrument, the system automatically generates a new tag number based on the preceding tag number value.
Therefore, the tag number allocated for the Flow Instrument is FI – 341.
4. Click File > Save to save changes in the data sheet.
 The data sheet changes must be saved as an .xlsm file.
When you save the data sheet in .xlsm format, an .xml file is generated in the same location where the .xlsm file was saved.
When sending the datasheet for offline editing, such as allowing a vendor, to add technical details for the tags, you must send both the .xlsm and .xml files of the datasheet.
To view tag numbers in a data sheet while you scroll to another area of the data sheet, you need to freeze the Tag Number column using the Freeze Panes command.
In the generated data sheet you cannot enter data in the Tag number column. The tag number is allocated by the ENS when you import the Microsoft Excel file with tag details into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
In the Microsoft Excel sheet, you can drag values from one cell to another only in rows that are already instantiated. The drag-and-drop feature is available only for cells within the same column.
Delete tags in a data sheet
1. Select the object or objects, and click Delete 
 on the task pane.
 The Delete command is available only if:
The selected cell represents the tag number on the data sheet.
The selected cell does not contain a defined name (such as HTABLE_MTRAUTHOREDEQUIPMENT.TAG_NUMBER) for the tag number. By default, the top row of the spreadsheet contains predefined column headers and their
corresponding defined names. But for the column header Tag Number, the defined name is the cell below it.
For multiple tags, select the range of continuous cells in the tag number column on the data sheet.
2. In the confirmation dialog box, click Yes to remove the tags from the data sheet.
 The tags are removed from the data sheet but are not deleted from the SmartPlant Foundation database.
Import tags into the authoring domain
The vendor can create tags for an object and provide its technical details in the Microsoft Excel data sheet. The data sheet with the tag details can then be loaded into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry using Excel Import functionality.
 Date in the Microsoft Excel data sheet must be in the YYYY/MM/DD format to import data into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
After receiving the updated Microsoft Excel file with the tag details from the vendor, import the tags into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry using the following steps.
 Make sure the Create Scope in the Set Active Scope dialog box is set to the appropriate plant to import tags into the MTRAuthoring domain. To set the scope to the required plant, see Set the active configuration by role.
1. In SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, click File > Excel Import > Excel Import to import the modified data sheet with tags.
2. Browse to the Microsoft Excel file that contains the tag information you want to load, and click Open.
3. Click Browse 
 to open the Select Data list Template dialog box, where you can select the appropriate data list template for the specific type of tags you are importing. Click OK.
 By default, data list definitions are delivered with the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry for standard tag types. You can find the data list definitions on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more
information, see Find sample data on Smart Community. .
 To view all the data list definitions delivered with the software, click Find > Datalist Definitions.
4. Click Next to load the tags.
5. Click Finish.
When you update properties or relationships that comprise the ENS for a tag, the name of the tag is updated automatically according to the defined ENS.
You generate a datasheet with tags from SmartPlant Master Tag Registry and modify existing tags in that Microsoft Excel datasheet. When you import the updated datasheet into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, the Import Results
Summary window shows that more updates were made rather than actual changes made in the datasheet. This is because the updates are triggered based on the container ID of the Excel file during import process.
Create an authored document and associate tags
 Before you create an authored document, you must register SmartPlant Master Tag Registry for integration. This registration procedure must be performed only once for each plant in which you want to publish documents.
For more information, see Register SmartPlant Master Tag Registry as an authoring tool.
1. Click File > New > MTR > Authored Register Document to open the New Authored Register Document dialog box.
2. Select the MTR Document type for which you want to create a new authored register document, and click Next.
3. Provide the required information to create an authored document.
4. Click Finish.
5. Click Find > MTR > Authored Tags, and search for the authored tags you want to associate with the new document.
6. Select the appropriate tags, and then drag them onto the new document, which is displayed in the New Items window.
 When you view an authored tag in the list view window, the document with which it is associated appears in the Authored Register Document column.
Create a snapshot of an authored document
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Register Documents, and search for the authored document or documents for which you want to create a snapshot.
2. Right-click the authored document or documents, and click MTR > Create Snapshot.
 You can also click Create MTRSnapshot 
 on the toolbar to create snapshot of selected authored document or documents.
When you create the first snapshot of an authored register document, the software creates and attaches new electronic files that contain the data associated with the document. When you create additional snapshots, the software
creates a new version of the document and attaches updated copies of the electronic files with the current data from the database, typically .xml files with the data from the database.
The Create MTRSnapshot command is available only for an authored document or documents that are in the Working revision state.
If the tags attached to an authored register document are more than the MTRDataChunkSize set on the site Settings node. Then, multiple data files are generated and attached to that document. The default value for
MTRDataChunkSize is 1000. Each data file can store up to 1000 tags.
Publish tags
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Register Documents, and search for the authored document you want to publish.
2. Select the document or documents, right-click, and click MTR > Publish.
 When multiple documents are selected, only property values shared by all of the selected documents appear in the table. Changing a value in the table changes that value for all of the selected documents.
3. Type the SmartPlant Foundation user name and password if prompted.
4. Perform the following in the Publish dialog box:
On the Publish tab, select a Workflow for the document to be published.
 By default a workflow is populated if it is configured through SmartPlant Foundation Server Manager
Select the owning group from the list of owning groups available in SmartPlant Foundation for publishing.
By default, the owning group selected for the previous version, if any, is displayed.
Select one of the following publish methods from the Operation list.
Publish to publish the selected documents immediately when you click OK.
Background publish to publish as a separate process so that you can continue working in the application. When you use this feature, an e-mail message alerts you when the process is complete. To configure your email
for receiving email notifications, see Setting up e-mail for SmartPlant Foundation.
5. Click OK.
6. In the Batch Publish dialog box, perform the following:
Select Publish each document in separate process for publishing each selected document as a separate process.
If this option is selected, the bulk publish processes in the background for each document, then closes after each successful publish.
If this option is not selected, the bulk publish processes in the background for each document, but remains open to publish documents in sequence. The default is that this option is not checked.
Select Enable concurrent publish for publishing multiple documents simultaneously rather than separately. This option is only checked if Publish each document in a separate process is checked.
Type value in the Publish pool size box to specify the maximum number of documents can be published concurrently.
 Setting the pool size to 1 is similar to not checking Enable concurrent publish. This option is only available if Publish each document in a separate process is checked
7. Click OK.
8. In the Publish dialog box, click OK.
 You can also click MTR Publish 
 on the toolbar to publish the selected authored document.
The MTRPublish command is available only for an authored document for which a snapshot is already created.
When the authored document is published, SmartPlant Foundation creates a published document in the MTRPublishing domain with the same name as the authored document.
SmartPlant Foundation loads and consolidates the authored tags into a published (MTRPublishing) domain and the CDW domain, respectively. Click Find > Consolidated Data > All Consolidated Objects to check that the authored
tags were consolidated.
 Before you publish tags, make sure that the value provided for the property Manufacturer Name in the Microsoft Excel data sheet is available in the ENUM List. If the value is not available in the ENUM list delivered with the
software, the SPF Administrator must create one. Otherwise, the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry does not allow you to publish tags into the MTR Publishing domain and displays the following error.
To add new entries in the Enum list, see Defining a List of Values (EnumEnum, EnumListType) section in A Gentle Introduction to the SmartPlant Schema <https://docs.hexagonppm.com/viewer/document/9A_G~AbMWyBOVt2aI81pYA> .
Loading Pipeline Tags Work Process
This section illustrates a standard work process where an EPC collects pipeline tag information from a vendor, includes the information in their authored tag data, and then publishes the pipeline tag data for inclusion in pipeline tag register.
 Click a box in the diagram to go to the associated procedure.
Generate and edit a pipeline datasheet
If you have already created pipeline tags in your SmartPlant Master Tag Registry database, and would like to import additional information from the vendor, use the procedure below to export the tags into an editable data sheet.
1. Click Find > Line List > Line List Records, and provide pipeline in the search criteria to find the pipe line tags that you want to put in the data sheet for editing.
2. From the list of results, select the line list records for which you want to generate a data sheet.
3. Right-click the selected line list records, and click Generate MTR Datasheet.
4. In the Generate MTR Datasheet dialog box, select the MTRAuthPipelineDLD data list definition, and click OK.
5. Select a location where you want to save the data sheet so that you can open and edit it.
6. In the File name box, type a name for the data sheet, and then click Save.
By default, the data sheet name is saved as <Template file name> in .xlsm format.
The data sheet can be accessed, reviewed, and edited by other users without using SmartPlant Foundation. The file can be moved as necessary.
7. Browse to the new data sheet, and open it using Microsoft Excel.
8. Enter information about the tag in the data sheet, as necessary.
For properties that are defined by an enumerated list, the complete list of available values is included in the data sheet in a list box.
The line list record name and pipeline are created based on the predefined ENS definition. So, in the data sheet, the Line List Record Name and Line Number columns contains the value “TBA: To be allocated using ENS.”
Do not modify the value in this column.
You must provide values for the properties in a data sheet such as, Fluid System, Fluid Code, Line Sequence Number, Nominal Diameter, Piping Material Class, and Insulation Purpose.
Click Properties 
 to expand the task pane and display the properties for the active cell.
9. Click File > Save to save changes in the data sheet.
 The data sheet changes must be saved in the .xlsm version.
This Microsoft Excel file can be sent to the vendor, who can provide technical details for the tags.
When you modify or delete the value of name property of an object that was related to the tag, the existing value for that property is displayed to you as a comment in the data sheet.
Generate a pipeline datasheet template
If the tags do not already exist in the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry database, you can use the data sheet to create the tags and provide the technical details.
1. Click Find > Datalist Definitions, and search for the MTR authored data list definitions.
2. Right-click a data list definition, and click Generate MTR Datasheet Template.
3. Select a location where you want to save the data sheet.
4. In the File name box, type a name for the data sheet, and then click Save.
By default, the data sheet name is saved as <Datalist definition name><(Template file name)> in .xlsm format. For example, the instrument type data sheet name is saved as MTRAuthoredPipelineDLD
(SPMTRAuthoredPipelineTemplate).
The data sheet can be accessed, reviewed, and edited by other users without using SmartPlant Foundation. The file can be moved as necessary.
5. Browse to the downloaded data sheet created, and open it using Microsoft Excel.
6. Send the generated Microsoft Excel file to the vendor for them to enter all their tag details.
Create pipeline tags in a data sheet
You can use a data sheet to create new tags and provide any technical details available for the tags. The data sheet can be accessed, reviewed, and edited by other users, including the vendor, without using SmartPlant Foundation. The
file can be moved as necessary, so that data can be provided from different sources at different locations.
By default, SmartPlant Master Tag Registry delivers sample pipeline datasheet SPMTRAuthoredPipeline Datasheets that contain sample data of pipeline tag type at Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For
more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
1. In the data sheet, select a cell in the Line List Record Name column, and click New 
 to create and enter technical details for new pipeline tags.
2. In the Create tags dialog box, enter the number of tags you want to create, and click OK.
 By default, all the empty cells in the data sheet are in a locked state until you unlock each row as described in step1.
The line list record name and pipeline are created based on the pre-defined ENS definition. So, in the data sheet, the Line List Record Name and Line Number columns contains the value “TBA: To be allocated using ENS.”
Do not modify the value in this column.
The top row of the spreadsheet contains predefined column headers. Do not modify this row.
3. Enter information about the tag in the data sheet, as necessary. To enter additional information about relationships the tags have with other objects in a data sheet, see Additional tag information to provide in a data sheet.
4. You must provide values for the properties in a datasheet such as, Fluid System, Fluid Code, Line Sequence Number, Nominal Diameter, Piping Material Class, and Insulation Purpose.
Click Properties 
 to expand the task pane and display the properties for the active cell.
For properties that are defined by an enumerated list, the complete list of available values is included in the data sheet in a list box. Click the list box, and then select the correct entry from the list.
For properties that require a unit of measurement (UoM), select the UoM from the list first and then provide the applicable value. If UoM value is not selected, you cannot import the data sheet into SmartPlant Master Tag
Registry.
For Installation Date, the value must be in the YYYY/MM/DD format to import data into the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
5. Click File > Save to save changes in the data sheet.
 The data sheet changes must be saved as an .xlsm file.
When you save the datasheet in .xlsm format, an .xml file is generated in the same location where the .xlsm file was saved.
When sending the datasheet for offline editing, such as allowing a vendor, to add technical details for the tags, you must send both the .xlsm and .xml files of the datasheet.
To view tags in a data sheet while you scroll to another area of the data sheet, you need to freeze the Line List Record Name column using the Freeze Panes command.
In the generated data sheet you cannot enter data in the Line List Record Name and Line Number columns. The tag number is allocated by the ENS when you import the Microsoft Excel file with tag details into SmartPlant Master
Tag Registry.
In the Microsoft Excel sheet, you can drag values from one cell to another only in rows that are already instantiated. The drag-and-drop feature is available only for cells within the same column.
Delete tags in a data sheet
1. Select the object or objects, and click Delete 
 on the task pane.
 The Delete command is available only if:
The selected cell represents the tag number on the data sheet.
The selected cell does not contain a defined name (such as HTABLE_MTRAUTHPIPELINE.LINE_LIST_RECORD_NAME) for the list record name. By default, the top row of the spreadsheet contains predefined column headers
and their corresponding defined names. But for the column header Line List Record Name, the defined name is the cell below it.
For multiple tags, select the range of continuous cells in the Line List Record Name column on the data sheet.
2. In the confirmation dialog box, click Yes to remove the tags from the data sheet.
 The tags are removed from the data sheet but are not deleted from the SmartPlant Foundation database.
Import pipeline tags into the authoring domain
The vendor can create tags for an object and provide its technical details in the Microsoft Excel data sheet. The data sheet with the tag details can then be loaded into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry using Excel Import functionality.
 Date in the Microsoft Excel data sheet must be in the YYYY/MM/DD format to import data into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry.
After receiving the updated Microsoft Excel file with the tag details from the vendor, import the tags into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry using the following steps.
 Make sure the Create Scope in the Set Active Scope dialog box is set to the appropriate plant to import tags into the MTRAuthoring domain. To set the scope to the required plant, see Set the active configuration by role.
1. In SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, click File > Excel Import > Excel Import to import the modified data sheet with tags.
2. Browse to the Microsoft Excel file that contains the tag information you want to load, and click Open.
3. Click Browse 
 to open the Select Data list Template dialog box, where you can select the appropriate data list template for the specific type of tags you are importing. Click OK.
 By default, data list definitions are delivered with the SmartPlant Master Tag Registry for standard tag types. You can find the data list definitions on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more
information, see Find sample data on Smart Community. .
 To view all the data list definitions delivered with the software, click Find > Datalist Definitions.
4. Click Next to load the tags.
5. Click Finish.
When you update properties or relationships that comprise the ENS for a tag, the name of the tag is updated automatically according to the defined ENS.
You generate a datasheet with tags from SmartPlant Master Tag Registry and modify existing tags in that Microsoft Excel datasheet. When you import the updated datasheet into SmartPlant Master Tag Registry, the Import Results
Summary window shows that more updates were made rather than actual changes made in the datasheet. This is because the updates are triggered based on the container ID of the Excel file during import process.
Create a pipeline
1. Click Find > Line List > Line List Records, and search for the line list records with which you want to create a pipeline.
2. Select the line list record, right-click, and click Create Pipeline.
 You can associate multiple line list records to a pipeline.
3. In the Create Pipeline dialog box, provide values for Vendor and Originator.
4. Click Finish. A new pipeline is created and associated to a line list record.
Create a line list and associate line list records
1. Click File > New > Line List > Line List.
2. In the New line list dialog box, provide the name and description for the new line list.
3. Click Finish.
4. Click Find > Line List > Line List Records, and search for the line list records you want to associate with the new line list.
5. Select the line list records, and drag them onto the new line list object displayed in the New Items window.
6. Create a line list record document and associate line lists. To create, select the line list, right-click and click Line List > Create Line List Record Document.
7. In the New Create Line List Record Document dialog box, provide required information and click Finish. The line lists will be associated with the line list record document.
 A single line list record cannot be associated with multiple line lists, but multiple line list records can be associated with one line list.
Create a snapshot of a line list record document
1. Click Find > Line List > Line List Record Documents, and search for the line list record documents with which you want to create a snapshot.
2. Select the line list record document, right-click, and click Create Snapshot.
Publish pipeline tags
1. Click Find > Line List > Line List Record Document, and search for the line list record document you want to publish.
2. Select the line list record document, right-click, and click Publish.
When the line list record document is published, SmartPlant Foundation creates a published document in the LLA (Line List Authoring) domain with the same name as the line list record document.
SmartPlant Foundation loads and consolidates the pipeline tags into a LLA (Line List Authoring) domain and the CDW domain, respectively. Click Find > Consolidated Data > Pipeline to check that the pipeline tags were
consolidated.
Managing Published Tags
You cannot include tags created in an authoring domain in tag registers until they are published to the MTRPublishing domain and consolidated into the CDW domain with tag data from other SmartPlant Enterprise authoring tools. After the
tags are published and consolidated, the consolidated tags are automatically associated with the respective authoring unit. Using the consolidated tags, you can create your tag register for handover. When you generate tag registers with
published tags, rather than authored tags, the generated register contains the engineering data about the tags that already exist in the CDW domain.
Query For Registry Tags dialog box
Allows you to search for the tags in the CDW domain.
Number – Allows you to search for the tag by adding criteria for the tag number.
Description – Allows you to search for the tag by providing some criteria for the description of the tag.
Status – Allows you to query for tags by their status.
Class – Allows you to search for tags by their class definition.
Based on register document - Allows you to search for tags associated with a specific register document. You can select the register document from the Register Document list.
 The properties defined in the view definition associated with the selected register document are displayed after you select the document. You can edit the value of the criteria of a property.
Set tag status for tags in the CDW domain
The CDW domain’s tag status property allows tag level control, which includes additional property settings in tag registers for handover. For example, obsolete or invalid CDW domain tags can be excluded from tag registers by setting the
tag status property to void. Tag status management is manually performed through List Editing.
What are the possible tag statuses that I can set in the CDW domain?
You can set the following statuses for tags in the CDW domain.
Active - Default tag status. Indicates tags active in the current design.
Preliminary - Used to identify tags that are valid but not yet ready for inclusion in a tag register.
Void - Indicates that a tag number is no longer available for use in the project.
Descoped - Indicates that a tag has been removed.
Why do I need to set the tag status in the CDW domain?
Except for the tag status property, all of the tag properties of an object are published from the MTRAuthoring domain to the MTRPublishing domain. So, you must set the tag status property manually in the CDW domain. This helps you
query for tags, check tag readiness, and generate a tag register for handover.
1. To query for the tags in the CDW domain, see Query for published tags.
2. Right-click the tags of different tag types for which you want to change the status and click Set Tag Status.
3. In the Set Tag Status dialog box, select the status of the tag and click OK.
Query for published tags
Allows you to view the tags present in the CDW domain. You can query either on the tag details or by the register document to which the tags are related.
Query for tags by tag details
1. Click Query > MTR > Registry Tags.
2. Select the Based on tag details option in the Query For Registry Tags dialog box.
 By default, the Number option includes the asterisk (*), which will return all the tags in the CDW domain.
3. Provide the required information to search for the registry tags.
Query for tags by register document
1. Click Query > MTR > Registry Tags.
2. Select the Based on register document option.
3. Select the register document on which you want to query. The property details that are set on the view definition that is associated with the selected register document are displayed to set criteria.
4. From the Criteria (editable) column, click the property for which you want to set the criteria.
5. In the Criteria Definition dialog box, use the Operator and Value boxes to define the criteria that must be met by an object before it can be returned by the report.
6. Click Add Criterion to list the criterion in the Currently defined criteria box.
7. If necessary, click either And or Or, and define additional criteria that must be met.
 You can remove or modify added criteria by selecting them in the Currently defined criteria box and clicking Update Criterion, Delete All Criterion, or Delete Selected Criterion.
8. Click OK to return to the Query For Registry Tags dialog box.
9. Click OK.
 You can also click Find > Consolidated Data > <TagType> to search for tags in the CDW domain based on the tag type.
Find published tags from the document
When you select a published document in the Desktop Client and display information about it using the Expand Drawing Items command, a list view appears and shows all drawing items related to a published document
1. Click Find > Published Data > Documents, and search for the document on which the tags were published.
2. Right-click the published document, and click Expand Drawing Items.
Terminate published tag relationships
To terminate published tag relationships from the system, you must first terminate the relationships in the authoring domain and republish a new snapshot of the associated document where the tag relationships have been removed.
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Register Documents, and search for the document associated with the authored tags with terminated relationships.
2. Right-click the authored document, and click MTR > Create Snapshot to create a new snapshot of the document. For more information, see Create a snapshot of an authored document.
3. Right-click the authored document, and click MTR > Publish. For more information on publishing the document, see Publish tags.
Delete published tags
To delete published tags from the system, you must delete the tags in the authoring domain and then republish a new snapshot of the associated document from which the tags have been deleted.
1. Click Find > MTR > Authored Register Documents, and search for the document associated with the authored tags that you deleted.
2. Right-click the authored document, and click MTR > Create Snapshot to create a new snapshot of the document. For more information, see Create a snapshot of an authored document.
3. Right-click the authored document, and click MTR > Publish. The publish process generates delete instructions for deleted tags, and deletes the tags permanently from the publish and CDW domains. For more information on
publishing the document, see Publish tags.
4. To delete tags with the tag status assigned from the MTRPublishing domain, click Query > MTR > Registry tags.
5. Select the Based on tag details option in the Query For Registry Tags dialog box.
 By default, the Number option includes the asterisk (*), which will return all the tags in the CDW domain.
6. Select the status and provide the required information to search for the registry tags.
7. Select the registry tag or tags, right-click, and click Delete.
Using Register Documents
A register document, which is created using register definition, is used to generate tag registers. It helps manage various versions of tag registers and allows you to filter the content in tag registers using prompt properties set on the report
definition. When you generate a tag register, a report is created in the selected output format, Microsoft Excel or ASCII, and is then attached to the new version of the originating register document.
New Register Document dialog box
Allows you to create a new register document.
Name - Allows you to provide a name for the register document you want to create.
Description - Allows you to provide a description for the register document.
Status - Displays the status of the document master of the register document you are creating. This option is read-only.
 When you create a document, the status of the master document is RESERVED. Once the document has been signed off, the status of the master is automatically updated to ISSUED.
Title - Allows you to provide a title for the register document.
Item owning group - Allows you to select the owning group for the register document.
Status – Displays the status of the first revision of the new register document you are creating. This option is read-only.
 When you create a revision of a document, the status of that revision is Working. Once that revision has been signed off, the status is automatically updated to Current. When a newer revision is created, the status of previous
revisions is updated to Superseded.
Label - Allows you to provide text that will appear in the header or footer margins of the printed document.
Watermark - Allows you to provide text that will appear behind the main document content when the document is printed.
Register definition - Allows you to select the register definition that you want to associate to the register document.
Prompt properties details - Displays the list of prompt properties set on the report definition associated with the selected register definition.
Set criteria - Allows you to provide the values for the prompt properties, as defined in the associated report definition.
Create a register document
 The New Register Document dialog box allows you to create a new register document that consists of a document master, the first revision, and a version of the document.
1. Click File > New > MTR > Register Document to open the New Register Document dialog box.
2. Provide the required information for the new register document. Asterisks (*) indicate required information.
 When you select a register definition, the prompt properties that have been set on the related report definition appear in the Prompt Properties Details section.
3. To set the criteria for prompt properties, click Set Criteria, which opens the Prompt for Values dialog box.
4. To set single criteria for a property, define the values in the Operator and Value boxes in the Prompt for Values dialog box.
5. To set multiple criteria for a property, click the browse button beside the Value box.
6. In the Criteria Definition dialog box, use the Operator and Value boxes to define the criteria that must be met by an object before it can be returned by the report.
7. Click Add Criterion to list the criterion in the Currently defined criteria box.
8. If necessary, click either And or Or, and define additional criteria that must be met.
 You can remove or modify added criteria by selecting them in the Currently defined criteria window and clicking Update Criterion, Delete All Criterion, or Delete Selected Criterion.
9. Click OK.
10. In the Prompt for Values dialog box, click OK to return to the New Register Document dialog box.
11. Click Apply or Finish.
Update a register document
After associating a register definition with a register document, you can change the prompt properties set on the report definition associated with the selected register definition. Then, you must update the register document associated with
that updated report definition and register definition.
To modify the properties set on a register document, right-click it, and click Update.
 You can also select the register document you want to update, and click Update 
 on the tool bar.
You can update the register definition associated with a register document only until a tag register is generated.
You can modify the prompt properties set on a report definition associated with the selected register definition even after generating a register. When you modify the prompt properties, you must also set the criteria for those prompt
properties while updating the corresponding register document.
Copy a register document
The Copy command copies all the document properties and then creates a new document.
1. Click Find > MTR > Register Documents.
2. In the search results, right-click the register document that you want to copy and click Copy.
 You can also copy a register document by clicking Copy 
 on the toolbar.
3. Provide the new document with a unique name, and update the register document details, as necessary. Asterisks (*) indicate required information.
4. Click Finish.
Query for register documents
1. Click Query > MTR > Register Documents to open Query for Register Documents dialog box.
2. Type or select your search criteria.
 When you perform a search using the * wildcard, the objects returned are limited to only those where there is some value for that property. Objects with no value for that property will not be returned. To include objects with no
value for a specific property, leave the box for that property blank in the query form. For example, if you search for an object using Name = * and Description = *, your search results are reduced in number because the criteria
specifies all objects with a name value AND all objects with a description value.
3. Click Apply or Finish.
Using Tag Registers
Using tag registers, you can compile information originating from various disciplines and data sources, at various points during a project. A tag register is a Microsoft Excel file or ASCII file that contains information about tag numbers and
tag details from CDW domain, linking together data published by all the authoring tools. The properties of the tag selected on the report definition are included in the register, based on settings defined in the register definition.
 If any changes are made to the tag data in the authoring domain, you must create a new snapshot of the document and publish it before you can generate a tag register with updated data.
Tag registers are generated using register documents. When you generate a tag register on a register document, a new version of the document is created, and the tag register is attached to that new version.
You can continuously monitor data required for handover by scheduling the regeneration of tag registers. Using scheduled tasks, you can have the software automatically generate tag registers at a later time, on a recurring schedule, as
necessary. You can then review these registers and use color coding to easily find the added, deleted, or modified tags.
Generate Register dialog box
Allows you to generate tag registers using the register documents.
Register document name - Displays the name of the register document for which you will generate the tag register.
Run now - Provides the option to generate the tag register immediately.
Schedule - Provides the option to schedule the generation of tag registers at a later point of time. It allows you to specify the time and date when you want the task to start. The tasks can be scheduled to be run once or repeated on a
defined schedule.
One time - This option allows you to run the task one time at a date and time that you specify.
Daily - This option allows you to regenerate the tag register every day until the end date that you choose.
Weekly - This option allows you to regenerate the tag register each week on the day or days that you specify.
Recur every - Allows you to select the interval of recurrence for the tag register generation.
Set Expiration date-time - Allows you to specify the time and date when you want the scheduled task to stop running.
Select comparison version - Allows you to select a previous version of the tag register with which to compare the new version you are creating.
 This option is available only if the Show change status column option is selected for the register definition you are using. For more information, see Create a register definition.
Generate a tag register
